Прочтите данный обзор перед тем, как вы решите купить эту игру.  В последнем обновлении разработчики изменили сложность босса - Uut The Screamer. В виду чего, если вы будете пользоваться "прохождением на Ютубе" - вы столкнётесь с тем, что на видео многие моменты проходятся проще. Что конкретно было изменено: Ловушки больше нельзя обойти рывком. Лазерные лучи теперь отбрасывают вас на половину экрана назад. Боссы очень сильно повысили здоровье и собственный урон. Некоторые артефакты, не имеющие подписи "Не действует на элитных монстров и боссов" не действуют на этого босса. В частности чумное облако. В комнате бесконечно спавнятся волны врагов. Вы больше не можете делать рывок и кувырок постоянно, теперь между ними есть пауза и анимация стала "медленнее".  Отдельно отмечу, что в игре ужасная отзывчивость персонажа. Не смотря на то, что её много кто сравнивает с Hollow Knight - тут персонаж не может во время атаки сделать рывок назад, войти из рывка в кувырок или прыгнуть сразу по завершению атаки - на всё нужны микропаузы, которые могут убить вас во время прохождения игры.   К сожалению, за красивой визуальной составляющей игры скрывается крайне странное решение разработчиков без каких-либо причин усложнить игру для новых игроков.

This game is billed as a metroidvania, but it would be more accurate to call it a Hollow Knight clone (thats a good thing).

缺點很明顯，很多人提過，諸如怪莫名的肉，要打超多刀，手把移動超級飄移，那第一個boss議會來說，故意把平台設計的超級小，外加極度漂移的移動(過度靈敏)，還要故意把基礎能力拆解到打通boss身上，比如攀附牆壁  雖然可以背板研究boss，但效率堪稱低下，用這種惡意的設計，教玩家玩遊戲，用這種方式，掩蓋內容的貧乏，就算打折也絕不推薦這遊戲  如果不是超過退款時間，我還懶得寫評價，這是一個就算看別人的遊玩影片，也很難察覺他的操作問題的一個遊戲  實在猶豫看不出來有什麼問題，建議多看評論，或者有疑慮就直接不買該遊戲，免得氣到傷身

To me the highlights of this game are the exploration and the atmosphere / sounddesign and music. It controls very nicely and performs well on my worklaptop. The combat also felt nice and I didn't think the bosses were too easy - some were lacking in attack variety for the amount of health that they had, so the fight felt a bit repetitive. I didn't understand / read every lore bit, but felt immersed in its world either way. All in all a fantastic game!
构式，一坨 攻速快，但打谁都要a好几刀；脚滑得要死，再加上部分窄小平台，跑图十分费时；MD，进出房间会刷新小怪，但我tm就1次回血，而且存档点之间还隔得老远；传送点也不知道怎么搞，硬是浪费一堆时间、反反复复跑你那b图；下批因为攻速快、下降速度快而十分别扭；主线不明，瞎跑；屎中屎遭遇战，左右夹击、高伤害、基本没回血机会（有也只有一次） ex死我了，很不推荐

The most underrated Metroidvania I've ever played, and one of the most underrated games in generally. 10/10 art, exploration, music (some absolute BANGER boss themes) combat and story/theme. Has some of the most unique traversal mechanics I've seen, you have the standards like [spoiler]double jump and a dash[/spoiler] but the other options are truly unique. I never got bored while exploring, which happens with some other Metroidvanias for me.  Absolutely on the same level as some the greats like Hollow Knight and SOTN. A must play in my opinion.

Metroidvania glané dans un bundle. Il n'avait pas que de bonnes critiques. C'est vrai que ce jeu démarre trop dur, en mettant en avant des difficultés et des murs à franchir peu subtils, malvenus et ne représentant pas la vraie qualité du jeu. Il me rappelle beaucoup Blue Fire en ce sens. En effet, c'est un jeu qui se raffine avec le temps. Passé cette première partie, c'est un metroidvania très bien conçu, qui se permet même d'avoir des pouvoirs qui "overlap" et qui permettent d'atteindre une zone avec un pouvoir ou un autre: cette liberté est toujours très sympathique et bienvenue. Aussi, j'ai eu envie de pousser jusqu'au 100% True ending ce qui est un bon signe. On a pas envie de lâcher.  Donc un début compliqué mais un investissement récompensé. Pas un top du genre mais fait passer un très bon moment.  12H - True ending - 100% MAP

I really like this game a lot more than I thought I would when I started. The beginning was a bit slow so I thought it would be an okay game. Once you get more movement options and attacks it gets a lot better.   Combat was fine at first but needed something. A few hours in and it got that something and I started to really like it.   I like that you can place a marker on the map to come back later when you have the right upgrade. Many Metroidvania games could really use that option. Some do but a lot I’ve played don’t.  The down pogo like Hollow Knight feels great and can really cheese some bosses. Overall it’s not a Hollow Knight or Silksong but a level or two below that. I’ll definitely get the sequel if one gets made.

Just finished it and wow! Not only a great metroidvania, but a great gaym overall. Its not perfect, but it really is worth your time. The atmopshere and music is brilliant, the combat is tight and feels precise and the build variety impactful. Honestly, if someone wants to get into these types of gayms, I would recommend Hollow Knight first, then Voidwrought second (over silksong or anything else). On the negative side - some of the writing and quests are a bit vague and overall I am not entirely sure wtf just happened. Also, the camera feels weird at the start and took me about an hour to get use to.  But, overall, a great gaym. Totally worth. A must play I would rate for any 2D soulslike metroidvania types.

Le he dado bastantes oportunidades y nunca he podido jugar mas de 1 h seguida porque me frustra. No tengo demasiado problema con los juegos difíciles, si con los aburridos e injustos. Para empezar no se quien ha colocado el sistema de teletransporte y habitaciones de guardado, pero, al igual que todo el mapa, parece 100% aleatorio y, sin embargo, siempre está lejos. A veces si que hay alguno cerca de un boss, a veces tienes que darte un largo paseo. ¿Largo? Si esquivas a los enemigos no tanto, y te lo recomiendo, les haces el mismo daño que si les soplaras incluso a los más básicos.  Además el juego te invita a abusar de los pogos, atacar hacia abajo rebotando, muchísimo, ya que pocos enemigos atacan hacia arriba. Así que ahí estas, saltando encima de un zombie random que muere tras un buen número de ostias. Peor bueno, ha pasado un rato y estas ante el boss. Oh vaya, te ha dado, no pasa nada, me curaré. Curando... curando... curando... Me veo una peli pirata mientras tanto... curando... curando. Ale, curado. 3 puntitos de vida, tampoco nos flipemos. Y agradece que tienes 1 cura.  Luego está la forma de "esquivar". Quiero entender que más hacia adelante tendrás algo que te haga más ágil, pero tras un puñado de horas tienes una especie de reptación cuyo único uso es colarte en agujeros como la bola de Samus, y un dash equivalente a arrastrar el personaje rápido con el ratón en el cual eres totalmente vulnerable. Eso unido a un combate aburrido, enemigos que son esponjas de vida y el largo paseo, hace que el combate se atragante bastante.  Pero bueno, seguro que tiene una historia medio buena, ¿no?. Nope, aquí no es. ¿La exploración? ¿Aquello que mueve todo buen metroidvania? Como he dicho el mapa parece hecho por un programa y no te invita en absoluto a explorar. Además, vas pasando de una zona a otra por donde te van dejando, pero vas por ahí, porque si. Asú que guardas en un sitio y vas tirando y tirando, entrando por puertas y puertas y dos o tres zonas diferentes, si no te han matado, quizás encuentres un boss, u otra zona de guardado o cosas así. Por el camino parece que te has dejado muchas posibilidades, pero en realidad no, todo esta bloqueado por evidentes poderes que ya conseguirás. Lo he dejado en una criatura que tenía un gancho en lo alto, probablemente para engancharme con una de estas habilidades. Monstruo que está tras una aburrida persecución con muerte instantánea y punto de guardado lejos, claro está. El tema es que yo no tengo gancho. Si, a fuerza bruta y pogo, al final caería, pero en mi cabeza se ha venido el pensamiento de que ojalá poder controlar el mando con una mano para hacerle pogo y rascarme la barriga con la otra. Así de interesante son las batallas.  Aquel que se meta en mi perfil verá que es raro que yo ponga reseñas negativas. Si no me gusta o no es de mi rollo, a los 20 min lo dejo y ya. Pero es que este creo que tiene potencial por algún sitio, quise pensar que solo el inicio es torpe, pero no.

Не знаю, зачем я даже решил это попробовать, когда есть HK и силксионг.  - Если в кратце, я хз для кого эта игра. Управление в ней ужасное. Разница между полным прыжком и минимальным - да почти не чувствуется. Через врагов можно перескакивать первым апргейдом, но он ужасно нажимается, то ли у него есть время когда его нельзя использовать после атаки, так он ещё и не гарантирует что ты уклонился... Мобы - вот самые бесячие... Прибавьте к этому зачем-то непонятно растяннутое хп, когда одного моба ты просто ковыряешь чтобы убить ,постоянно от него отталкиваясь. Когда появляются воздушные начинается раздражение. Непонятный левел-дизайн, где ты вот получил улучшение, но они настолько странные, что разраб специально будто не хотел давать что-то интересное. Первый босс - первый раз когда я подумал игру дропнуть, но в итоге ловил себя на мысли, ну не может же быть тааак плохо... Да Босса я прошёл, но ненадолго меня хватило. Ибо далее просто странные непонятные комнаты со спамом мобов. Вроде и пытались сделать как у других - а получилось - никак.  Игру я крайне не советую. Даже по скидке. Шанс игре вы дать можете, если вам ну прям совсем делать нечего. И то я бы подумал.

- От постоянно мельтешащей туда-сюда камеры при быстрой смене направления тошнит. Будто пытались соединить камеру из Полого рыцаря и Кастельвании, но вышло ужаснтно. - Атака персонажа слишком короткая. Из-за этого и прыгать на врагах с помощью атаки неприятно, потому что хрен по нему попадешь из-за размера атаки хитбокса и маленькой задержки перед ударом.   - Это еще не говоря про то, что персонаж при удачном отскоке быстро взлетает и так же быстро падает. Если в игре есть апгрейд для увеличения радиуса удара, то до него просто не хватит терпения дойти. - Враги респаунятся при выходе из комнаты. Полагаю это было сделано чтобы было легче фармить валюту, потому что иной логичной причины для этого я не вижу. Но враги, постоянно возвращающиеся и мешающие пройти - бесят. - Управление громоздкое и неудобное. Враги громоздкие и топорные. Боссы громоздкие и кривые. Вся игра ощущается громоздкой, кривой и полусырой. В это просто неприятно играть. Откуда столько восторженных обзоров?

This game started really slow and boring for me. It is a bit unfortunate that it starts being good a few hours in. Only after getting a few Simulacrums it began to grow on me. I liked the progression system, the idea of expanding your "base" was fun and rewarded exploration, and I love exploration in metroidvanias.   Backtracking was a ♥♥♥♥♥ though. Not all paths I couldn't go through because of lack of some ability were visible on the map, and map markers you put on the room you are currently in are barely visible, because  your character icon obstructs it – this one made me roll my eyes every time for real.   I also didnt like one of the later traversal unlocks, as it was too slow and killed the momentum, especially in mentioned before exploration. I late game MV should gain more pace IMO, and Voidwrought does gain it, but this ability totally killed it for me.  Overall the game was not groundbreaking, but it was good.
Great game

В очередной раз убеждаюсь, что подавляющее большинство фанатов Magnum Opus («halav nigh») - это секта безмозглых хомяков, не способных ни на какой непредвзятый анализ, ведь их глаза беспросветно заварены швом сварщика 6-го разряда «Жукентием Половичем».  Да, игра явно использует визуальный стиль Magnum Opus, да, есть похожие геймплейные моменты. Но игра делает это хорошо, она вполне себе самобытная и выделяется из тени трупа на золотом троне. Я с удовольствием провел в игре почти 30 часов, прошел ее 5 раз на всех сложностях и поместил в топ-10 своих любимых метроидов, в то время как к «Августейшей особе» я не хочу прикасаться даже в перчатках.  Кажется, это болото затягивает тем глубже, чем больше с ним взаимодействуешь, поэтому - к ОБЗОРУ.  МУЗЫКА. Я много играл в метроиды с прекрасной музыкой, и эта игра не исключение. Что уж говорить: когда после прохождения игры ты покупаешь ее саундтрек, он в ней ни разу не средний. Заглавная тема меня просто убила. Пока я настраивал управление, я услышал, пожалуй, СОЧНЕЙШЕЕ СОЛО НА ГИТАРЕ, ever существовавшее в видеоиграх этого жанра. Я невольно начал подпрыгивать на кресле с мыслями: «Твою мать, вот это качает!» Монгольское горловое пение на старте так вообще заставляло меня покрываться мурашками, пока я не привык.  ГЕЙМПЛЕЙ. После такого аудиального оргазма здесь, на удивление, просто нормально. Есть как хорошие решения, так и не очень, а местами как будто и вовсе забыли про некоторые фичи. Например, мне понравились механики дэша и хватания за уступы, которых нет изначально в наборе способностей героя. Так мало того, дэш еще и не дает тебе неуязвимость - это где вообще такое видано? Но именно поэтому эта деталь так и ценна. Все привыкли к этой условности, а тут - новый взгляд на вещи, хоть и довольно маленький в перспективе.  Наличие «шестеренчатых» головоломок меня честно удивило и порадовало: довольно приятно пошевелить мозгами в перерыве от спинномозгового мэшинга кнопки атаки (хотя он и залипательный). Единственное - они все одинаковые. Хоть их и 3 на всю игру, я бы как игрок хотел видеть измененный паттерн или, например, большее число дисков. Так же классно стилизованы пассивки, ака души, которые выглядят как враги или NPC из игры. Да, лор «дарксолс» - додумай сам и прочитай все в предметах, но он при этом мрачный и лично для меня интересный. Я большой фанат всякой космической хтони, и мне очень понравились локации поверхности и подводной лодки с экипажем из людей-машин. Если проецировать это визуально, то действительно ловишь ужас от осознания произошедшего в мире игры П*ЗД*ЦА.  А вот что мне не совсем понравилось, так это отсутствие по большей части функционального применения мощной атаки. Да, в игре есть пара головоломок с кубами, которые двигаются ей, 1 босс, где можно прятаться за платформой, применив эту атаку, и ЦЕЛЫХ 2.5 моба, которые этой атакой убиваются, но как будто бы здесь не реализован большой потенциал. Например, босс, который бы покрывался панцирем, или доп. прокачка этой атаки, чтобы ее можно было использовать как альтернативу основной. Сами боссы визуально интересные, но у них действительно не хватает набора атак и какого-то динамического ИИ (буквально накинуть каждому еще по +2 - и было бы уже совсем по-другому даже без изменения ИИ).  Режимы НГ+ концептуально интересные, я таких не встречал, но как будто бы этого всё равно мало. Да, это лучше, чем просто +урон и +ХП мобам, но НГ+ на то и НГ+. Игрок, который уже пробежал все локации, просто не получает челленджа: игра скипается за час. Хоть это и весело - спамить все тринкеты (большинство из которых абсолютно бесполезные и не вносят никакого урона, хотя визуально прикольные), кривая сложности стремительно летит вниз. Если юзер не развлекает себя сам, то ему банально станет скучно.  Как по мне, это прекрасный представитель жанра - что визуально, что аудиально, что геймплейно (хоть в этом пункте и с огрехами). По моему личному мнению, игра намного больше берет от Ultros, если конечно разработчики вообще ей вдохновлялись / хотя бы слышали о ней. Так, например, сад и его OST показались мне списанными с очистителя Геггамоджа из вышеупомянутой Ultros.  Однозначно к ознакомлению всем причастным к жанру метроидвания.

You don't have to worry about making it back to a save point.  I played it for an hour and wasn't really having fun.  I got a few upgrades and they honestly didn't feel like they did much. Voidwrought, o silksong antes de Silksong ter lançado.  Dito isso, esse jogo é maravilhoso, sua estética feita a mão é muito bonita, as animações são muito fluídas, os cenários são um dos pontos altos do game e as músicas? Dê um Oscar para o compositor.  Sobre a gameplay, Voidwrought literalmente bebe da fonte de HK, o sistema de amuletos por exemplo é muito semelhante, o ataque principal é meio básico, só com alterações de dano conforme avança na história, mas em questão de exploração de mapa, pra mim Voidwrought supera HK, os primeiros minutos/horas são mais lentas, mas após pegar o dash na terceira área, vira simplesmente cinema.  De dificuldade, honestamente, não muito alta. Os bosses são até previsíveis e com ataques repetidos, eu morri mais para inimigos padrões doq para bosses em si.  Sobre lore, você é a última esperança de três irmãos para salvar um reino, feito do vazio, seu objetivo? Matar o que causou tudo. Uma história simples, mas a questão de você ser feito no vazio é muito explorada com seus upgrades, que melhoraram seu corpo diretamente, como o dash que tem o nome de “Contração Muscular.”  Por fim, a platina, uma das mais divertidas que eu já fiz, ela exige que você zere o jogo no mínimo 5 vezes (uma para desbloquear NG+, três rotas de NG+ e o modo hardcore). Mas a gameplay é tão fluída e dinâmica que eu fiz as 4 runs finais em uma noite, de resto, são conquistas de história. Platina 10/10  Em geral, se estiver em uma promoção e você quiser ter um gostinho de Silksong, Voidwrought é minha recomendação depois de HK. Recomendo muito

Voidwrought completely pulled me in from the very first minutes. The atmosphere is incredibly dark and the visuals are beautifully stylized. It offers smooth and satisfying controls, and a combat system that feels great to master. Every run through the map brings new discoveries and a real sense of adventure, showing just how much care the developers put into it. I was genuinely surprised in the best way possible. there’s always something new to uncover, and it never feels boring. Boss fights are especially memorable, each one unique and challenging, with no copy-paste designs.  It also gives off strong Hollow Knight vibes and thats what i love about it, but honestly it plays even better. What I also really appreciate is how the game respects the player  it almost feels like it takes care of you like your own son. There’s no frustrating death penalty, secrets are readable and you can actually spot them.   Also, one of the u could say ,, ability '' is hidden so well that I literally went around finding all the others before realizing I’d had it with me the whole time... the small dev team are absolute trolls for that one (in a good way).  Overall, Voidwrought is an absolute peak. A highly addictive metroidvania with a unique art style, great level design, and a compelling world that keeps you pushing forward. You can really feel the passion behind it. Highly recommended.

This game was played in its entirety on a MacBook Air M1 with 8GB Ram and a 7 Core GPU via CrossOver 26.0. I encountered no bugs or glitches and all achievements were gained at the appropriate time.  This was a really great Metroidvania. I've seen many people refer to it as a "meat and potatoes" MV (in that there is nothing wrong with it, but it is fairly plain and not very exciting), but I do feel that this game brought a lot to the genre.  I played this game to its completion five times: 1) one main playthrough for 100% and the true ending, 2-4) doing each of the path challenge play throughs, and 5) a hardcore run. I'm not sure everyone will want to do this much in this game, but I enjoyed it enough to go for everything.  The audio work deserves the most praise. Even after five play throughs, I never got tired of the music. Each area has an amazing sound track to go with it. There is quite a range of music here too, from a melancholy tune that features kitars, to upbeat kpop style tracks. The sound effects are very good too and they are designed smartly. The things you'll hear most often (your swing and yourself being hit) are fairly minor sounds that do not get annoying. Rarer things (like teleporting) have an awesome bass thump to them (that you can really feel).  The graphics are really great. They are colorful and quite varied. Each area has its own theme, and that also tends to include a fairly limited palette for the area. You have the bright greens and golds of the garden, the cold grey stone of the commons, and the purple psychedelic wavy effect for the void, as examples. There are two negatives to the graphics: 1. When you get hit, your body turns almost completely translucent. You do get a generous invicibility timer on each hit (almost 2 seconds, it feels like) but you can easily lose yourself in a hectic fight because you've gone translucent, and then you get hit again, which keeps you translucent, and so on. And 2. There are two bosses that have projectiles that really blend in with the background. It's not the worst, but there has been so much quality of life given to this game through its patches, that I wish this would be corrected.  The gameplay is the reason you stick around for five play throughs. It's extremely solid, with two exceptions. The movement traversal abilities are varied, and you'll see some common abilities (double jump, dash, hook shot) and some highly unusual ones (machine possession and slithering, as examples). They all work really well and I used them all throughout the game. The controls are well designed, and they feel very snappy. There is a lot to collect in this one, but (other than the five play throughs required for a true 100%) it does not overstay its welcome.  The two gameplay aspects that I did not like were: 1. Your movement is much floatier than I would like. I got used to it, for the most part, but even towards the end of my time with the game, it was still way too hard to actually jump and land on a really small platform. And 2. Enemies, across the board, have way too much health. This is at its worst at the start of your first play through, as well as your hardcore play through (if you don't combine them). When you have no damage upgrades and no abilities or passives, your main option for defeating enemies is just to stand there, whacking away at them for what feels like way too long. Sure, towards the end, you feel very powerful, but I would be ok with every enemy (regular and boss) having maybe 75% of their current health.  Thanks for reading this far, and I hope this helps you decide if the game is for you!

Гра в найкращому жанрі для мене: метроїдванія. Інші ігри цього жанру які я грав, подобались або дуже сильно подобались, я й не думав, що це може коли небуть змінитись. Але це сталось, цю гру мені є більше за що сварити ніж хвалити. Також багато чув про цю гру, що розробники просто взяли Hollow Knight, зробили все за гарною формулою, нічого нового не додали. З цим не погоджуюсь. В грі присутні багато цікавих ідей, загадок, деяких цікавих босів. Почну з сильних сторін гри: 1. Графіка та дизайн гри. Приємно дивитись на таку картинку, навіть дуже. 2. Звукові ефекти та музика класні, в кожній локації щось унікальне, моторошне, загадкове. 3. Цікаво досліджувати світ. Як тільки отримуєш нову здібність, відразу мчу відкривати попередні місцини де я був і де розумів, що потрібна саме ця здібність. Ніби і звична справа для метроїдваній, але азарт і бажання є. Тепер недоліки гри, які я для себе побачив: 1. Персонаж відчувається важким в прямому сенсі цього слова. Здається, що він важить 200кг. Стрибок виконується як і в інших подібних іграх-платормерах, але саме приземлення - дуже незручне, важке, неприроднє. Впливає на ігролад - важко вхопитись за виступи та платформи. Ніби з часом звикається, але все одно інколи напружує. 2. Нові збідності дають практично в перших секундах гри. Ну й нехай, так задумано. Але загалом в грі дуууже багато здібностей. Важко назвати здібність з подібних ігор, яких тут немає. Подвійний стрибок, гак для стін, деш, навіть другий деш додали. Як на мене з цим перемудрили. Оскільки дуже часто гравець отримуж щось, в нього немає вже тих відчуттів, які б він отримав після важкого здобування здібності, або коли здібність дають через досить довгий проміжок часу. В цій же грі, цього явно забагато. Деякі здібності мені навіть не знадобились, оскільки я вже мав на той момент іншу здібність, яка дозволяла мені потрапити в ці місця. Цей момент явно не продуманий. 3. З босами взагалі біда. Я не про кількість, а про якість, виклик, цікаві задумки. Загалом вони всі дуууже легкі. Фінального боса я поклав з другої спроби. З другої спроби карл! Виклику ніякого немає. Коли я зустрів наступного боса, відув, що ідея в нього цікава і з надією почав битись з ним. Але в результаті це був ще один простенький сильний моб. Сам дизайн моделей босів - класний. В декількох навіть закладені класні ідеї, але цього не досить, явно не досить. Цим пунктом дуже розчарований. Особисто для мене пункти з недоліків переважають над позитивними сторонами гри. Нажаль не можу рекомендувати цю гру.

I play a TON of Metroidvanias. I got Voidwrought in a bundle and it hadn't previously been on my radar. This title really floored me!  The movement abilities are quite fun, the map is top notch, and the sense of exploration is very well done. You'll happily be retracing the (rather large) map over and over as you unlock more traversal abilities.  The base difficulty is quite reasonable, with no one boss requiring more than a dozen or so attempts, and there are SO many upgrades to be found that if you're really getting knocked around by a baddie you can simply buff up and return. In fact, the final boss was a breeze because I spent so much time collecting health and weapon charges.  My only real gripe - and this is probably just a *me* problem - is that the lore is pretty opaque and there are almost too many NPCs to interact with. This lead me to ignore some of what was going on at my "home base", so I didn't discover the unearth/renovation stuff until I was ~12 hours in. That's silly, and robbed me of a good deal of fun along the way. Don't be like me, talk to the NPCs in the starting area throughout your play!  8/10

Good metroidvania.  What you need to know: 1. It's a pretty classic metroidvania. Go forward until you can't then go another way. Unlock movement upgrades and then more paths open and you can access stuff you couldn't before. 2. It can be difficult at the start with low health and no damage upgrades, but difficulty levels out and gets pretty easy by the end. Only one boss took more than a few tries besides the last boss. 3. I would highly recommend it for the fun bosses and the VERY cool ambiance.

Great metroidvania. Cool music, although it's mostly ambient-ish (I really wish there were more tracks like the bazaar music, which is really great), and some kind of out-of-place over-the-top metal tracks at some boss fights. Great art. Very cool lore/story, although you have to piece it together from all the journals, whispering stones, descriptions, and conversations (like Dark Souls, and not many do it as well as this game does it, IMO). A bit on the easy side for an experienced MV/platformer player, even with the NG+ challenge runs. 100%ing it is a bit excessive and requires minimum 4 playthroughs, but likely 5, because if you try to do one of the NG+ runs on hardcore (one life mode) you are pretty much screwed, because you only heal when you die on that mode (and, obviously you can't die on hardcore mode or your save is gone). With good routing and knowledge of the game, the NG+ runs are like, 1-2h long each. Nice pretty nonlinear design, with many valid paths through the game. Some movement abilities can be used to sequence break many areas (which is likely intended), especially if you have the hook and the magic that creates a hook-point; with that, you can go almost anywhere. I just wish it had more meaty challenge in the enemy and boss design (it might seem like enemies are too tanky at first, but with a couple upgrades you just start shredding them to bits). Skilled or very experienced MV/platformer players will probably find it too easy, but I still found it fun despite the lack of difficulty.

Fun metroidvania. Plays great on Steam Deck. Decent challenge, but not near as difficult as Hollow Knight. Took me a little over 15 hours to complete.  I saw a number of negative reviews for Voidwrought, but I’m a metroidvania junkie and thought I would give it a chance. Refunded. At first glance, the game looks pretty interesting: the art seems stylish, the atmosphere is there, and at first it gives the impression that it might be something in the spirit of Hollow Knight. But in practice, it feels much weaker. One clear downside is the lack of Ukrainian language support. For a game that could at least partly rely on atmosphere, lore, or worldbuilding, that is already a minus. If the story and writing were strong enough, maybe that could have compensated for some of the other problems, but that did not happen here. The art itself is not complete trash, but it feels too blocky and stiff. On screenshots it looks better than it does in motion. Overall, it is passable, but definitely not impressive enough to carry the game. The biggest problem is the gameplay. There is almost no mobility. The combat feels weak and unsatisfying. The dash/slide is terrible: short, awkward, and almost useless in real fights. There is no proper dash, no parry, and no real feeling that the character is smooth or satisfying to control. The attacks are repetitive, and the enemies feel inconsistent: some are overly annoying, while others feel completely harmless and lifeless, almost like cardboard. The spells also felt underwhelming and mostly useless. They do not have much impact and often do not even feel strong enough to deal with a single enemy in a satisfying way. In the end, the game feels like a cheap Hollow Knight imitation, but without the things that actually make Hollow Knight great: satisfying movement, impactful combat, memorable enemies, and exploration that feels rewarding on its own. As a metroidvania, it also did not impress me. The secrets feel too obvious, and the backtracking will most likely feel more like a chore just to progress further rather than something genuinely enjoyable. Maybe some people will enjoy it for the atmosphere or general vibe, but for me that was not enough. Refunded.

Played on steam deck and was able to get a consistent 90 fps on uncapped, but probably could lower the tdp and get close to the same or lock it to 60. It is a bit of a hollow knight clone, but it does differentiate itself somewhat with the more Lovecraftian monsters. Exploration was a bit of a pain if I'm honest, although the number of warp points help with that. Bosses were entertaining to fight but nothing you haven't seen before. Was an enjoyable play but not groundbreaking.

The game is decent, but does have some issues. First off, you unlock fast travel pretty quickly which is nice, and you get some cool movement abilities including this wall blob ability which is pretty unique. Theres a good deal of bosses and enemies with varied attacks, and the difficulty, while tough, feels pretty fair for the most part. Some bosses do have wayyyyy too much health which can be annoying but for the move part you've forced to learn patterns which takes a couple tries. Hitboxes are good for the most part, save 1-2 bosses, platforming is tight. Theres a few varied environments including the surface but for the most part is dark blocks and temple like structures. Theres also some different special abilities that have varied functions you unlock to play around with, as well as some relics that can affect things. Its also fun with uncovering parts of your base as you progress along. The visuals as well as the style of the game is pretty nice to look at, including animations.   For the relics you basically will use 3 the entire game, which was healing, more health, and i-frames, which should have just been something included in the game and not wasted on a relic. The abilities I did change here or there, but I still used just a select 4-5 throughout the entire game, as some are clearly better. There should be health bars for enemies/bosses, as some bosses the fight goes on for like 3 min and you have no idea how much damage you're doing and how close you were to winning for next try. This lack of information also diminishes experimenting with different abilities as you can't visualize the effectiveness. Big swing and a miss there.   The game has tons of ways to go and at times you can just continue to wander. Theres no objective markers for no good reason, and the map does a poor job of giving you information. I would see doors or rooms I hadn't gone into, but I had no idea if it was an ability gated door, a path not yet taken, etc, leading to backtracking there only to realize I couldn't go that way. They also don't list vendors or other things, so the map leaves a lot to be desired and can be quite frustrating to navigate. It would have also been nice to get more than just claws as it does get kind of boring just mashing the basic attack from minute 1 to the final part of the game, more weapons increased novelty. You'll also get a ton of items that are never explained and you have no idea what to do with them and you may never use, a common complaint with the game. The story is meh and ends VERY abruptly as you kill the boss, no cutscene or text or anything.  The other issue is how the game is laid out. Many of the necessary traversal mechanics seem to be hidden in rooms or random boss encounter rooms which aren't signposted and you kind of just wander into. Then you need to wander around the map hoping to find where to use them because of poor map information. Later on in the game I spent almost an hour looking to find the last traversal upgrade to progress further, going over the map multiple times, only for it to be in a random room in 1 corner blocked from 1 side. This is kind of how it is, and instead of funneling you into these necessary upgrades it seems haphazard. That would make sense for random relics or special abilities, but not traversal mechanics.   Overall the game is still fun and I enjoyed my time with it, but there were many things that could have been tightened up and fixed. 7.5/10.

The three pillars of a metroidvania are combat, movement, and exploration. I can safely say, I don't think Voidwrought excels in any of these.  Combat felt flaccid, and never satisfying. The lack of any real defensive options like blocking or parrying means you are basically locked to using one of your 3 artifact slots to give your dash invo frames. That or you can just run away from every attack pattern, or face tank I guess. Neither of those feels good, though. Related to combat, I also didn't enjoy any of the boss fights, really. Which is a shame, because those are usually my favourite part of any game.  Movement actually felt really nice... after you unlocked every movement ability. Which won't be until about 90% of the way through the game. Or you could be like me and actually miss a movement ability [spoiler] I missed the double jump [/spoiler] on my playtrough. This results in...  Exploration feeling bad because you have to slowly trod through places you been over and over again. Even after unlocking fast travel, the fast travel points are fairly spread out, so likely you'll still need to go through 5 or 6 rooms before getting to where you want to go. I know it's a metroidvania and the name of the game is unlocking new areas via getting new movement abilities, but even in that genre it felt like an excessive amount of backtracking right from the very start of the game.  Overally, I'd give it a 3/10. I think the art is fantastic, but gameplay wise, it just doesn't hold up.

Steam says I played 12 hours, but the in game timer says 16, and I trust that one more. Played on Steam Deck.  Fantastic game, took maybe an hour or two for me to actually get into it - things start a little slow until you start getting some real upgrades. Tons of options for passive and active abilities, many of the upgrades felt big and meaningful. Really enjoyed the boss fights - balance felt good where sometimes you'd hit "this feels impossible, I'll come back later" then you'd get an upgrade that makes the fight doable (and occasionally easy if you use the right combo of abilities). Exploration and movement felt great.  I'm sure I'll be playing through this one again to make different choices.

I just completed Voidwrought. 30 hours but I wish the game was 100 hours because I personally enjoyed everything about this game.  Before I played, I read multiple disparaging things about this game.   1-I read that the bosses were too easy , and that was probably true when the game was first released, but the developers made updates that balanced the gameplay and there wasn’t ANY boss that I just walked through. In fact, most of them took me ￼more than 20 times but it was fun on every attempt .   2-I read that this looks like a flash game. I don’t know why that’s derogatory (especially when everyone raves about pixel art) and I read the same thing about the Watcher Chronicles, which is also a game that I absolutely loved, but I found the visuals in this game to be absolutely awesome.   3-I read that this was just a Hollow Knight re-skin clone which if you played through the game to the end, you would know that’s just lazy opinion-hazing. I also don’t understand why copying HK is such a taboo level bad thing. Why do we praise Castlevania clones and Metroid clones but lambast anything that clones HK? I wish there was more HK type games.  All in all, this was one of the most fun MV games that I have played and I will stand on that rock just like I did with BIO-GUN, which is also a way better game than most of these overly lauded and much loved games that everybody else brags about. We are all entitled to our opinions, right?  Iron Diamond, Bo:Path of the Teal Lotus, BIO-GUN, and now Voidwrought are all underrated MV games that I have played in the last year that were a complete blast. I would take those games over AfterImage or Timespinner and I definitely had way more fun with them than I did with 9Sols.🤷🏻‍♂️  But thats just my opinion of course 😉

Voidwrought é um jogo similar a Hollow Knight. Pode até comparar com holow mas HK é melhor, porém só de ser similar e chegar perto de HK eu indico o jogo.   Voidwrought  é um belo metroidvania, com arte feita a mão e por sinal muito bem feitas! Com gráfico muito bom na minha opinião, pois se a arte do jogo é boa então o gráfico é bom!  Trilha sonora esse sim, é o ponto mais forte, as músicas são muito boa, nenhuma enjoativa, e caí bem em cada fase,  da pra ver que focaram bastante no som do jogo, que fizeram com carinho e o som ajuda os requistos abaixo.   Diversão  e exploração é muito boa,  a trilha sonora ajuda muito a vc voltar o mapa todinho com vontade, e os upgrades são da hora, alguns são necessários para passar de uma parte do jogo porém vc pode chegar no final com o mínimo de upgrades e itens, vc pode pular o caminho várias vezes e isso não vai te dificultar já que o jogo é muito bom mas na questão de dificuldade deixou um pouco a desejar, poderia deixar no estilo hollow, mas os bosses são bem fáceis um ou outro pode tomar um pouco do seu tempo, achei poucos boss no jogo e poderia ter mais e poderia colocar até mais monstros bem diferentes, mas isso não tira o mérito de Voidwrought. Uma coisa top na diversão é a questão dos upgrades, achei top demais ter colocado..... vishi ..... esqueci o nome.... em fim ele vira um metamorfo ou tipo uma neblina de sinfonia da noite mais bem diferente a mecânica e achei isso muito bom mesmo abrindo um leque de possibilidades de chegar em vários lugares.  A história é tipo Afterimage... não é lá essas coisas.... não é ruim.... mais também não é tão boa suficiente a ponto de vc querer ler todas as conversas, por mais que tenha pouco diálogos a história focada nos tempos dos Deuses deixa muito fantasiosa e imaginário, ou seja.... vc precisa ficar viajando na maionese pra entender certas coisas, confesso que não é o tipo de história que me prende, mas mesmo assim o jogo é bom suficiente pra te prender  te levar até o final do jogo querendo achar os itens ou fazer 100% do mapa, a história não faz mudar sua jogatina assim como em Afterimage.  CONCLUINDO:  GRÁFICO: 9 (ARTE FEITA A MÃO TOP)  HISTÓRIA:8 (DEPENDE DO GOSTO DA PESSOA SE GOSTA DE HISTÓRIAS FANTASIOSAS E QUE NÃO VAI DIRETO AO PONTO)  DIVERSÃO:9,5 ( JOGO TE LEVA COM UMA TRILHA SONORA TOP , E MOVIMENTAÇÃO RÁPIDA E UM MAPA BEM AGRADAVÉL COM MISTÉRIOS)  TRILHA SONORA:10 ( ESSE JOGO PODE PERDER PRA HOLLOW, MAS NA  MINHA OPINIÃO NA TRILHA SONORA ESSE GANHA DE HK, MÚSICAS MUITO BEM FEITAS TOP)   VALE APENA O PREÇO?  O preço cheio  60 conto.... uhnmahaiiiiiimmm maaaaais ou bénoss mais ou ménoosss, eu peguei na promo ( aham grande novidade) e pra mim na promo não bateu nenhum arrependimento, dependendo da sua condição financeira eu diria que ele vale apena o valor inteiro, mas se vc não é um cara que pega jogos por 300 ou 200 contos sempre pois quer economizar, então  é melhor pegar numa promoção e vc não vai se arrepender. Agora se vc pega sempre jogos acima de 200 conto sem problemas, então pega esse no preço cheio sem medo de ser feliz!  Valeu fui!

The more you play this game, the more it falls apart. Even its place in the genre is questionable: the metroidvania rookies would do better playing other titles, the veterans won't see anything new. Now to the pros and cons:  + Great music (throat singing OP) + Feels nice at the beginning + The surface location looks nice + The lost expedition actually offers some kinda mystery  - Broken boss that just won't attack - A lot of resources that have up to no apparent use (divine remnants, artifacts) - Secrets and resources are just there to comply with the formula, but aren't enjoyable to find - 1.5 attacks for the whole game - QOL hidden behind equipable relics. You would do good to always have short healing and more healing equiped, so not much of a choice here - Game gives you no info on enemy health and your damage. The active powers unlocks feel randomly scattered around the map and are rarely useful and/or interesting - The Gish form allows you to travel the whole map rending future movement upgrades obsolete - Still, the Gish sections are very slow and tedious - Map without auto-markers for upgrades, the void sections entrances look just like a door you haven't entered, so you ought to have useless backtracking if you haven't marked them - Still, there are too few markers to note all the different things that require additional powers to go through - Some of the NPCs speak in wannabefromsoftware gibberish, much like in Last Faith. That makes some quests very hard to understand. Others are quite simple, though - Closer to the end the game becomes annoyingly easy  I could go on and on, but I think you've got the picture by now. At one point, an hour or so before 100 %-ing it, I just couldn't answer a question "why am I still playing this?". A shame, for this might've been a good game, espectially considering all the assets. Maybe they could've actually make some turn-based colony simulator in the same world with the same assets and it would be a much better choice.  It's half-baked to say the least.

I'm giving this a no but if you are a diehard metroidvania fan it might be worth giving it a chance. Unfortunately the game is just pretty meh and isn't very long. I normally 100% these types of games but after one playthrough I felt like I had enough and from other reviews it seems that 100% would only take an additional 10-15 hrs.  A lot of the abilities you gather to get to new areas don't feel very impactful and how the world connects with it self feels a bit weird. A lot of things I collected I wasn't even sure what it was for or just didn't seem very useful. Some of the upgrades in the game I felt were not spaced out in a way that was very helpful. Maybe I just missed stuff or didn't remember to go back for it but I spent more time in the game than I would've wanted stuck on 2 heals and no damage upgrades and by the time I started getting them I got multiple back to back since I had enough abilities to move around the map.  I think the biggest problem I had with the game was the combat. There are occassional rooms where you have to fight 3 waves of enemies however after defeating 1 wave the next wave can just spawn on top of you making damage that feels really bad. It also seems that the boss fights were not designed with a player pogoing on their head in mind. Multiple bosses had at least half their attacks nullified just with a simple pogo and it made them insanely easy.  Overall I think the game isn't terrible but definitely fumbles in certain parts that other metroidvania titles have figured out. If you are a real die hard for metroidvanias pick it up on sale, otherwise I would skip.

First review I didn't recommend the game after 3.8 hours of playing. After beating the true ending, there's a lot more to like, but there's still so many problems I have with it.  Let's start with initial impressions and how my opinion has changed.  1. "Enemies are stupidly tanky."  This has done a 180 after realizing I hadn't gotten any upgrades (which I will address later.) I got all 4 of them back-to-back near the end, and 1-3shot every single boss after (including the true ending.)   Which brings me to my first big criticism: Damage isn't tuned very well, especially with how the game lacks direction (again, will address later.) The only boss who was challenging, was what I call the "elevator boss." Turns out, I thought he had too much health...but it would seem as though not having any damage upgrades was the true culprit. Funnily enough, he's the only boss I remember, and ultimately the best boss in the game.  2. "Clunky lockout between abilities."  While it's not close to Silksong levels of fluid, it becomes a lot more enjoyable to control your character as you keep playing.   3. "Bosses are solid, but the gauntlets? Not so much." Bosses become too easy after you get upgrades. Some of their mechanics are still pretty good, but as a whole it's not Voidwrought's strength. Gauntlets consistently spawn enemies right on top of you. Early on, there are gauntlets which spawn way too much in a very small area. Unsure how I didn't die...still didn't feel right.  4. "Assassins teleporting onto you in the Court Gauntlet are BS." Yep. They do damage with their teleport animation, which is the equivalent of taking damage when someone pulls their sword back before they swing. Worst monster design in the game by far. I'm not being spiteful here. The worst other enemies get is "they're overly simplistic" far as I can remember.  Now, onto new things that turn this game from an easy 8/10 to a 6.5/10.  - Map progression is incredibly messy. I love exploration, but at one point I had...37 markers on the map. All over the map. After getting a new ability, I went to ALL of them...and made 20 more for a *new* ability. It happened 2 more times to lesser degrees.   You could say this is a fantastic use of limited map space, but I think it shows a complete lack of direction. Voidwrought took a key point to metroidvanias and pushed it to 150%. Some liked it, but I got screwed over by two things here (spoilers ahead):  1. I refused to help Achretis. They sent me to the Court. What this made me do, was: 2. Not meet Ose until after I beat the final boss of the game. He was at the door to the Court...the place I was teleported into. I got out of it by finding a warp zone. So yeah, choosing an option broke a key point of progression.   I also never saw him in a shrine, if I'm remembering correctly...so I didn't get any explanation. Not properly looking at the controls is on me. It didn't work once, so I didn't try again. I later realized the up/down controls get locked out easily.   - Map outline has very poor contrast. Combined with how map exploration works, I missed a few small areas due to not realizing I hadn't visited them yet. Even Ender Lilies map felt more useful, which is really weird for me to say. It may have been a me issue, but I don't see a reason to not make map walls more apparent/visible.  - When you take damage, your character flashes in and out of existence, making it easy to lose track of where you are in some encounters. So not all the time, but very disruptive on occasion.  Anyways, I could add more. This review is way too long, but I get the most frustrated with games that are really fun, but have a few problems that just...ruin it for me. It probably makes me an A-hole to say Voidwrought is "wasted potential," but it's what I genuinely think.   The game constantly shifts from a 5 to an 8.5 or even a 9. If the game is on a heavy discount, then I think it's worth buying if you're ok with having very little explained and having no idea what to do a lot of the time (like looking at the map and not seeing anywhere to go. I really hope they improve map readability via contrasting the walls.) I'm ok with it to a degree, but this playthrough really felt bad due to it at times.

This is not the best metroidvania that you will ever play, but it is a solid 7/10. It has its faults, with slightly janky jumps and floaty controls that take getting used to. It's not very complex, and combat is rather basic. The balance is a bit wonky. The mood is there, but the story itself is nothing to write home about.  On the other hand, it's pretty good at being a solid metroidvania. The exploration is satisfying, the game is very open. You can get upgrades in many different orders, and there is tons of reasons to backtrack with new abilities in tow. The progression in power is satisfying. The secondary weapons give a nice choice of styles. The difficulty is on the easier side, which is sometimes nice after battling through another game that demands your full focus and attention.  If that sounds good, give this one a try!

Si te gustan los metroidvania, indudablemente, debes darle una oportunidad a Voidwrought. Comenzaré por lo que me pareció que hizo. Cuenta con una gran variedad de armas/poderes, lo que hace el combate muy dinámico, también la velocidad de ataque en relación con la respuesta  de las veces que presionamos el botón para atacar, es prácticamente instantánea, por lo que si somos rápidos, podemos hacer muchos ataques seguidos. La ambientación lovercrafniana llama mucho la atención, ya que estamos como en un mundo espacial rodeados de criaturas deformes y extrañas, una temática poco explorada para un metroidvania y que lo hace llamativo. Las peleas con los jefes ofrecen algún distintivo que lo hace único, por lo que son muy entretenidas. Las habilidades para desplazarte por los mapas, aunque muchas ya se han visto en el género, como el doble salto o rebotar en las paredes, hay otras muy únicas que resaltan la temática espacial/lovecrafniana. La música, aunque está bien, es solo eso, está bien y diría que no tiene ningún tema que destaque mucho, cumple con lo que debe. Ahora, pasaré con lo que no me gustó. La sensibilidad para controlar al personaje, es muy alta, por lo que todo el tiempo sientes que estás patinando, como si estuvieras en un piso de hielo o algo así. Los bosses, aunque ya mencioné que sus combates muy entretenidos, falla en el aspecto en que son pocos y cuando digo pocos, diría que no llegan ni a 10, por lo que puedes pasar rato sin tener un enfrentamiento importante. Los escenarios no se conectan muy bien salvo por algunas excepciones y en cuanto a las horas de juego, yo, que exploré muchísimo, tardé apenas 12 horas, por lo que es relativamente corto en comparación con otros del género.

Based on the other reviews, people seem to really like this game.  Honestly I don't understand the enthusiasm.   I feel kind of silly giving this game a "don't recommend" after having 100%ed it.  But it's a Metroidvania-collectathon, and the controls are smooth enough that it's satisfying in a certain lizard brain sense.  (Plays well on SteamDeck too FWIW.)  Finishing and 100%ing was pretty much effortless, and the downsides weren't annoying enough to get me to quit half finished.  But the overall experience was unremarkable in a way that kind of meta-annoys me.    If you're the kind of person who rolls your eyes at the phrase "cohesive vision," then read no more, count this as an upvote instead.  But this game lacks a cohesive vision.  Every aspect is kind of derivative and seems to have been added with no real goal, just ticking off a list of Metroidvania standards without any heart to it.      The art is weird, it's kind of hard to scan visually, like everything is kind of muddled, and nothing is really thematically consistent.   And the writing is just straight gobbledeguk.  It's all just word salad trying to sound weird and cryptic but you can tell it's meaningless.      The monsters don't make any thematic sense, and they all have like, 1 move.  The bosses may have a few but they're pretty straightforward and predictable and the difficulty is scaled by making them spongier or throwing too many guys at you in a locked room.  And the map layout... the Metroidvania of it all, the unlocking abilities and backtracking, it appears that to avoid really fine tuning the flow of the game and for fear of soft locking or sequence breaking, most of the secret paths will be accessible through 3 or 4 different skills.   By the end, it was so predictable that wiping out the whole thing was akin to coloring in all the squares on a sheet of graph paper.

A solid metroidvania through and through. I enjoyed the art, especially the design of the player character and his various animations for new moves you get from progression. The bosses are pretty well tuned and some are pretty creative with how they incorporate your various movement abilities to interact. Difficulty I would say is overall normal, not easy but not brutal either. I killed most bosses either first time or in a handful of tries (3-5). There are a few hidden bosses as well. I finished with the true end in about 17 hours, so it's decently meaty but doesn't overstay its welcome. The exploration is quite open ended after the first few areas, so if you like freedom in a metroidvania, its an easy recommendation. The story and lore are a bit cryptic. I prefer the storytelling to be a bit more up front, at least for the main objectives. And because I didn't complete 100%, I'm still missing a few lore texts that might help me get the full picture. Regardless, if story is a big demand, this one might be hard to work with. I did come to appreciate the sense of wonder the game's strange world had, but I didn't quite understand my player character's exact place it in, or why the antagonizing figures were doing what they were doing. Regardless, the enjoyable world and gameplay made up for it in my opinion. My only other gripe would be the tunnel sections reminiscent of morphball segments in metroid. I appreciate the way the way the power equivalent in this game works, but it can be a bit slow and tedious when used for long stretches which kind of causes exploration to grind to a crawl in these sections. I think if the movement of both you and the enemies in these tunnels could be cranked up, it could make them a bit more tense and fun, because there are kind of a lot of tunnels to find secrets in, and finding the secrets was fun, but tunneling through to get to them was kind of a slog. All in all, I give this game an 8.5/10. If I am being objective it might be closer to an 8, but I have a bias due to the cool art design that has a sort of ancient and mystical wonder to it. Music and sound design are both pretty great too. I would absolutely be down to play more of this, so if the dev launches any updates or DLC, I would certainly buy it and hop back in.

This game was absolutely phenomenal!   I have played (at least tried to play) almost every MV that has come out since Ori and Blind Forest got me completely hooked on the genre. A lot of games in this genre just miss the mark a bit and don't keep me engaged enough and as a Metroidvania fan at heart my games need to draw me in and keep me wanting more, Voidwrought did that in a big way. It's easily the best Metroidvania I have played in several years and kept me engaged throughout. And while It does take a large amount of influence from the fields top games (here's looking at you Hollow Knight and Ori), its done in a way that doesn't feel derivative! I was worried about that initially but by only 2 hours in i could really tell that this had its own heart and legs to stand on.   The art style and music are so incredible, I don't know if this expresses how much i think good music can do to keep the game moving and fun, but these compositions really kept me in the game. The art was very well done and created a vibe/asthetic all its own. The Sci-Fi smashed fantasy world is cool as hell!   What truly got me hooked was- The gameplay. It was engaging as heck, the controls were tight (slightly different from what i have gotten used to in other games in the genre), and the enemies always felt dangerous (i recently played Shadow Labyrinth and wanted to love it but couldn't finish it because the enemies felt too one dimensional). Movement felt great and although they dole out your traversal and movement abilities pretty slow once you get the double jump the game just opens up- like a flower.   AND  I really had fun with these bosses. They were all different and mostly pretty difficult (until/unless you always use the pretty broken relic you can find fairly early). The mid game was so fun that I continued to search and play until i had 100% the map and upgrade options (of which there are many). The last 3 bosses (one that is hidden in plain sight) were incredibly difficult initially, so much so that even with ALL of the upgrades i died more than a few times. Once i really had attack patterns memorized i was able to knock them out though!  My only complaint would be that a few of the souls and relics are OP AF but for a decent chunk of the game i just played without those ones too much to keep my difficulty curve at that sweet spot, where winning isn't guaranteed but still wasn't quite at a  soulslike level.   Overall i give this game a strong 9/10. The only reason its not a 10 is because someone else did it (ever so) slightly better and someone has to be the best (Here's looking at you HK).   IF YOU LIKE METROIDVANIAS- PLAY THIS GAME!  YOU WON'T REGRET IT.

Voidwrought is the single most dissapointing metroidvania i've ever played because it could have been one of my favorites. The setting, visuals & ambience are exactly what i vibe with but the gameplay is so incredibly frustrating that i couldn't help but be glad when i was finally done. There's 3 main reason that made playing the game excruciatingly painful; poor controls, horrible balance and a severe lack of QoL. Let's start with what was immediately obvious namely the controls. The player character in this feels afwul to control because he jumps up rapidly but falls down equally fast as well as feeling slippery as if he doesn't stop moving immediately. There's lots of tiny platforms that you can easily overshoot and to make matters worse bumping into any hazard or enemy knocks you back a lot. It's quite easy to accidentally bump into things with these floaty controls which is made even more annoying due to contact damage on everything. Which leads me to the second and most pronounced painpoint; the combat/balance.  Combat is even worse than the platforming because it's brutally difficult thanks to unbelievably tanky enemies, but especially bosses. Only half an hour in i was met with the game's first boss, one that had multiple phases each with a significant amount of health. This would be fine if attacks were fair like Ender Lilies which also had tanky bosses but in Voidwrought they're not. The final phase was a continious attack that moves left & right where you have to try to sneak in chip damage without bumping into the bosses contact damage hitbox. Based on achievement rate over 20% of all players quit at this first boss which is unsuprising as it is ridiculously hard, i eventually gave up and cheated invincibility. I know this does not give me a good view of how the combat actually is but it still allows me to notice a few glaring issues like the annoying HK esque self pushback on your melees which are very short range anyways so you'll compensate by running forward and take contact damage again, fun!  Despite invincibility i could also notice how tanky everything was from the beginning and it only gets worse. Every boss and every enemy is an absolute damage spunge throughout the entire game unless you find the weapon upgrades which make it slightly less excruciating. I say unless because it's not clear how or when you get this as the game loves to be overly cryptic to the point of basic mechanics being confusing. There's 4 vaults that increase your damage, but they require an ability you get later AND a special key which can be bought. Once i had my ability i went back to these vaults but it didn't do anything cuz i didn't have the key at the time. This vault key isn't exactly obvious where to use either as the vaults only get called vaults once you've opened one... The game is riddled with things like levers stating 'something stirs in the distance' when you hit them, cool & mysterious but not exactly helpful. Even with the damage upgrades in tow though most late game things are still quite tanky, especially bosses.  Most bosses also hover above ground so you have to jump to reach them but the jumping feels bad as mentioned before which makes air combat also horrible. Obviously there's lots of flying enemies to make that as annoying as possible because why not? To make combat EVEN worse you don't get a dash untill several hours into the game at which point you've already killed at least 3 bosses all without a dodge. And the best part? It doesn't even have I-frames as that's an upgrade that takes one out of your meager 3 charm slots and is very expensive. The whole game relies on lots of currency for most basic QoL features and even the very metroidvania progression itself. So even if you wanted this basic feature of dash I-frames you better either grind for it or halt your actual progression. Finally in regards to combat is the healing system which uses flasks, this is fine when the level design accounts for that with good checkpoint distribution to refill but the game doesn't. Which means boss runbacks and a war of attrition between saves with very few flasks that don't even heal that much anyway.  Even if you pop them they have a channel animation which is just an extra difficulty. Most games let you channel a spell which is infinite as you can recover mana but riskier to pull of OR a flask that's near instant which refills at shrines. Voidwrought takes the most punishing aspect of both and combines them to make a difficult game even harder. Again i used invincibility for the most part of the game because i wasn't dealing with all these poor design decisions but they were still noticable. All of this was a great big shame as the enemy designs were fantastic, not their attacks which were unfair, but their actual visual designs. The whole game is filled with some of the coolest character designs i've seen, really nailing that eldritch horror vibe that i love. It's why i'm so dissapointed, i would have loved this if the game wasn't so hellbent to destroy you with unfair combat that's hard to engage with due to faulty controls. Speaking of controls there's a morph ability you unlock later that is used A LOT which controls even worse than the regular controls.   It's also tediously slow to move around in this form and the worst part is that it's very usefull, to the point that it trivializes most other movement upgrades. You can even discover this upgrade before the ones that get rendered obsolete by it, not really great metroidvania design now is it? Another horrible design this game relies on is pogoing with these horrible controls which make it feel even worse than pogo games that do control well. The level design luckily doesn't rely on this too heavily but it does love to be convoluted. Don't get me wrong the game does nonlinearity pretty well but the map cannot keep up with that. It's detailed enough but there's only 4 map pin types and they really don't cut it for all the progression blockers the game has, most of which can eventually be overcome with the morph. Before you have that though you'll backtrack a LOT looking for any clue where to go next as the game completely leaves you in the dark. And if you're looking to backtrack with the fast travel you'll quickly notice that it's slow and tedious.  Why? Because activating a fast travel point takes you into a literal 5s animation before you can even select your warp point (or back out of the menu). Even when you teleport spawning in then takes a few more seconds which adds to a feeling of tedium and time waste, but it's not exclusive to fast travel. Opening levers (the ones that don't give cryptic clues), taking elevators or unburrowing your sanctum give you multiple second long slow cutscenes to waste even more of your time. None are as time wasting as going for 100% in this though, and i'm not talking only about missable content that requires multiple playthroughs, i'm talking about the 100% tool which makes you have to manually check each room for it to tell you you missed something, thus i wasted 2 hours grinding out the last collectibles something i would NOT recommend. The level design as a whole is made to be slow and tedious and the platforming isn't all that great either which makes retreads annoying, especially given that enemies respawn when you re-enter the room.  Given how confusing everything is, how long it takes to do anything and how much progression relies on currency (and thus combat) to excavate your temple this is one MV that does NOT have satisfying progression. At least the visuals are incredible, not only the aforementioned designs but the artstyle as a whole is beautiful. The enviroments are ancient and filled with intrigue and excellent worldbuilding thanks to good albeit obtuse writing. The SFX and music do an incredible job making it even more immersive/atmosphere and performance/polish is stellar but the gameplay is so bad that it's just not worth it.

A mia grande sorpresa questo titolo si è rivelato uno dei più divertenti ed appaganti di quest'anno, ho percepito una sintonia ed una connessione videoludica raramente provata. Probabilmente perché rispecchia pressoché il mio prototipo ideale di metroidvania. Dispiace di non averlo potuto giocare prima, attendevo una traduzione in Italiano che fortunatamente ho beccato su un sito amatoriale che ringrazio, perché per quanto capisca l'Inglese ed ho giocato a titoli non Italiani, è sempre un'altra cosa giocare nella lingua Madre e non avendo fretta ho atteso. Per fortuna ho colto anche i saldi e mai fu scelta più giusta. Questo titolo riesce a combinare molto egregiamente progressione e difficoltà, tutto sommato scorrevoli e bilanciate. Non risulta mai noioso o sbilanciato, graficamente un piacere per gli occhi e la durata tutto sommato è ok anche se, a malincuore, non è per niente un gioco impeccabile anzi tutt'altro. Spesso paragonato e assimilato ad Hollow Knight, dopo averlo giocato, direi che il paragone è un pò esagerato. Per quanto sia ad oggi tra i miei titoli preferiti, è ben distante dal picco toccato dal Team Cherry perché soffre di una componente narrativa molto scadente e trascurata, pochi dialoghi o scene di trama o approfondimenti ben chiari. L'ho percepito molto disordinato e dispersivo, anche i comandi hanno problematiche. Soffrono di un basso frame d'input. In pratica se usi più comandi contemporaneamente il gioco salta dei tasti perché bisogna rispettare un timing di frame specifico che rovina un pò la fluidità meccanica. Anche la difficoltà, durante la fine, se si completa tutto al 100% come ho provato a fare io, arrivi lì che spammi e vinci anche concentrandoti poco nello schivare o memorizzare i pattern. Percui in conclusione si è rivelato per me un grandissimo gioco, ho sentito un qualcosa di speciale nei suoi riguardi, ma personalmente se devo distanziare l'amore provato per sto gioco è ben lontano dai colossi principali giocati in passato. Lo consiglio comunque al 100% anche a prezzo pieno, li vale tutti!

Ver solid. Has a few scuffed bits but generally a pretty good addition to the genre. Some particularly scuffed bits I'm willing to forgive because they are in service of really creative ideas for some bosses. However, the developers did commit one sin. If you kill 100% of these secret creatures, basically a collectable hunt, you get a secret ending. But if you don't, you get no ending at all. You kill the final boss and the game just ends. No cut scene, narrative, nothing. They just give up. Finding information on this game is difficult if you're looking for anything specific, so I wouldn't recommend trying to 100% this. Just play it for the core, enjoy it for what it is, and be satisfied when it's over.

第四十五款通关的类银，老实说属于有点类空洞了，不管法力槽还是分支支线，碎片剧情等等都有浓浓既视感，下劈反弹高度高于空洞，喜欢下劈的玩家可以做到很多skip，地图设计也和空洞类似，不说优秀也属于比较好的那批，存档点和传送点都还比较合理，后期查漏补缺有道具显示，探索体验较好，且美术音乐都不错，比haiku更像空洞代餐，比较推荐。另外，全成就难度中上，强制需要四周目。互斥成就较多，但因为全成就必多周目所以后面也能补。  然后一些个人玩的不太爽的点：最大问题是脚滑！面对一格宽的平台一不小心就会掉下去；然后部分关键能力放在装备里（如闪避无敌），但装备格只有三个有点少；回血道具和空洞共用法力槽不一样，而是渎神那种用了以后除非回存档点否则不能补充；虽然有探索道具但是不提示非隐藏，建议后续加一下更方便玩家查漏补缺。  大伙一定要善用标记！不然补探索巨折磨！

One of a handful of well done metroidvanias that ended up getting underappreciated. This happened mainly because the game used to be poorly balanced. However, patches have since fixed the game's balancing issues. Also, some in the metroidvania have falsely branded this an igavania. It is not, that is a complete lie. This is Hollow Knight, but shorter and without any artificial difficulty such as cornifer, corpse runs, and other souls BS. If you're fedup of Hollow Knight clones but you still love a good handdrawn artstyle and an open world with plenty to explore and secrets within secrets than this is a great metroidvania to play.  the biggest issues I witnessed were NPCs failing to properly handle situations where you complete a quest before the quest was given to you. This happened many times and left me with gaps regarding the in-game lore as a result. The other issue is that the jump just kind of sucks, especially in the beginning of the game. This issue gets better with time but its definitely going to be a bit of a learning curve at the start.  I recommend this for all metroidvania fans.

De las mejores ofertas que he conseguido nunca, muy contento con este juego la verdad, me ha sorprendido muy gratamente. Todavía no he tenido ningún bug.

[h1] Voidwrought makes you feel like you're playing a HK 0.00001 alpha prototype at 2x speed[/h1] Which translates as: a terrible and cheap Hollow Knight clone that feels awful to play in almost every possible way. It's an offense to see this game being published as a finished product.  Animations are very very raw or nonexistent. Exploration feels heavy af, it's like they couldn't properly tune gravity and your character weights a ton being pulled at lightspeed when falling. And because animations are SO ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ bad, reaching areas through vertical hits is the worst feature you can possibly imagine. Combat is also absolutely trash. Button mashing against plain dumb enemies causing null damage through annoying overly repeatable 2 frame mc attacks...  It's just utterly bad. I guess the only point where Voidwrought shines is graphics-wise, presenting a cool illustrated world. Unfortunately, that's all about it. Any price on this piece of garbage will be a steal.

The game gives you a quest in the beginning: go here, kill, come back.  Then you're on your own.  ... and that's how a metroidvania/soulsbourne game should be.  Sure, there are quests, but the game wont hold your hand (aside from purple lamps for a save room, and statues in front of a boss room). 95% of the time, finding a secret, new ability, collectable, is solely on you.  There are 4 different maps markers, but they're infinite (which is good; you'll need them), and PLENTY of good collectables and abilities scattered around.   Anyone familiar with the type of game will be happy about the amount of special attacks ("Relics") and combat buffs ("Souls") you can find. Play around.  Sometimes John Voidman is A LITTLE TOO floaty, making some jumps a bit annoying, but that's probably the only bad thing I can think of.  Art: 10/10 Gameplay: 8/10 Story: 5/10 (I wasn't really invested, sorry) Music: 9/10  8/10 game for $8 on sale. Can't get much better.

Voidwrought is a dark and evocative Metroidvania that draws its strength from atmosphere, movement, and its stark, oppressive sense of worldbuilding. Developed by Powersnake and published by Kwalee, the game opens with a haunting premise: a cosmic event known as the Red Star awakens a Simulacrum—an artificial being created to carry out a grim task. As this construct, you traverse desolate ruins, ancient laboratories, and decayed biomes in search of Ichor, the lifeblood of long-slumbering gods. This blend of cosmic horror and decaying high fantasy sets the tone immediately. The environments feel oppressive yet beautiful, bathed in heavy shadows, twisted organic shapes, and eerie lighting that accentuate the game’s brooding tone. Voidwrought’s hand-drawn visuals do an impressive amount of narrative work without relying on long text boxes or explicit exposition.  The moment you take control, it becomes clear that movement is one of Voidwrought’s strongest features. Jumping, dashing, wall-climbing, and aerial transitions feel exceptionally smooth, giving the game a distinct kinetic rhythm. Combat is grounded in close-range melee exchanges that reward timing and spatial awareness. Early on, your kit is deliberately sparse, which gives the game’s opening hours a sense of vulnerability. As you progress, new movement-based abilities open previously inaccessible paths, lending a satisfying sense of discovery as you loop back through old areas and uncover secrets with a renewed sense of power. The design emphasizes precision: enemies hit hard, hazards punish missteps, and mastering the controls becomes essential for navigating later zones that combine platforming and combat pressures seamlessly.  The structure of exploration reinforces the feeling of entering a hostile, ancient world whose secrets are not meant to be easily revealed. Each biome—ranging from rusted mechanical caverns to corrupted biological pits—feels distinct, both visually and mechanically. Hidden alcoves, concealed walls, and branching tunnels encourage deliberate exploration rather than brute-forcing your way forward. Voidwrought often expects the player to read the environment carefully, watching for subtle visual cues rather than relying on map markers or overly explicit guidance. While this design philosophy rewards attentive players and creates the thrill of personal discovery, it can also become a point of friction. Without clear direction, some sections risk feeling obscure, particularly when progression hinges on spotting deceptively hidden passages.  Combat escalates as the world grows more unforgiving. Enemies exhibit varied patterns, and bosses are striking in both appearance and attack design. The game’s grotesque creature concepts make each encounter feel like a clash against something ancient and unknowable rather than a mere obstacle. When the combat system comes together—precise dodge timings, planned aggression, and confident use of aerial movement—it feels exhilarating. However, Voidwrought’s approach to combat progression leans more toward refinement than expansion. Most upgrades reinforce what you already possess—stronger stats, enhanced mobility, better survivability—rather than dramatically changing playstyle. This can lead to a sense that the combat toolkit plateaus before the game itself concludes, which may leave players wishing for more variety in weapons or transformative abilities.  Despite its limited weapon variety, Voidwrought maintains a strong sense of progression through world expansion and ability-based growth. The Shrine system, where you channel Ichor to awaken upgrades and advance your capabilities, acts as a narrative and mechanical anchor. Each upgrade feels like peeling back another layer of the Simulacrum’s potential, even if the upgrades are more practical than flashy. The story itself unfolds in subtle ways through environmental clues, cryptic encounters, and the steadily escalating presence of cosmic forces. Rather than offering a heavily scripted narrative, the game opts for mood, implication, and sensory storytelling—allowing players to piece together the broader mythos at their own pace.  In terms of scope, Voidwrought delivers a focused and tightly crafted adventure rather than a sprawling epic. Most players will complete the main journey within ten hours, with additional time required for discovering secrets and optional areas. While the length is modest compared to some genre giants, the concentrated design ensures that the experience rarely drags. Performance is stable, the animations are fluid, and the art direction elevates every moment of traversal and combat. Where the game may fall short for some players is in sheer content breadth: it doesn’t aim to overwhelm with dozens of areas, weapons, or builds, and those seeking a massive, long-term Metroidvania may find the adventure more compact than expected.  Voidwrought ultimately succeeds by delivering a cohesive and visually striking blend of cosmic horror and precision platforming. Its world is memorable, its movement satisfying, and its atmosphere thick with dread and mystery. While it stops short of redefining the genre or offering extensive combat customization, it excels in presenting a refined, atmospheric, and engaging journey through a world teetering on the edge of divine collapse. Players who value meticulous world design, challenging traversal, and the eerie pleasure of uncovering ancient secrets will find much to appreciate in Voidwrought’s focused and haunting vision.  Rating: 7/10

I expected at some point over the 5 hours I've played to reach a point where I found myself enjoying the game, but at this point I expect that is never going to arrive.  I do want to point out that the graphics look great, and the soundtrack is fantastic.  But that's where my enjoyment just kind of ends.  I found the combat to be absolutely dreadful.  There's not really any impact when you're hitting enemies so you'll often have to back up or jump over enemies to continue your attack without taking damage.  Boss fights are pretty simple, although they are the more enjoyable combat experience overall.  Level design is mostly fine, with little secrets tossed around here and there to make exploration a little rewarding.  But traversing through everything feels like such an insane chore.  Especially when you die and have to return to participate in more mind-numbing combat that makes you wish you were doing something else.  And so, I toss my hands up in defeat.  I really wanted to like this, but it just isn't happening.  If the combat was a bit more engrossing I might want to keep playing, but it simply isn't.  And that holds everything back.
