Voidwrought é excelente. Sim, é muitíssimo inspirado em Hollow Knight. Sim, Hollow Knight é melhor. Mas Voidwrought é excelente e até acho que faz uma ou outra coisa mais interessante do que HK.  A exploração em Voidwrought é fantástica. Até agora foi o metroidvania que mais me deu a sensação de estar perdido, de forma positiva. Quando eu desbloqueava uma habilidade nova, eu nunca sabia que ponto que eu havia marcado levaria a um caminho novo, uma área nova, e que ponto levaria a apenas um item, alma ou fragmento de vida.   Diversas vezes ocorreu de eu ter "certeza" que um local teria apenas um item escondido. Contudo, ao explorá-lo, eu descobria que o ponto levava a uma nova área onde eu obteria a próxima habilidade.  Também mais de uma vez aconteceu de eu ter a sensação de estar explorando determinado caminho num momento errado, persistir no caminho e acabar progredindo para novas áreas e desbloqueando alguma habilidade. Até agora não tenho certeza se o caminho era ou não era inevitável.  Ainda sobre a exploração, o jogo conta com uma habilidade de movimentação original e extremamente interessante, além das muitas habilidades tradicionais em metroidvanias. Não vou detalhar que habilidade original é essa, mas dizer apenas que ela abre possibilidades de caminho no mapa totalmente surpreendentes.   Essa habilidade em questão foi uma das últimas que eu desbloqueei. Porém encontrei um relato de um youtuber que encontrou essa como uma das primeiras. Fico empolgado em pensar como isso aponta para possibilidades de experiências de jogo totalmente diferentes!  O jogo também apresenta uma arte maravilhosa. Todos os designs me agradaram, tanto das diferentes áreas e seus temas, que têm muita identidade, quanto dos personagens, inimigos e chefes. A trilha sonora também é ótima e contribui para a ambientação.  Finalmente, o combate é sólido. Talvez um pouco básico, principalmente nos chefes. É mais fácil do que eu esperava, mas também não é chato. Por um lado, as relíquias trazem alguma variedade, por outro, ricochetear na cabeça dos inimigos é talvez fácil e seguro demais. O mesmo pode ser dito dos momentos de plataforma: são apenas básicos, pouco desafiadores.  Em suma, Voidwrought é excelente pelo mundo interessante e maravilhoso de explorar. Só não espere um combate espetacular ou desafios de plataforma.

You can tell the devs of this game REALLY liked Hollow Knight. That being said, so did I, and when it's done well I don't think there's anything inherently wrong with wearing your inspiration on your sleeve.   It's not the best or most polished of this flavor of game, but the art and music are great, the world is fun to explore and there's decent variety for the length.

En soit, Voidwrought remplit le contrat de base du metroidvania : une carte assez grande et qui donne la part belle au backtracking, des powerups basiques (double saut, grappin, dash, attaque chargée, etc.). Les graphismes et la musique sont vraiment excellents : la DA est très bonne, les décors sont travaillés, et la musique est variée et globalement excellente.  Seulement voilà. Même si, comme on dit, « jeu à grappin, pipo met 20 », il faut reconnaître que nous avons là un jeu... fade.  On explore, on tue des monstres, on perk up, on avance, et... c'est tout. Voidwrought est l'exemple parfait du « bien mais pas top ». Le jeu est correct, mais pas du tout marquant. Il se termine rapidement, heureusement, mais ne laisse aucune trace derrière lui.  Ce sentiment est dû à plusieurs choses : comme je le disais au début, le jeu remplit le contrat de base du metroidvania, et c'est un contrat tout ce qu'il y a de plus épuré. Rien ne se démarque des autres titres que vous pourrez trouver ailleurs. Que ce soit le scénario, les abilités, le lore, tout est très « metroidvania 1.0 ».  Les combats et les mouvements d'une manière générale sont très rigides. Même si le jeu est beau, il manque clairement un travail de fond au niveau des animations et de la QoL. Le simple fait d'avoir les thèmes musicaux des différents biomes qui coupent abruptement au changement d'écran coupe la dynamique de jeu pendant une partie.  En soit, le jeu vous occupera une petite dizaine d'heures. En promo, pourquoi pas, mais il y a beaucoup de titres qui valent plus le coup dans le genre.

I like this game a lot. I like it so much that I bought it twice - for Switch 2 and for PC. On Switch 2 I have over 25h and almost 100%. Even though I like this game, I still believe it has awful design choices for some of the bosses (after patching game because at first it was considered stress-free power fantasy) - some are too easy and some are way too hard/chaotic/visually cluttered. Few examples: damage upgrade (first vault) is almost obligatory for Eternal Engine, Pustulant Prince has way too much hp and all that with awful lot of frantic movements and The Font is perhaps the most awfuly designed boss I witnessed in all my gaming experiance (obnoxious visual clutter). But even with all those flaws it is still exceptional metroidvania with amazing atmosphere, great production quality, fantastic sound design, well designed map, dark&weird lore and beautiful art style. To be honest, I had much more fun with Voidwrought than with Hollow Knight or Silksong (Voidwrought has SO MUCH better designed save and fast travel points, there is nothing wrong with convenience in this area). So, I give Voidwrought solid 8.5/10. Awesome metroidvania.

虽然我是当作空洞骑士的代餐游玩的，但这游戏设计得确实还挺不错的，有自己的东西，也挺好玩的。地图设计的很不错，小怪种类多，boss战前一定有存档点，打完boss一定有东西，虚空的空间穿梭也很有趣，扒墙角和穿墙做得很好，但爬墙就一坨了（） 以及道具设计得很好，伤害也不错，但是魂灵可装载的位置太少了。 然后缺点，手感很怪（主要是跳跃小平台以及爬墙），以及敌人血量是真的厚，我打boss的时候伤害有一半来自道具。最主要的是冲刺，冲刺冷却非常非常长，打天空之子的时候给我恶心坏了，你冷却时间砍半我说实话都不影响游戏平衡。 如果你作为一个想玩类似空洞的游戏的玩家，购入这款游戏作为代餐是没问题的，内容也挺丰富，不亏，也还算有趣。然后就是提醒一下想要买来试试的人，这个游戏的回血比较困难，回血使用的盐晶不能在战斗中补给，用一个少一个（直到找到存档重新补给），所以探图和打这些血牛boss就很艰难，增强治疗效果的魂灵更是身体的一部分不可卸下。 二编：我收回对这个游戏一切的喜欢，尼玛的失落花园敲墙敲了一整天有没有懂的，然后这个b提琴手还是真结局必须收集品，我收集你吗 三编：主脑长得真tm帅啊，和解了

12時間で真ENDまで到達しました 素材はとてもいいホロウナイトライクなメトロイドヴァニアで、一年前に出ているのにかなりシルクソングっぽい作りでもあります が、あのレベルではないどころの敵の固さと導線の酷さと全体マップの見にくさ 攻撃の出がいくら早くても笑えるくらい雑魚もボスも固くて序盤がとても大変です 時間停止や炎の壁など強力なレリックも中盤以降で手に入り戦闘の難易度は下がりますが、攻撃力最大にしてもなお固い ボス戦ではライフ減少で攻撃力の上がるソウルを付ける事を推奨します かなり探索の自由度が高い反面、自分が何をしているのか終盤までほぼわからずただひたすらに遺跡を彷徨う羽目に マップの壁線が色がフロアとほぼ差がない上に細く、空白があるのか凄く見づらいので最後までずっと困りました 難解なフレーバーテキストも合わさって、ここまで足元に地がつかないメトロイドヴァニアもそうそうありませんでした またレリックにもリキャストがあり、豊富なレリックの中で実践投入できるものはごくわずか 探索の難易度の高さは好き嫌い分かれるでしょうが、かなり楽しめました 周回要素もあるのですが、ワープゾーンに逐一入る無駄な演出時間など細かい部分でプレイの意欲を削ぐ要素が散見されるので、100％にする気力も2周目やる気力も湧きませんでした 罠を下攻撃で弾くなど楽しい要素もあるので、製作者の無駄なこだわりがプレイ体験を損なう惜しい内容です 全体的な難易度はまずまずなので、メトロイドヴァニアに飢えている人は触ってみてはいかがでしょうか　初心者にはまずオススメできません…

Despite the fact that during the first few hours I was swearing and seriously considering refunding it, I actually ended up liking the game. However, I’m still leaving a negative review, because I can’t recommend it — neither to newcomers to the metroidvania genre nor to most other players in general.  As many people have already pointed out, this game tries to be Hollow Knight without understanding what exactly makes Hollow Knight a great metroidvania.  The bad:  - Painfully long animations for some basic actions (fast travel, decoding, unlocking doors). It’s unclear why this mechanic even exists — it just wastes time and breaks the pacing. - Clunky controls. The character feels stiff and unresponsive instead of smooth and fluid. - Uneven difficulty curve. Early to mid-game feels frustratingly underpowered, while by the end you suddenly become overpowered and the challenge disappears.  The good:  - Great variety of relics and passive abilities. You can really customize your build to fit your playstyle, and that part feels rewarding.  In short: a game with potential and some good ideas, but too many frustrating design decisions to recommend it.

This game is like hollow knight without understanding hollow knights systems. -The sub weapons are clunky and slow to use for seemingly no reason. -The Game World design is kind of bad. While I think it's cool to allow you to explore many places at once, there's way too much obvious backtracking that needs to be done. -The map doesn't tell you about specific locations that locks off pathways (which is a prevalent issue in a lot of metroidvanias tbh) -The Fast Travel system is confusing enough for me to not understand it 2 hours into the game, even though you get the item for it seemingly from the first boss.  This game would be good if it had more polish. The art is great, but everything else just kinda makes it mediocre.

Nach etwa einer Stunde Spielzeit und zwei besiegten Bossen muss ich leider sagen: Voidwrought hat mich enttäuscht. Die Steuerung wirkt schwammig und wenig taktil – besonders das Springen fühlt sich unpräzise an, was in einem Metroidvania dieser Art einfach frustrierend ist. Vielleicht bin ich durch Hollow Knight und Silksong verwöhnt (beide zu 100 % abgeschlossen), aber das Movement in Voidwrought wirkt auf mich schlichtweg unausgereift.  Die Hintergrundmusik? Subjektiv nervig. Zwar lässt sie sich deaktivieren, aber man kauft sie quasi mit – also muss man damit leben oder verzichten. Für mich war das keine Bereicherung, sondern eher ein Störfaktor.  Insgesamt bin ich froh, dass Steam einen kulanten Kundensupport bietet. Für alle, die Spaß an Hollow Knight und Silksong hatten: Greift lieber zu etwas anderem. Voidwrought bleibt für mich ein blasser Schatten seiner offensichtlichen Inspirationsquellen.

I really wanted to like this game. It’s clear to me that the devs wanted to make something special. This is particularly evident by the game’s art style, which is simple and evocative. That is, unfortunately, the only thing this game has going for it. Moreover, compared to other games like Blasphemous, the art isn’t all the much to look at mostly because there just aren’t that many unique assets (either in enemies/NPCs or the backgrounds). What this games feels like is an attempt to make a game like Hollow Knight, without actually polishing the core combat and exploration mechanics to achieve this. Not every game has to match Hollow Knight’s shocking level of excellence to be fun. Ghost Song, for example, is a pretty meh looking game in terms of its art design and world feel, but I enjoyed the combat and customization enough to play through. Blasphemous has kind of clunky feeling combat, but is backed up by such evocative art and clear combat telegraphs that it is extremely fun. This game basically has good enough combat, exploration, and design to be in early access, but as a released game it just isn’t there. My honest advice is to not buy this one.  [b] Combat [/b]: The basic combat mechanics of this game are just off. This is, more than any other factor, what stops this game from being good. Your character’s basic attack is way too weak or basic enemies are simply too tanky. Even some of the enemies in the starting area take fully 6 or 7 hits to kill. This would be fine if they were meant to be “mini challenges” or bosses, but they’re just regular old enemies. On top of this, some of the early challenge arenas/fights are against mobs of enemies so fights feel protracted and simple. They’re often hard not because of the actual mechanics, but because I would lose focus after the second or third wave of 6 enemies that each take anywhere from 5 to 10 hits to kill.   This is combined with fundamentally sluggish and off movement. The character just doesn’t handle in a fun way either in combat or exploration (see below). You have very few defensive options and the ones that are there feel sluggish. On top this, your character’s area of attack is pretty short, so you end up going to hit basic enemies three or times and then jumping away again. Rinse and repeat.   This is exacerbated by poorly telegraphed hitboxes. Sometimes enemies spawn right on top of you with no notice. On the second major boss, the Eternal Engine for example, it isn’t clear to the player can slide under the enemy as it charges you because this tool is basically only used for exploration before this. In fact, basically none of the upgrades you use to unlock new areas can be used in normal combat. They’re keys, not abilities. So the complexity of the combat is solely on the enemies side, not the player’s. Getting new upgrades that actually make the game feel and flow better in combat and exploration takes a shockingly long time. Most of this game feels like the first two hours of Hollow Knight when you don’t have a dash or a wall climb etc. This game was clearly heavily inspired by Hollow Knight, especially clear in the pogo mechanic. As such, it’s a mystery why a ledge holding technique (an unlock-able btw) was not added to beef up combat AND exploration. The ledge ability is only for exploration, it is effectively useless in combat.   [b]Exploration[/b]: All the rooms look and feel the same, even between different major regions. This means that the game feels simultaneously really small AND I was getting lost constantly. It’s too late now to fundamentally shift placement of rooms, but more different assets need to be added so it actually feels like you’re exploring a world and not playing Escape the Backrooms.   Added to this is that the game leans too heavily on the idea of locking areas off behind future upgrades, this includes currency and upgrade materials in areas you’ve already been through. Moreover, so many ledges or paths look like you almost can go through them, but then your jusssst short of being able to reach them. When it feels like half of the paths are locked off like this, it becomes frustrating wading through an unending tide of samey rooms to realize that you still don’t have the right macguffin to access those items.   While this in theory encourages backtracking and deep exploration, if all the rooms look the same and it isn’t clear what you need to get those items then it just becomes a frustration. Every single time I went through an area I would see tons of stuff I just couldn’t get, which ironically discourages exploration because it doesn’t feel rewarding either visually or for materials.   [b]For the devs[/b]: There is obviously no real way to fully change the problems I have listed below at this point. Increasing the time between the telegraph of an enemy spawn and its actual spawn is a must. Right now, some of those arena mob fights are both too hard and boring, which is the worst combination for any game mechanic. If it was easy, at least you can just brute force your way through, but spending even 15 minutes learning a fight in this game feels awful. However, just increasing the reach of the basic player’s attacks and either increase basic attack damage or decrease enemy health. My suspicion is that this design was done to pad out what is a game that has very few unique enemy types and areas.

Look, i really wanted to like this game, im almost 100% achievements, but theres just too much wrong with it.  First off, the combat. Certain bosses feel great, but the enemy challenge rooms and some enemies in general just dont really feel fair. Its like they took hollow knight enemies but removed the parts which made them better.  The bosses arent all great either. One of them is essentially just hole in the wall, but at least theyre unique and each have their own charm. One of the later game ones in the hidden area with Cerebrum has a move which if you get unlucky enough is genuinely undodgeable.    Moving onto collectables and exploration, there are extra than what you need, which would be fine if the achievement didnt require finding every single secret. In the end it just feels underwhelming at best when you find yet another divine remnant or ancient artifact.  NPC quests are fine but hard to understand what to actually do sometimes, and the story is more difficult to understand  The art, music, and so much about the game is fun, but the hours of required exploration that you have to do, especially the devotees mechanic, just make the game so tedious and awful to play after and even during the first run. If you dont plan to 100% the game, then it is a fine game, otherwise i can't recommend it

[h1] Bom Jogo ! [/h1]  [hr][/hr]  [h2] Prós: [/h2]  -Jogabilidade responsiva e seções de plataforma muito boas , as seções de plataforma são incriveis , eu realmente gostaria de ter algo similar ao palacio branco de hollow knight , uma área focada em plataforma de tão bom que é se mover pelo mapa , ainda mais com todas as habilidades de movimentação -Estilo de narrativa não intrusiva , a historia desse jogo é contada através de "diários" e falas breves com NPCs , e por mais que por boa parte do jogo , não tenha prestado muita atenção na historia , os poucos detalhes que percebi observando detalhe no mapa , foram muito gratificantes de se notar -Direção de arte , artes de personagens , inimigos e cenário incríveis , uma boa variedade de biocas , cada um trazendo algo de diferente , mas ainda mantendo um ar sóbrio e melancólico condizente com o tema e historia do jogo , as animações são bem feitas , não se limitando as animaç~eos de personagens como também de efeitos e cenário , no geral , muito bom de se observar -Exploração , vc pode acessar no menu , um "guia" que lista a sua porcentagem de conclusão por área , assim como segredos ainda não revelados e partes da historia ainda não lida , tudo isso sem dar grandes spoilers , e o backtracking do jogo não é tão maçante como em outros jogos , isso , aliado com a vasta variedade de movimentação , faz da exploração desse jogo , ser muito boa  [hr][/hr]  [h2] Contras: [/h2]  -Habilidades e relíquias , a maneira com que a progressão de habilidades é estruturada , por vezes parece sem ordem , eu sei que o jogo é um metroidvania , mas a ordem que eu peguei as habilidades foi meio confusa , com habilidade que eu achava que seriam as primeira a pegar , sendo as ultimas , e habilidades que trivializam umas as outras , mas não vou entrar em spoilers . A cerca das almas/relíquias , elas completamente quebram o jogo , vc consegue montar uma Build no começo do jogo , que trivializa qualquer encontro de chefe , e substitui boa parte das habilidade de movimentação -Combate de chefes , simplesmente bata , não tem estrategia , se bater mais do que apanhar vc ganha , ou , se preferir , use uma build focada nas relíquias , e vc derrete qualquer chefe , mas é um pouco satisfatório só debulhar o chefe na porrada -Nenhuma penalidade ao morrer , apesar disso encorajar o jogador a tomar atitudes arriscadas , o fato de não ter uma punição ao ser morto , faz com que não tenha peso de morrer e perder recursos , somplesmente voltando como se nada tivesse acontecido

Siendo un seguidor de Hollow Knight, buscando alguna historia y juego que pueda llenar ese vacio esperando a Silk Song (20-08-25), este juego llega para intentarlo. No lo logra al 100%, pero tengo que reconocer que es un excelente juego. Se ha vuelto de mis favoritos, ya que para darle fin bien y tener la partida al 91% he disfrutado de 16 horas muy muy muy buenas. Una parte de mi siente que al haber jugado HK, me hizo avanzar mucho más rápido al estar acostumbrado al tipo de juego, mapa y secretos ocultos en las áreas.  Recomiendo altamente este juego.   Cosas que se me quedaron como muy buenas: -El mapa y sus áreas están bien hechas -La música -Los retos sorpresa -La dificultad de los jefes y ver que se hicieron con cariño.  -La dificultad está hecha para disfrutar -La multitud de objetos, diarios, etc -Creo en mi opinión que no me fui por el camino principalmente pensado por los programadores, y no fui castigado por ello, más bien, se sintió el reto... pero era opcional para el jugador  Áreas de oportunidad: -Creo que debieron haber hecho más teletransportadores o algo que al tener un avance muy alto, pueda hacerte llegar rápido a uno sin tener que pasar por todo el mapa corriendo, etc. -A veces se siente un poco raro cuando se agarra de las esquinas. En algunos movimientos se cancela y toma la opcion de tomar la esquina en peleas, etc. -Hay que poner cosas que apoyen más el encontrar objetos o coleccionables, además de agregar pines al mapa. No digo que estén mal los 5 que dan, pero por la variedad de cosas seria bueno tener más.  ?, Comerciante, etc... o que en el mapa se indique una vez de descubierto. -No sé por qué ningún juego hace lo que hizo Mystical Ninja Starring Goemon en N64. Cuando no sabias que hacer y estabas bien perdido y no veías por donde... estaba este personaje que le pagabas y te decia para donde ir (PLAAAAASMA), el juego se puede sentir en unos momentos lento o irrintante al no saber qué hacer.  FELICITACIONES AL EQUIPO Me alegra haber pagado cada peso que di por este juego.  Estaré pendiente de más de sus juegos

The start felt a little clunky, the story was so cryptic it might as well have been nonexistent, and certain bosses felt particularly beefed up, but it was still an enjoyable game overall. However, if I have to fight the true final boss one more time, I will be needing professional help.  P.S. - Shout out to the wonderful people that created the map guides, I basically scoured the Lost Gardens for that 1 missing health upgrade around three times before the interactive map pulled me out of my misery.

I get why people say that the enemies are HP spongy, but the review sort of reek of "but my DM doesn't DM like Matt Mercer"  So far, my biggest complaint, is that "secondary progression" in the first 2 hours is basically 0, I've found one "item" and lots of currencies, otherwise, as HP tanky as the normal enemies can sometimes be, the bosses are fine, and as much as the hitboxes might not be perfect, they're really not that bad.  This game is one where I think if you just play it don't fight it, it works fine.  It's not going to make any GotY lists, but it doesn't need to.  EDIT:  Having beaten it, not real change in opinion, it evens out over time, the bosses are all varied, interesting, and fun, even if not perfectly polished  Biggest issue here is that it took all the worst things from Hollow Knight in terms of how to do exploration and finding.  Things are just needlessly hidden.  Doing a post game 100%/True Ending pass, it's tedious, and with the minimalistic map style means you really have to scrutinize to find all the stuff, but it didn't really cause any issues other than toward the 100% ending collections.  Thankfully, the compendium (silently, read it somewhere else) lists what's in each region so you have a completion percent.  Game's fine, just imperfect

But I still finished it so it's not a bad game, but there are better games out there.

One of those games that seems to have an interesting premise but even in normal (lowest difficulty) the gameplay consists of dying over and over since you have to i-dodge through enemy lingering hitboxes and get hit anyways most of the time.   Every special enemy or boss is a damage sponge even early in the game where the gameplay consists of the same 2 hitter spammed over and over and your "dodge." So you repeat the same two things ad nauseam. They shouldn't have made things that sturdy in my opinion until you have more moves to switch up the combat or make it more varied.   Also I really do not enjoy games where you have to hold the jump button to get max jump height and EVERY ledge requires max jump height, a short jump is never beneficial--even during combat--so why have it? Its one of those relics from the past that makes things obtuse NOT more engaging.  A billion collectibles including these white spheres that drop from enemies and bosses and tracked on your screen. Can I use them to upgrade something, or get skills, or equipment of some sort? Who knows? The game doesn't explain it and there is no experience gain or levels in this game.   Even if you can't outright use the currency there should be an explanation of its relevance. I started to just skip enemies after a little bit, after all, you're risking taking damage before a boss to obtain some random currency that seems to do nothing.  Don't know if the game gets better, not sure I'll revisit it either.

I had a good time with this game. It's a Metroidvania through and through, with pretty tight controls, a good variety of enemies, boss fights of varying difficulty and a fair amount of upgrades and weapons to find.  One of things that really stands out for me is the exploration. I just really enjoyed traversing the different biomes, gradually opening up the map and occasionally backtracking to access previously inaccessible areas.  The art style also made the exploration very enjoyable. The different biomes have a well-defined identity, and your character looks pretty cool. It's a colorful game, but darkness is utilized in some clever ways to conceal secrets and the like.   The music does a good job of enhancing the atmosphere in the different areas of the game, ranging from ambient, moody weirdness to explosive metal bangers, always appropiately suited to the current biome or boss fight.  Apparently I was fortunate enough to play this game after the release of a balance patch that aimed to make the bosses more difficult. For me, the difficulty was spot on, just hard enough that most bosses gave me a bit of trouble and a few gave me a good amount of trouble.   If there's a sore spot, it's the story. I just didn't really get it, to be honest. I liked the setting a lot, though - it's mysterious and strange, and there appears to be a lot of depth to the world, but since the narrative seemed a bit messy to me,  I gave up on following it about half-way through. Maybe I'll be able to get more into it during a second playthrough, something I'm seriously contemplating because I want to see what the New Game+ can offer.  All in all, this game surprised me and I'll wholeheartedly recommend it to anyone who likes Metroidvanias that poses a decent challenge without being too difficult.
Very decent metroidvania. Remarkable but not excellent. A bit on the easy side, though some busses gave me a run for my money

已经全成就  这游戏我觉得挺好玩的，类空洞不过很多设定，手感完全不一样的，有人说脚底抹油，开始确实，后来习惯就好了  100%真的很难找，要借助后期20个日记给的那个符文，地图有隐藏进地图会有提示，离隐藏点近了也会有心跳声  有两组互斥的成就，去攻略帖找存档，很快完成  初期可能引导比较弱，不知道干嘛，然后有很多道具可以skip，例如那个白色飞刀，可以横向无限下劈，还有一个虚空挂钩，很多能力之间也是可以实现相同横向、纵向位移的，那个迷雾团能力拿到，什么二段跳，爬墙什么的其实都不需要就能跑全图了，当然有了更好；我的建议就是照着攻略地图，优先拿全所有的技能，然后慢慢探索  硬核模式记得多备份存档，一周目100%了其他模式没有必要全收集，能力拿全，拿3个钥匙，6个紫色火台，就能通关拿成就了  几个NG+模式说下，因为初始全血全魔全符文全技能，其实前两个都不难，也是直奔主题，注意10个能力是没有的，要重新拿，之后就是3个要是6个紫色火台  第一个模式，伤害低了一点儿，敌人碰一下2血，总体不难，很快就推图过  第二个模式超级爽！法师模式，削弱平A，法术无限魔，真的超级爽！推荐大家2个技能，一个是固定火墙，打Boss必备，多练习一下因为会向前方一定距离放置火墙，这个用好了秒boss；还有一个是技能页面第一个，一个向前移动的火墙，威力超高，清屏神器，怪一片一片倒，如果一免火墙不够，那么就两面！火墙移动速度没有角色快，装上CD符文，可以实现几个火墙一起向前推进，神挡杀神！第二个模式真的超级超级超级爽！  第三个模式着重说下，感觉比硬核还硬核，怪碰一下就2血，boss技能碰一下4血，没有任何回血！死了减1血上限，加攻击，这个很绝望，血越来越少，比硬核还要小心！！！加的攻击非常不明显！！！我一路都是提心吊胆过来的 可能很多人会问，一直死一直死，会发生什么，我找遍互联网都找不到答案！！！只有自己实践！！！那么我来告诉你，最后会卡在5血不再减少了！为何是5？小怪3下，Boss2下，不会怀挡的放心！！！就算5血状态，攻击提升真的不明显！！！   OK，总体非常喜欢游戏过程，我觉得不输给空洞，祝大卖，希望之后会有DLC，增加类似神居一类的，海库机器人后来也是这样的

초반에는 진행이 조금 힘들 수도 있지만,가면갈수록 늘어나는 플레이어의 여러가지 스킬이랑 그에 걸맞는 능력을 사용하여 클리어 해야하는 보스들이 등장하여 재밌어짐   내가 성장형 캐릭터를 키우는 뽕에 하고 싶다 하면 강력추천함  할인할때 사기는 했으나,정가로 판매했어도 샀을 것 같은 좋은 게임이였음  게임만들어 주셔서 감사합니다!   The beginning might feel a bit tough, but as you progress, the growing number of player skills and the bosses that require smart use of those abilities make the game more and more fun.  If you enjoy that satisfying feeling of nurturing a growth-focused character, I highly recommend this game.  I picked it up during a sale, but honestly, it’s so good I wouldn’t have regretted buying it at full price. Thank you so much for making such a great game!

It's one of these games that seriously needs time to breath and evolve. I almost ditched the game because the first few hours are a serious pain in the ass: You lack all the abilities which make this an awesome game, you can only jump and hit, you cannot reach most areas and you cannot mark different areas with sufficiently different map icons, there's just a few variations and there's A LOT to explore. It seems frustrating...  BUT once you get some nasty relics and souls and abilities and eventually even grasp something about the world and story. The dark void atmosphere will engulf you and once it does you won't be able to resist :) The bosses are really fun, but not really hard. There's only normal mode and hardcore more (you die once, it's game over), maybe add a more difficult "normal" mode at some point.  Anyway, it's a really fun game you certainly don't want to miss if you like Hollow Knight and Aeterna Noctis. It certainly has all the ingredients needed: fun, surprising and does not overstay it's welcome. I will remember this one and go for 100%

Malheureusement le jeu manque un peu de difficulté, aucun ennemi ne représentant vraiment une menace et contrairement à ce que j'ai lu dans des avis ce ne sont pas vraiment des sacs à pv surtout si on prend le soin d'explorer et d'améliorer sont attaque. Les boss sont eux aussi assez faciles et peuvent être pour la plupart être face tankés en bourrant X. La seule réelle difficulté vient du fait que les salles sont globalement très confinées avec peu d'espace pour manoeuvrer (mais bon c'est cohérent avec l'ambiance du jeu).  Univers riche et cryptique, bestiaire et pnj très variés avec pour chacun des entrées de journal ce qui est un plus si on apprécie ça. 8/10

I'd say a 3/5 is fair. It's very solid BUT, there is awkwardness. I had to fight against grabbing onto ledges many times. sometimes I'd want to pop up and attack something from a ledge grab but it would just grab right away again, sometimes I would want to dash over the edge of ledge, only for it to grab the ledge and my dash launch me in the other direction.   Getting out of water without the double jump feels awful. Using the slither underwater feels like you're moving through molasses and god help you if you need to move UP in water while slithered. You move slower than a snail.  Some of the bosses were frustrating, especially the first one felt like a slap in the face. Later bosses were annoying (Like the one you have to run from before you can even fight it. I only had slither and slide at the time so that run was a pain) or fine. Once I remembered that the assassin soul existed (When I was going against the final boss and final final boss) it was a cakewalk and I'm sure the rest of the bosses would have been if I knew it was there.  Overall, while it has some jank, it's a very solid game with some great artwork.

Very combat heavy metriodvania, but the combat feels unpolished compared to Hollow Knight, which it seems to try to emulate.   Enemy hitboxes extend outside of the actual sprites as far as their contact damage, but the main weapon is super short range and seems to struggle to make contact even when the weapon overlaps the core of the enemies.  The character movement feels fairly stiff and the camera moves to match the character in a jerky way that instill motion sickness when you plummet after reaching the peak of your jumps.

Voidwrought es increíble. Lo disfruté muchísimo en las casi 13 horas que le invertí buscando y explorando cada recoveco y rincón de un mundo extenso, misterioso, deprimente y hermoso.   La narrativa e historia que presenta el juego es ominosa, misteriosa y a veces difícil de comprender por la forma escueta y oscura en la que nos la presentan. El juego tiene matices que exploran una narrativa clásica del horror cósmico por lo tanto, resultar insondable y confuso es atinado, y que estos aspectos estén presentes a lo largo de la experiencia me hizo disfrutarlo aún más.   Los controles son ajustados, con plataformeo preciso y la navegación es suave. Con la adquisición de mejoras o habilidades de desplazamiento, la exploración se vuelve rápida y satisfactoria sin mencionar la gran cantidad de secretos, paredes destruibles, atajos, senderos ocultos, etc. todo lo que juegos de este género acostumbra. Voidwrought lo hace de forma excelente.   La dificultad en algunos momentos puede ir decayendo a medida que el progreso del Simulacrum avanza, contando con mayores herramientas y estadísticas a su disposición. De igual, la adición del New Game + puede solucionar este aspecto, dotando a la aventura de un par de escalones de dificultad adicional con tres modos distintos.   El apartado sonoro de este juego es soberbio. Los sonidos ambientales, gemidos e impactos sobre enemigos, susurros, la melodía de ciertos flautistas (que son una evidente referencia a los Flautistas amorfos de Azathoth), puertas, mecanismos todo estos efectos son sobresalientes. La música, por otro lado es maravillosa, desde ominosa hasta explosiva y metalera en los combates contra jefes u hordas de enemigos.   Mi única queja observación reside sobre la cantidad limitada de marcadores que se pueden colocar sobre el mapa, pues me resultaron escasos y en algunos casos me vi obligado a apuntar referencias. 😅   El equipo que desarrolló este juego, hasta donde entiendo, es de solo 3 personas y todas ellas tienen mis aplausos.   Voidwrought es una recomendación sólida.

先说结论：佳作。 战斗系统类似空洞骑士，有碰撞伤害，有上下劈，普攻击退自己，好消息：自带快劈；坏消息：敌人3刀一个变5刀一个。更好的消息：开局4格蓝并且3刀一格蓝没变，法术伤害很高。开局没好用的法术打小怪有点虐腱鞘，这游戏的核心设计就是法术，以至于有一整页法术任君选择。 手感就不如空洞骑士了，普攻没有爆汁差点意思但是世界观不同不能强求跳跃有惯性这个也需要适应。 地图设计开放，想去哪就去哪。（前提是你会用下劈skip）我提前三个图拿到二段跳开启速通真的很惊喜。 接下来说些雷点。有个跑图能力居然需要在基地购买挖掘才能拿到，有点反常理，不过比光蝇灯笼便宜多了这游戏有黑灯区但没有灯笼给玩家买也是有点搞。好在黑灯区没有任何地刺。 boss设计较简单，看几遍就能无伤，还没有战斗房堆怪难。普通结局流程10小时左右。 注意事项：早点打神庙右侧地图失落的探险队拿地图传送，以及挖掘有一项是从复活点传送回神庙的能力，这些对流程顺滑度大有帮助且无需任何能力就能拿。 开局建议避战先去失落的探险队利用劈怪伪二段跳skip拿到前期一下一个怪的强力法术，保护腱鞘从我做起 很棒的空洞代餐，手感还是氛围上都很空洞

★★★☆☆ 3.5/5  A very solid Metroidvania overall. The gameplay mechanics are well-designed, and the boss fights are on the easier side—which I actually appreciate from time to time. Sometimes it’s nice to enjoy the flow without constantly hitting a wall.  The only real downside, in my opinion, is the story and lore. Even if you try to immerse yourself, the game doesn’t do a great job of giving you a clear or compelling understanding of the world. It feels a bit vague and underdeveloped.  Still, if you’re here mainly for the gameplay, this one delivers.

Baştan söyleyeyim oyun oldukça ortalama sayılabilecek bir oyun ve bunun başlıca sebebi bossların yeterli derecede tatmin edici olmaması. Bosslar hem çeşitlilik açısından yavan hem de zorluk açısından yeterli değiller. Öte yandan karakter geliştirme kısmı klasik ve yeterli, iki tane relic kuşanabilirsiniz ve bu relic'ler özetle void yükü (diğer deyişle mana) harcayarak kullanabileceğiniz özel yetenekler sağlıyor. Oyunda yeterli sayıda ve farklılıkta relic bulunuyor, bu relic'leri düzenli ve doğru şekilde kullanırsanız oyununuz fazlasıyla kolaylaşıyor. Bir de soul'lar var bunlar da kuşanıp bir takım pasif geliştirmeler elde ettiğiniz itemler. Oyunun lore'u ve sanat tasarımı bence ilgi çekici değildi. Tam olarak kötü bir oyun denemez ama combat çok tatmin edici değil, oynayacak oyun bulamıyorsanız ve metroidvania açlığınız varsa denemenizi tavsiye ederim yoksa diğer alternatiflere göz atmanız daha mantıklı olur.

1.游戏美术不错。很多动态细节也很棒。但是，游玩体验一言难尽。如果打分我给美术的高分。 2.游戏最大问题，重复跑图比例太多。 a.很多小boss需要反复背板，跑路太浪费时间。 b.地图被各种关键道具卡关，前期没这个道具只能看，打个标记路过，但关键道具拿到之后，玩家还要大老远过去重复跑图。 看似合理利用美术资源(地图)，实际是策划没系统性思维的证明，这并不能证明道具藏的多妙，谜体多有设计感，只是没有逻辑的拼接，在我看来，只单纯为了为难玩家的要素，这是最下等的水平。 c.各种恶心设计就不提了，我印象最深两个影响体验的点：黑灯地图，玩家变毛球爬墙的奇怪操作感，兼职浪费时间。
总体来说还是好玩的，下劈的力度比空洞骑士还大，难度不高，手感也不错，但是基本也只有战斗是可以拿出来夸的，剧情过于谜语人和套路化导致完全不想看也不想懂，很多引导也不怎么友好，最要命的还是有很多要跑的图又臭又长，传送的加载动画也是，叠加在一起完全磨灭了全收集和二周目的欲望。顺带一提，在很长一段时间都以为主角是一位小胡子独眼男而不是面具侠
The combat is very tight and exploration feels very rewarding. overall, if you like the genre, you should totally try this game out

Voidwrought es un metroidvania oscuro y atmosférico que encantará a los fans de Hollow Knight. Comparte con él no solo un estilo visual sombrío y detallado, sino también una estructura de exploración no lineal que te invita a perderte —literalmente. La sensación de soledad, el diseño de niveles intrincado y la constante sensación de estar descubriendo secretos enterrados lo convierten en una experiencia absorbente.  Uno de sus aspectos más peculiares (y polémicos para algunos) es que puedes llegar casi hasta el final del juego sin haber encontrado todas las habilidades esenciales. Esto habla tanto de su diseño abierto como de la facilidad con la que puedes desviarte sin darte cuenta.  La dificultad está en la línea de los juegos tipo Souls: exigente, con combates desafiantes y jefes que pondrán a prueba tu paciencia y precisión.  ¿Lo recomendaría? Sin duda. Si te gusta explorar, enfrentarte a enemigos duros y te enamoraste de Hollow Knight, Voidwrought te atrapará. Solo prepárate para perderte… en todos los sentidos.

A solid metroidvania with a few weaknesses: -I didn't like how the game would often lock you into a combat arena, especially when said combat arenas led to acquiring new abilities. I think it would have been better if all abilities were connected to some boss -the biggest surprise of the game was when it was over. I didn't know that triggering a door would lead to a boss, and even then, I was surprised that it had just one phase. Maybe I didn't explore thoroughly enough (I netted some 60% completion and was able to lay waste to the final boss without much trouble).   Theming and story fell flat for me. I didn't care/remember any of the NPCs beyond their utility. Mechanics are solid. The one thing that did annoy me was that you need to wait a few frames after attacking if you want to dodge in the opposite direction. It didn't feel snappy, just something I had to get used to.  Final score: SOLID

Pros: Great hand-drawn art and nice music blend well into the Eldritch atmosphere. Great map and ability design. Some may consider it a weakness, but I appreciate the lack of guidance and non-linear nature of this game.  Cons: Difficulties/ growth need some balancing. Early mobs are incredibly tanky. Overall, not the perfect game, but it's still great, and I would recommend it to anyone who loves both Lovecraft and Metroidvania.

一款极其复古的类银(或者说类空洞)，仅推荐很喜欢空洞骑士且能接受复古游戏的玩家入手。 游戏的主要优点如下： 1、优秀的纯手绘美工，画面精细、动作流畅 2、不错的地图设计，房间设计水平在线，串联也很有水准，探索要素很多 3、精妙的能力交互，无论是探索还是战斗，都能充分运用获得的能力 4、丰富的敌人种类，每个地图地图都有至少五种新敌人，几乎没有换皮 游戏的主要问题如下： 1、跑图极其浪费时间，传送能力前期解锁不了且传送点极少，存档点不承担传送功能，哪怕从存档点回到神庙这个功能都要费劲儿解锁。加之类银游戏有各种软锁，你每次获得新能力可能都要去之前的地方再跑一遍，跑三百六十五里路非常消磨探索热情。 2、引导可以说是几乎没有，如果这类游戏的经验不丰富，可能经常会有我是谁我在哪儿我该干什么的疑惑。 3、敌人非常耐草，开局就能遇上要砍四五刀的杂兵和能把你刀砍卷刃的小精英，后面有一张图你哪怕把攻击拉满了，还是一只小杂兵就七八刀，小精英更是十几刀，不少boss也是相当之肉。更要命的是，游戏所有敌人都不显示血条，很多boss也没有阶段划分，所以不少boss战中根本无从判断自己打了多少血。敌人又肉，自己又对敌人的血量状况没数，非常破坏战斗体验。 4、主角的性能设计可以说是一坨稀，制作人做出这个运动惯性以后肯定觉着自个儿可牛逼了，你看我这物理引擎吸附逻辑做的多吊，晚上躺床上得轻哼仨钟头睡不着觉，但实际游玩体验就是你感觉自己在溜冰。还有主角的各种强化机制，到后期主角都十几滴血了，你回复还是3点，还得在原地发呆特别久。那内位要说了这不有强化回复量和缩短回复时间的饰品么？那可不是么，主角的各种机制都给你放到饰品上了，这不就和你玩个二游把角色机制都放命座里头一样。饰品还只能带仨，所有饰品里头能增伤的只有一个血量越低伤害越高，就这个主角的成长强化机制真的是没什么价值。 总的来说，这个游戏虽然有着不少优点，但你想体验这些优点又很费劲儿。例如地图设计的不错，但跑图能烦死你。这游戏和“酣畅淋漓的吃史”体验正相反，好像你在陕西想吃一碗正宗美味的羊肉泡馍。就你说这羊肉泡馍再怎么好吃，但掰馍这一块儿是真要了命。

Whenever someone talks to me about how great Hollow Knight is it feels like they're talking about this game and not HK.  To me this is basically a slightly less well executed HK (not by much but there's a few points where I can say HK has this beat like the bosses and lore)  with none of the insane padding HK has, which is to say it has 20 hours of rewarding content to explore, not 20 hours of content with 30 hours of padding.  This game does the choice of keeping you very much immersed in a world that is and stays mostly alien to you, things happen and you kinda lack context and consequences, it works better in horror styled games where the "not knowing" helps build tension, but here you are the cosmic being interacting with other cosmic beings that are just kind of doing their things whether those things interfere with what you're doing or not so things kinda happen and that's that.  On the gameplay side everything is pretty well refined, doesn't feel floaty, the movement options are pretty good, none of the challenges you face are that intense and you won't be completely blocked by anything specific though one of the boss might be a bit more demanding than what you're used to up to the point you're figthing it, exploration QoL is okay but could've been a bit better (per area collectible list, equippable that tells you if you've got something left to find in a room)  There's a few extra modes, hardcore is cheeseable and the other (spoiler: quit to menu) are okay if you're looking for an excuse to replay the game  Other than that yeah, great metroidvania, had a great time playing through it, would recommend to anyone that enjoys the genre or disliked HK for being too long / sparse in interesting things.

Heavily "inspired" by Hollow Knight, but clearly worse. For a metroidvania its a good game with great artwork and nice challenges. However the progression system and abilities you unlock is broken imo. You can easily miss the unlocking of the fast travel. Once you get the slither ability you don't need the double jump or get the grapple hook. This opens the world a lot and you can manage to go mostly anywhere, until you can't and have to backtrack. Also the first part of the game is much harder than the rest and without upgrading your weapon the enemies are too tanky. I attempted the fetid boss without upgrades and after 200 hits the thing was still alive.  Recommended for fans of the genre or if it is on sale

Bad game, map and story design. Enemies are bullet sponges, sometimes unfairly placed. Fighting them does not feel fun or rewarding. You do not get properly directed through the map via accessibility by discovered items, and when you discover another area, it does not feel like progress since all enemies share the same flawed movement/attack patterns and hit points. There is no progress to the players character either since all additional abilities just do not feel rewarding, and they also come randomly. The story wants to be super mysterious, religious-spiritual-ominous, but falls flat because what the story writers wanted to achieve, was beyond their skill. So you will get dozens of lines of pseudo cryptic text, but they do not justify the flatness of the story as far as I have played. It fails to click/catch the player from the beginning. I do not care if there is a big story reveal and improved movement after 6+ hours if the way to get there is not fun. And it is not.

It's a nice metroidvania with eldritch horror flavoring.  WIth that in mind, the intro feels a bit lacking, it takes a while till you get a proper feel for the game. Part of that, to me at least, was that most of the mobility skills were available only later on, which, as others have said, leads into a lot of backtracking which can be annoying.  I like the music in some areas, the monsters. The narrative... well... you look into the void and the void looks back. It has this weird disjointed feel that, I think, matches the theme of eldritch horrors greatly. If you expect a sane coherent narrative.... it does not match what the game is going for. I feel like once you pass maybe the first 5 to 6 hrs (at least for a slow player), you get to start enjoying it more. You get more skills, more items, more methods of attack, you get a lot more upgrades.... the first few hours... eh, they could be better but maybe it's just that an insane world would not allow you to understand it from the get go.  Overall, I have liked. Is it perfect... nope. Does it scratch that itch for anyone waiting for Silksong? Yes, within reason.

Bueno, termine la primera pasada del juego (o sea, desbloque el NG+ y vencí a varios jefes, pero aun hay misiones y objetos que me faltan conseguir, ademas de hacer la NG+).  Hasta donde llevo, es un metroidvania que tiene jefes desafiantes y entretenidos, una exploración aceptable y una duración bastante decente si queres sacarle todo el juego (aparte que viene con varios logros); la banda sonora esta buena (capaz lo único molesto es que al pasar a otra sección de la misma zona, la música se reinicia en vez de mantenerse, a pesar de ser la misma zona)  Ahora, los problemas que yo note: la progresión del juego la note torpe, debido a que no sabia donde debía ir a explorar, ademas que el orden de las evoluciones del personaje resulto un poco random (conseguí antes el doble salto y el gancho en vez del desliz por las paredes), Los poderes y las mejoras se sienten bastante flojas (por ejemplo, hay un total de 5 invocaciones en el juego, y solo 1 sirve para hacer daño de manera consistente; aparte que otra sirve de soporte vital pero tira curaciones cada muerte de obispo y en un lugar a veces apartado, y así con cada cosa); la movilidad no se siente fluida (o capaz es que yo soy algo manco, pero no se siente tan ágil como tendría que) y las builds se sienten bastante carentes dado a lo que dije antes sobre las mejoras y poderes. Aparte la colección de flautistas se siente bastante tediosa.  Al menos, para la "primer pasada" y sin saber nada del juego, diría que 50/50, porque fue mas disfrutable que frustrante, pero si pase varias horas sin saber a donde ir o que mejoras necesitaría para avanzar. No sabría si recomendarlo o a quien, ya que el apartado visual y la música me gustaron, pero los jefes capaz resulten algo pesados y la gameplay no enganche tanto.

I actually wanted to return the game. But that wasn't possible anymore. The game is okay, and I like supporting smaller developers. But I just don't enjoyed the game so much. But at 30-40% the game gets significantly better. The enemies are boring and have way too much health. Every other fight is three hits, keep your distance, and then hit again. Thanks to each enemy's high HP, you repeat this until they die. The combat chambers only become "difficult" because of the masses of enemies packed into the smallest space. The hitboxes often feel strange, and the platforming is a thing of its own. Often, the protagonist glides or slides as if on ice, causing you to fall off the small platforms and have to do everything again. And the most annoying thing was the healing animation, which simply takes forever and, in some fights, doesn't fit into the combat pattern at all. Because there's virtually no time to heal, or you just have to be lucky that the boss decides not to spam attacks again and again in this one run.  I love Metroidvania games and hope the developer sticks with it and the next game is better.

I wanted to like this game. I really did. It unfortunately suffers from several design flaws that really ruin the experience. That said I did finish it which I usually wont do if I dont find something redeemable in a game. There is a good game in here somewhere, its just marred by all the questionable design decisions.   Let me start with what this game does right, the redeemable part that did lead to me to actually finishing it despite its flaws. That will be short then I can move on to all the frustrations and poor design decisions.   What it gets right is simply presentation. This game has a great art direction and an intriguing narrative. The writing is good and right from the start it introduces an interesting premise steeped in a lot of mystery. Your motivation to keep going is simply trying to figure out what is really going on. Basically the opening hook of the game worked. after finishing it I'm not sure how well it paid off, but I did not go for "true ending" so I won't say it doesnt.   Now for what it gets wrong and all the design choices that ruin the overall experience. First let me say I think any one of these in isolation, maybe even a couple of them collectively might be excusable, but ALL of them together interweave with each other to produce a sum total trifecta of pure frustration and tedium.   Controls: The player character has no evasion/defensive options for way too long in the game and even the ones you eventually get are not great and/or require too many unlocks. Your best defensive option is the "pogo" head bounce. Your jump feels very limited and you fall as fast as a 2 ton weight caught in jupiters gravity. You also have some very weird momentum problems that can make re-aiming the head bounce very difficult and has the character skidding off platforms. Even fully kitted out I never really felt like a master of the environment like full progression in these games typically produces. You can zip through a lot of things with the full kit dont get me wrong, but it still feels clunky even as you fly through the stages. Many of the movement ability unlocks end up being redundant to each other as well.   Level design: levels are very claustrophobic and often give you little room to move around, especially in the early game with limited movement options. For a character that has no other defensive options this is a major problem. Darkness as a mechanic is over used. The slithering through narrow passages is over used and slow. Elevators and other things are slow. Lots of blind jumps with no option for looking around and there is almost always something placed where you will land to take damage from that is usually pretty unavoidable until you just know that from trial and error. During fights the level design is often just an unfair death trap before you are well kitted out in movement options and during traversal it is just a time waster of sitting around waiting for slow moving platforms to re-position correctly or for enemies to do so.   Enemy design and placement: Enemies are very tanky early on and offer little in the way of a fair and skillful challenge. They are basically just environmental obstacles that are always placed in the worst spots that make some platforming nearly impossible to do without just tanking the damage or require waiting around forever for them to position so you can carry on with exploring. Tons of slow moving flying enemies that always hover out of jump range and very slowly move back into range after you land one strike that knocks them away. Also plenty of ground enemies that are tall enough to exceed your jump range and eliminate the one defensive option you do have in the pogo bounce. fighting is more a test of patience than skill.   If the levels werent so confined you could maybe move around to compensate for the lack of defensive options and clunky controls. If the controls were better the lack of defensive options might matter less and allow you to better navigate the confined spaces. If the enemies werent so tanky you might be able to take them out more quickly to free up that space you need to move about and deal with the poor enemy placement better. If you had better defensive options the clunky controls and poor enemy placement might matter less and introduce more skill to combat. This is why none of these things alone make for such a bad experience. Its really does take all of them collectively playing off of the others and amplifying the problems of each to really completely ruin the experience the way they do. Unfortunately they do all just end up amplifying and feeding off of each other, resulting in nothing but tedium, frustration, and pain for way too much of the game time.    Overall I would say this is a Low C tier game at best and maybe a D tier game. It did give me enough to finish it even though I considered rage quitting several times. That is the only reason its not a clear D tier for me. I honestly believe the game could be a solid B tier game (maybe even a low A tier) if just some of the problems didnt exist and didnt feed into to the others so perfectly to create that trifecta of pain, frustration, and tedium that this game regularly produces when all of its flaws are working together like they so frequently do throughout its entire runtime.

Well, I spent 20 hours in the game and here's what I can say  1. Art style.  The game looks absolutely amazing. The world is huge and diverse. There are many different looking locations here that have a narrative design. Sometimes you can just stare at the backgrounds.  2. Metroidvania. The world is huge and full of secret places. You can wander for a long time, and it is really interesting to explore. World is really interconnected. Though it's annoying that sometimes the passages are blocked by enemies. At the same time, enemies respawn every time you enter a room. This slows down backtracking a lot.  And there are also many places that require some ability to get to. But once you get there, you'll find that there's a passage that requires another ability. And you've already walked half the map to come here and ask yourself "why the heck did I come here?"  3. Regular enemies Dull. Usually they just walk back and forth, or simply throw projectiles while standing still. There are very few enemies with more than one action.   4. Bosses Not bad. But not very good either. Most of them could be beated by 3-5 attempts. Many are preceded by unskippable cutscenes that are just annoying. Couldn't beat the boss on the first try? Watch the cutscene. Couldn't on the second? Again. You are punished by watching this annoying cutscene. Again and again. In addition, bosses often have a completely non-obvious collider, and it is impossible to understand which part of the boss deals damage, which can be hit, and which is just for visuals. Some bosses have very short and non-obvious attack openings. Also, bosses like to fill the entire screen with flashes, particles and huge glowing projectiles, so that you stop seeing the boss, your character, and anything else.  5. Items Active items are variations of projectiles and AoE. Passive items more interesting and similar to Hollow Knight charms. And also healing items. Maybe it's my fault, but I spent the first 15 hours with only one of them. Only after that I collected three more at once.  6. Currency There are two types of currencies and a bunch of consumables in the game. And this is a problem. Every time you find a new secret, you hope to find something interesting, but there lies another useless artifact fragment or a bunch of currency. Seriously, by the 20th hour I already had 20,000 currency and about 30 artifact fragments, and that's not counting a whole bunch of other junk.  7. Fast travel The WORST part of the game.  Firstly, fast travel requires unlocking a skill that is hidden behind an optional boss. That is, you can just walk past. Because this is a metroidvania, you can go through it in different ways. And the first 3-4 hours of the game can pass without fast travel, and you will have to walk. And the world is HUGE. Secondly, there are not so many fasttravel points, and they are quite far from all key points and NPCs. And the world is huge. Be prepared to walk A LOT. And the cherry on the cake - fasttravel comes with an unskippable cutscene! Every time you need to go somewhere - watch the cutscene. Went the wrong way? Watch the cutscene! Over and over and over and over. I think out of the 20 hours I spent in the game, at least 2 hours were just watching this annoying cutscene.  8. NPC The characters are quite colorful. It is interesting to learn their stories. The problem is that the stories usually seem underdeveloped, and the characters themselves give a lot of uninformative dialogues that are simply boring to read.  9. Side quests Usually, "bring me that", "bring me 6 pieces of that", and I'll give you a useless artifact fragment or currency, which you already have a lot of.  10. Map The map is not very useful. The usual for games of this genre map of multi-colored squares, on which landmarks are not displayed in any way. It is annoying that shortcuts are not always displayed. For example, elevators are indicated by lines, but their horizontal counterpart (the void) is not indicated in any way. The player must remember that this door does not lead to an unexplored room, but is a shortcut. And the world is huge :)  11. Hub location The hub location at the beginning of the game is a completely useless and boring place. And if I hadn't accidentally entered it while passing by, I would never have known that after a certain point there is a huge amount of activity there.  Seriously, there is even a fasttravel point there! But IT NEEDS TO BE UNLOCKED FOR MONEY! Why? Why is there no direct road to such an important place?  Why couldn't just make the fast travel point accessible from the start? Especially since the fast travel system is still unavailable until you get the ability? For what purpose?  12. Resume You may have noticed how often I write the word "Annoying" in this review, and this is how the game feels. The game isn't bad, it's exactly annoying.  I don't want to watch that cutscene again, I don't want to read that block of text again, I don't want to kill that monster blocking the way again, I don't want to trudge across the entire map to check the passage again.  I just want to explore this huge, beautiful world.  The game had a huge potential due to an interesting setting and absolutely awesome art style. But a lot of poor gamedesign decisions spoils everything very much.  If you like eldritch games or you like this art style and characters - you can probably play it. The world is fascinating to explore, but the content of the world is not very pleasing.

クリアしたので追記。評価は変わらず。画面暗くして移動速度低下状態で迷路やらされるみたいなのが頻発してテンポが悪く、探索がつまらないというか苦痛なのが残念ポイント。全般的に画面暗くなるマップが多いのは遊びにくいだけだった。  この状況で全部のマップ探索しつくすというのは苦行だと思う。実績の達成率がかなり低いのも頷けた。  全体的にスキルの入手順がおかしかった気はする。二段ジャンプなしだとまず勝てないボスに二段ジャンプない状態でいけるとか、そういう細々としたところの調整が微妙なのも残念ポイント。  ボス戦はまぁこんなものかなというバランスなんだけれど、とにかく道中が敵が硬いとかギミックの待ちが長いとか暗闇迷路とかでテンポが悪くて、終始爽快感が欲しいなぁという感想で終わってしまった。さすがにこの状態だと落穂拾いはしたくない。  ---  ちょっとやったけれど自分にはあわなかったのでレビュー。やたら硬い敵を相手にヒットアンドアウェイするタイプのメトロイドヴァニア。難易度が高いというよりは、敵が硬くてダレて雑なプレイをして被弾してやる気なくしてまた被弾そして死亡、みたいな悪循環が頻発するゲーム。序盤に爽快感がないのが致命的で、一般受けはしないかなぁというのが率直な感想。辛抱強く頑張れる人にのみおすすめという感じ。  死にやすいポイントとセーブポイントの配置がちぐはぐなこともあって、全体的に遊びにくさが勝ってしまっている。敵が密集していてごり押される感じのストレスも多め。とはいえちゃんとメトロイドヴァニアしている感はある。一気にやれるタイプのゲームではなく、少しやってダレたらやめるくらいの距離感で付き合うのがいいかな。集中力を求められる玄人向けゲームという感じ。ちょっとずつやろうとは思うけれど、他にいいゲームあったらそっち優先かなぁ。  マップデザインとバランス調整のミスが大幅減点ポイントなのでおすすめはできないけれど、ちゃんとメトロイドヴァニアしているのでおしいゲーム。

As someone who has played countless platformers over the years, I can confidently say that Voidwrought has effortlessly secured a spot in my top 3. What truly sets it apart from the sea of other platform games is its unique movement mechanic: the character accelerates as they move, giving the gameplay a smooth, dynamic flow that feels both fresh and satisfying. It adds a subtle layer of momentum-based challenge that veteran platformer fans will appreciate.  Visually, Voidwrought is a treat. The color palette is rich and atmospheric, perfectly complementing the eerie, otherworldly environments. The graphic design is polished and cohesive, with every detail — from glowing backgrounds to environmental effects — contributing to the game's immersive tone.  And let’s not forget the adorable insect creatures scattered throughout the world. They're full of personality and bring a charming contrast to the darker themes of the game. Whether you're jumping across platforms or simply exploring, their presence adds a delightful touch of life to the alien world.  In short, Voidwrought is a standout platformer that blends engaging mechanics with gorgeous art direction and subtle charm. Highly recommended for fans of the genre looking for something both familiar and refreshingly original.

More frustrating than fun for me. Mainly did not like how the character controls in terms of input response (which in turn made combat frustrating.) I prefer more button mash/twitch style combat but seems it's not that kind of setup in this game; more souls-like deliberate button pressing is enforced. E.g. if you're mashing attack, don't expect to rely on being able to escape/dodge enemy attack whenever it comes up by dashing out, because the dash press won't register sometimes. Also, without an upgrade item (which takes up one of your limited upgrade slots) dash doesn't have i-frames. Meaning, by default there are no i-frames so good look dodging/avoiding getting hit in general... and since just touching the enemy is a hit... (no metroidvania should have this setup, because you will get cornered, and you won't be able to do anything about it but get hit; there's no shield, no counter ...no way out) but besides the controls/combat, was fun otherwise ...for the amount I played before I rage quit.

Fantastic, cosmic horror themed metroidvania which ticks just the right boxes: - Big, interconnected map - Lots of secrets - Meaningful exploration - Lots of collectibles - Good looking, easy to navigate ingame map - Good animation - Great music  It has only a few minor annoyances, but nothing major: I could use more different map markers, backtracking is a pain until you get your gate warp ability.  Highly recommended.

OST is really great an oppressive. Lot of comparisons to HK and I get that to an extent but I think it’s also reductive. This game has a style of its own and the environment is wholly unique.  Combat feels good and responsive. Exploration is snappy and there’s a lot of secrets.  It’s a solid metroidvania that didn’t overstay its welcome. Each boss was challenging enough and fun to fight.  My only complaint is that there’s not enough feedback for what your items are doing or where they’re going. I had a bunch of items that would disappear because I had given them to NPCs but I had no clue which ones I gave them or why. Just strange, but it’s minor!  Fun to near complete too! A great piece of art here.

this game is... like a 7/10  IMO the endgame movement and the tail end of the game is fun, and the combat is interesting, but this game is dragged down by some weird developer decisions that im not too big a fan of.  Pros:  Great visuals, the game looks great end game movement very fun, very fluid lore is solid from what i can tell tons of collectables Very open, many possible paths and routes   Cons: early game is a slog tons of collectables (that are very easy to miss) Menu's were clunky Game reuses a lot of puzzles Absurdly slow animations. seriously.  Now you might be asking, why recommend the game then? and that's simple, overall i enjoyed my time with this game. not quite enough to give it a second run for 100% all achievements, but i believe that this is a good game, just a little rough around the edges.  If you do buy this game, and you do try it, i STRONGLY suggest that you make use of markers on the map, this games map is very simple, and without the markers you will spend ages just checking locations.

Вот и была мной пройдена ещё одна замечательная метроидвания, и, наблюдая за скромным количеством отзывов о ней, я принял решение высказать и своё отношение к этой игре.  Собственно, стоит начать с того, что игровой процесс очень напоминает Полого, однако я хочу донести, что эта игра совершенно не является его копиркой. Да, перемещения персонажа в пространстве могут быть очень похожими на ХК, но главная задача разработчика - сделать так, чтобы это было играбельно и не копировало бы всё подчистую. Несмотря на богатый опыт игры в ХК, я с гордостью заявляю, что опыт игры в VW оставляет уникальные впечатления, и сама игра реально является самостоятельным продуктом со своими уникальными механиками, квестами и боссами. Особенно порадовал последний аспект, так как на данный момент непросто создать босса, который не был бы просто списком паттернов; здесь же каждый из них ощущается, как головоломка с элементами битвы, и это очень приятный подход к созданию боссов в рамках подобных игр: не просто затыкать, затанковать или зазубрить, но реально погрузиться в бой, вложить в него свою душу. Приятно было исследовать карту, несмотря на немалое количество бектрекинга, поскольку он не душный, щедрый и интересный (но советую с самого начала игры ставить метки). Сам игровой процесс проходил очень динамично, почти не было скучных моментов или случаев непонимания почти - окружение весьма отзывчиво, ни один шаг не будет лишним, поэтому можно смело заниматься почти всем, чего пожелаете. Лор здесь не пестрит особой уникальностью или глубиной, но он не лишний и весьма интересный, параллельное его изучение будет очень кстати. Дизайн у игры - просто огонь, всё очень гармонично, красиво, плавно и брутально (особенно всякие лорные персонажи с этой футуристичной и сюрреалистичной внешностью). Саундтрек тоже очень приятный и подходящий, западает в душу, но не глубоко :) Ну, а теперь перейдём к недостаткам: многие враги не ощущаются уникальными, а лишь грушами для битья и препятствиями, однако это не играет особой роли, поэтому можно закрыть глаза. Безусловно, одним из главных недостатков можно считать хронометраж; было бы просто шикарно, если бы игра была раза в полтора длиннее. Также почти все квесты достаточно скучноватые и выполняются без особого осознания, но не помешало бы увеличение их количества. Также печалит, что боссов в игре тоже немного, и хотя бы некоторое дополнительное количество стандартных и привычных в понимании боссов не помешало бы. Немного расстроило большое количество (как мне кажется) бесполезных артефактов, но их в игре предостаточно, и кто-то, наверное, сможет найти им применение.  В целом, в игре нет существенных недостатков, а её положительные стороны многократно перевешивают количество минусов, поэтому фундаментальной критики тут возникнуть не должно. В остальном же, отличный метроид, который дарит уникальный и приятный игровой опыт в соусе из замечательного визуала и звукового сопровождения. Я бы дал игре 9.5/10

Voidwrought is a very well-designed game on a mechanical level. The combat, exploration, and just general working your way through the world is really good with very few complaints. Don't get me wrong, I enjoyed my time with the game and I do recommend it, but just bear this in mind.

[b]Voidwrought[/b] is a visually striking, atmospheric action-adventure that, while intriguing on the surface, repeatedly stumbles over frustrating design choices and pacing issues that hold it back from greatness.  [b]Let’s start with travel.[/b] The fast travel system in this game is bafflingly slow — around six seconds per use — and considering the [i]constant backtracking[/i], this quickly becomes a noticeable drag on the experience. And backtrack you will, over and over, thanks to a map system that starts out promising by indicating your next objective but drops that feature entirely after the second boss. From there, it’s a guessing game where you mark countless spots on the map, hoping you'll remember which was which when you eventually get the required ability. Even when an NPC later marks a few locations for you, it feels more like a token gesture than meaningful guidance.  [b]Combat is another mixed bag.[/b] At the start, your damage output is pitiful. Enemies take [i]five or more hits to kill[/i], which is unacceptable for a first impression. While things improve as you find upgrades, combat hovers between "fine" and "annoying" — never quite reaching satisfying. The small melee range exacerbates this, especially when dealing with enemies that explode or are surrounded by poison gas, which forces the player to either trade hits or resort to spells. Boss fights suffer similarly; with how close you need to get to deal damage, some of their attacks feel nearly impossible to avoid, with little room for counterplay outside of sheer patience.  [b]Enemy variety is one of the game’s better points,[/b] and it’s appreciated because you’ll revisit areas constantly. Unfortunately, [b]aberration encounters (mini-boss fights)[/b] are disappointing. Most of them throw two weak, poorly designed enemies at you instead of one memorable encounter. Unsurprisingly, their attack patterns desynchronize and you’re left playing the waiting game.  [b]Mechanically, the game can feel like it’s actively wasting your time.[/b] Elevators crawl at a glacial pace. The slither ability — one of the traversal upgrades — is slow and awkward. Even mundane things like opening certain doors involve pointless QTEs where you mash the attack button. It’s all filler — tedious padding that feels like it should’ve been caught in QA.  [b]The vault puzzles are easily one of the worst mechanics here.[/b] Puzzles in non-puzzle games often feel shoehorned in, and this is no exception. Unfortunately, these are tied to damage upgrades, so skipping them isn’t an option. It’s a frustrating, irrelevant hurdle between you and the improvements that make the combat tolerable.  [b]To its credit, Voidwrought looks fantastic and sounds even better.[/b] The art direction and music are genuinely standout — if you value a dark, moody atmosphere and enjoy uncovering lore, there’s something worthwhile here. Movement, too, improves later on once you collect a decent set of upgrades, and it feels good when you’re actually progressing through new content.  [b]I finished the game in 7.5 hours — and I probably thoroughly enjoyed about 4 of those.[/b] I left behind an entire ability and plenty of collectibles, but I felt I’d seen enough. It’s hard to recommend Voidwrought without caveats. If you come for the exploration, art, and audio, you’ll probably find those worthwhile. But if you’re hoping for a combat-driven experience that feels consistently rewarding and tight, this game misses the mark. The combat is there, but it’s merely serviceable, and sometimes even a chore.  [b]Final Verdict:[/b] A beautifully atmospheric game marred by clunky, unbalanced combat, needless filler mechanics, and irritating pacing issues.

Voidwrought is very clearly wearing Hollow Knight all over its sleeve. Aggressively so. That said, it's not a half bad imitation. No, it never reaches the highs of HK, but if you're looking for an 8/10 knock off then this will do more than fine.   Voidwrought feels like a game of inconsistent design quality. On the one hand, you've got a near flawless execution of art design and art style. The game world and characters look fantastic in my opinion. This is sharply contrasted, however, by the absolutely atrocious UI at times. Some of the Menus and text blocks look like they had zero work to make them fit within the game/ world. The platforming is precise and feels great as you continue to get your various upgrades. This feels contrasted with a combat system that never really gets its footing. Perhaps it's that your character is too large in this world for contact damage to feel like it can be navigated and avoided? Or that combined with enemy design and attacks (especially bosses) feeling hard to read? Whatever it is, combat never fully clicks. I was able to craft a build that I had fun with as the game progressed, however it was more so in the way that i had made something that felt broken and allowed me to disregard damage to an extent.  The world you explore is very cool and has Scifi Egyptian vibes. The map is lame looking. its a small thing but one that bugs me in MV's. The enemy variety is quite good. The boss list is serviceable, nothing spectacular here aside from one or two standouts. The character and hub area progression is satisfying. The trophy list sucks. Some really annoying multiple play through stuff on extreme difficulties. No thank you.   My play through came in at almost 18 hours. I think this is somewhere between an 8 or 9/10. 9 feels high, but there are some really well done and polished elements to this game.If you are a fan of MV's and HK to the point where you're out here looking for alternatives, then I think you should play this game.

Pra mim, os maiores destaques desse jogo são: 1 – A música é sensacional (o que não é nenhuma surpresa, porque eu amo as músicas do Jouni). 2 – Alguns chefes/monstros/cenários têm uma arte linda.  Fora isso... você passa o jogo inteiro sem ter um dash que realmente desvie dos monstros. Alguns monstros não reagem direito ao hit, tive alguns bugs com o mapa e chefes que, do nada, ficavam parados. Então, num geral, Voidwrought não traz nada de inovador ou diferente do mesmo. Tem a sua parte boa, mas, sinceramente, é só um "ok". Avaliando com as músicas fodas que o jogo tem, é um 7,5/10; sem elas, um mero 5/10.

地图问题，地图引导做得稀烂，逆天隐藏更是找都找不到，存在恶性bug，不知道为什么就是找不到路打不开门，地图不能100%。 收集问题，一周目不能全收集就算了，收集了半天想打隐藏boss，没存档试了其他选项，结果给两个没用的选项导致一周目遗器不能全收集，神经啊，给两个没用的选项NPC就不见了，也没给东西，也不说去哪了，都懒得找了。 BOSS问题，第一个boss就四个阶段对新手不用好，还是带些机制的boss，血量也厚，玩家上来只有两个血瓶，没有基础也没有玩过这类游戏的玩家只能死磕，被打中了硬控，没有反应时间，强行延长游戏时间。 闪避问题，闪避CD特别长，为了延长游戏时长特地给游戏做了一些恶心改动，带了冲刺无敌的魂灵还是不能闪避一些boss的攻击，这破道具有什么用呢，制作组玩过自己的游戏吗？ 跳跃问题，主角跳跃高过了台阶都上不去，手感又飘又滑，特别容易掉下来，什么失足少年。 魂灵问题，为了不让主角性能过强，把一些魂灵的触发改成了概率的，触发概率低的感人，全游戏唯一增加伤害的魂灵是低血量增伤。

Voidwrought es un muy buen metroidvania que da al jugador lo que necesita, un buen mapa para explorar, combates emocionantes y desafiantes, puzles ligeros y saltos milimétricos donde demostrar su habilidad y nervios de acero. No presenta grandes innovaciones en el género pero lo que se propone lo logra con éxito.  Su apartado artístico está a un nivel altísimo en todos los niveles, desde sus preciosos dibujos realizados a mano hasta su impresionante banda sonora, pasando por la ambientación oscura llena de horrores lograda. Simplemente por verlo y escucharlo ya merece una oportunidad, pero si además es divertido, no sé a qué estás esperando. Un imprescindible para los amantes de este género.  Leer análisis completo en Retro Pixel Press: [url]https://www.retropixelpress.com/an%C3%A1lisis/an%C3%A1lisis-de-voidwrought[/url]

精美的手绘画风，游戏画面泪水之城和真菌荒地的既视感，熟悉的下劈等元素，又一款类丝之歌发售前的类空洞代餐。整体游玩下来的感受是一部中等偏上的类银河城，下面来简单说说优缺点，对于我这种黑魂9智战士的玩家，照例是不评价剧情部分。无脑砍怪就完啦，剧情什么的太废脑子了 优点部分： 1.画风精美，不同区域之间也做到了差异化的视觉表现，甚至两个隐藏区域呈现的独特画风还有些奇异伴随着惊喜给人眼前一亮的感受。 2. 不错的地图设计，前期设计了很多能力锁，需要在后续逐步解锁能力后才能到达新区域和拿到前期无法获得的一些道具，虚空钩爪（遗器）和蠕行（幻影，即人物能力）这两个设计还是给我带来了一些惊喜感。 3.游戏2套独特的系统遗器+魂灵通过不同的组合也可以搭建丰富的build，针对不同boss和小怪，选择不同的遗器和魂灵应对能产生事半功倍的效果，比如 打先驱“尖叫着乌特”用刺客（魂灵）+锯齿腺体（遗器）+虚空钩爪（遗器）的组合就能让战斗难度下降很多；打先驱“圣泉”用塔浑香炉（遗器）+凝聚之光（遗器）就能快速将BOSS打入二阶段等等  缺点部分： 1.前期数值设计的崩坏，你敢想象初期的一只最低级的小怪居然需要5下普攻才能击杀，稍微厉害一点的怪需要10刀，甚至10几刀才能击杀，而且普攻对小怪的攻击击退可以说是微乎其微，再加上一些恶意的怪物组合（能想象第一次碰到“光明使者+建造者”组合带来的恶心程度吗，那个光明使者就他妈跟个疯婆一样啊啊啊啊拿个法杖在那放火球，然后建造者在那边扔大石块，砸死你呀，然后我们主角就在那边，哎哟痛、太痛了，你告诉我怎么赢？） 2.动作手感的奇怪，无论是跳跃的手感，下劈的手感，人物移动的滑步，都非常怪，需要时间适应。然后地图又设计了很多那种单个的平台，很容易在跳跃的时候掉落  总体评价8/10，值得一玩。
