A concise and robust Metroidvania offering open exploration and Hollow Knight-inspired combat.  [h3]Likes[/h3] + Gorgeous art style and good soundtrack. + Similar combat feel to Hollow Knight with Pogo mechanics. + Movement is precise and smooth flowing. + Able to explore large portions of the map initially.  [h3]Dislikes[/h3] - Requires a lot of backtracking. - Easy to get lost and not know where the next objective is. - Certain lategame bosses require more visual clarity.
solid game, pretty good graphics, still early game but the bosses have been challenging enough to make them fun.

Voidwrought se destaca entre os Metroidvanias por sua jogabilidade estratégica, exigindo adaptação e planejamento na exploração e combate.  Com um sistema de Relíquias e Buffs, o jogo oferece liberdade para criar estratégias. Sua arte lembra Hollow Knight, mas possui identidade própria, misturando ruínas, vegetação, elementos cósmicos e um toque futurista místico.  A variedade de inimigos (74 no total) é um ponto forte, mas alguns chefes podem parecer fáceis em comparação a inimigos comuns devido à baixa vida. O mapa pode ser confuso no início, exigindo marcações constantes.  No geral, é um jogo com trama envolvente, jogabilidade satisfatória e um estilo artístico impecável, apesar das dificuldades iniciais com a navegação.

敵がノックバックしないのと、効果音が明らかに弾き飛ばしてる音がするボスがいたり、固いくせに二段ジャンプしないと絶対に攻撃が入らないボスがいて連続攻撃が難しくて無駄に戦闘が長引いたりします。特に神の泉戦は最後のほぼ足場が消える場面は、ノックバックが無い＋攻撃の頻度がやたら高い上に足場が狭すぎてダメージを食らうと後半一気に持っていかれます。こちら側に非情に不利なうえに避けきれません。確実に当たります レリックでちまちま攻撃してもHPが高すぎて全然削れません、ダメージごとに攻撃手段が変わっているっぽいのですが最終過程に入ってから全然HP削れてない気がします  アイテムの場所が分かりづらくて(探索は楽しいのですが)詳しい場所やヒントが無いので同じ場所を何周もしなければならずにストレスが溜まります。

Voidwrought is a beautiful game with crisp controls, absolutely amazing soundtrack and a decent amount of content. If you're a fan of metroidvanias, I don't think you'll regret buying this game, BUT I do have some criticism that genuinely threw me off and probably will you too.  -Base damage is too low or enemy health is too high. I personally would suggest reducing the impact of upgrades and increase the base damage, to make up for it. that wouldn't throw off new players as much.  -let the explorer Relic show missed items, not missed tiles. That you can do if your map would be more detailled, but it just becomes a chore, especially because people already saw most of the game once they get the relic. Also It doesn't seem to even work with some items.  -adding damage numbers would be great so players can find out how effective their abilities actually are, I never bothered to use anything except the empowered attacks and everything laser.  -Bossfights have an amazing atmosphere but what I was missing is the feel of a duel. Every boss just randomly throws projectiles that you need to dodge because you happen to be in a room with them. I don't feel like there is any gameplay interaction with them  (except the small green dude).  The NG+ Modes where a good idea, although I only really liked the Undone. that was actually tough :D  As a metroidvania, I'd give it a 7/10  EDIT: Add Boss Healthbars :c

Очень качественная метроидвания вдохновлённая hollow knight. Схожесть заметна в рисовке, в игровых механиках. Правда, тут не теряются ресурсы при смерти и нет механик для их возврата. Лора в стиле "понапишем бреда в записочках про богов, пустоту и т.д." тоже навалили. Очень приятно играется - управление чёткое, отзывчивое. Враги атакуют быстро, персонаж игрока тоже.  Карта огромная. Рекомендую отмечать торговцев - не все из них собраны в хабе и мне в конце игры, имея на руках тонны местных валют, было просто лень искать где там на карте был один из торговцев, которого я видел один раз. Урон прокачивается, но мобы в актуальных локациях требуют много ударов. Сами удары персонажа быстрые, что провоцирует спам по кнопке X. У меня уже в первый час начал уставать палец, так что пришлось через настройки стима сделать макрос-автоповтор, чтобы просто зажимать кнопку и персонаж долбил без остановки. Правда, потом пришлось ещё извращаться, чтобы можно было использовать заряженные удары, которые делаются на долгое (примерно вечность) зажатие клавиши атаки. По сути, нужны только для разблокирования неоторых проходов на карте и для пары мобов с неуязвимостью от обычных атак. Боссы приятные, с впечатляющей рисовкой, разнообразными атаками. Без явно отделённых фаз. Сложность боссов ровная, примерно на несколько попыток каждый. В итоге не понял зачем надо было весь этот пафосный бред в диалогах и записочках наваливать, если после финального босса ни мультика, ни даже текста не вставили, чтобы хоть дать понять чем всё закончилось. Тем более странно, что при входе в комнату босса короткий мультик есть. В общем, если скипать сюжет, игра очень достойная, играется приятно, рекомендую.

Underappreciated game.  Exploration is superb, difficulty is high but fair, bosses and enemies are fun and variable.  With actual great soundtrack with reference to old SEGA games, and lovecraftian overall vibes.  What more could a heart desire?

Voidwrought is a surprisingly good Metroidvania experience.  Despite the visual and gameplay similarities to Hollowknight, it manages to have its own identity separate from its inspiration.  My only real issue is that I didn't feel like I was receiving any definitive answers about the nature of the world, and that the significance of the final boss has yet to click with me. Nothing that detriments the gameplay and quality itself, just something that feels missing.  I'd give the game an 8/10 overall, not much is wrong with the game IMO besides what I described.  Definitely worth it on sale, decently worth it off sale, 100% worth it if you're a dedicated Metroidvania fan.
A solid 3.5/10 metroidvania.

I played thru the entire game, got the first ending and then the true ending. It is a solid metroidvania, great graphics and atmosphere, character movements are fast and fluid. Much like a true metroidvania, at the beginning you are very under-powered, I struggled with the 2nd boss of the game because I felt so under-powered. I couldnt figure how to do more damaged until I got an ancient cipher and solved one of those puzzle rooms. By the end of the game, I felt over-powered. But the final true boss is a challenge so you need all the power-ups, relics, etc. that you can get. Overall, I would very much recommend this game to anyone that likes metroidvanias like Hollow Knight (even though is has its own identity). Great work Devs, looking forward to your next game!

Voidwrought is a cosmic horror MV where you play the embodiment of an ancient civilization, navigating through a temple city overrun by a curse that destroyed most of its inhabitants. Unlock powers, recruit followers, and get closer to the source of the corruption. The game's story is patchwork mess, but the gameplay is a ton of fun. The many enemy types require unique approaches, the game has a large number of magical artifacts with unique attacks, as well as "spirits" (read: Hollow Knight-style "charms") to swap in an and out. There's a large number of things to find and collect, and there is a wide range of biomes   keeping things fresh. Bosses are unique and quite memorable. Traversal is smooth, and though the game isn't really platforming-focused, trying to 100% the map is a ton of fun.

For me, as (at best) a mediocre player, the game was way too difficult at the beginning, and I gave up several times. But I thought it was so beautiful that I kept going anyway, and once I got the first damage upgrade, the game really opened up for me. Now I think it's excellent!  A graphic style I really appreciate, tons to explore, fun and slightly different abilities, a good map, varied enemies. Also, it's very nice that your character moves quickly and smoothly. (I can't stand games where you move slowly.) Eager to see what the rest of the game has to offer!

Just finished the game. Well saying "finished" might not be the most accurate word here, since I am about 60% completion, but I defeated the final boss and literally got the achievement "Finished Voidwrought" so there's that. But I do intend to play it more and perhaps get 100% completion.  The game looks really nice. The game sucked me in with its atmosphere. The story seems a bit convulated, but unique and   there is plenty of lore and I did not uncover all of it yet. The bosses are not hard, I assume that's why developers released hard mode.  In summary, it is definitely worth it for every metroidvania fan and I enjoyed the game and do not regret buying it. It feels like there is enormous room for DLCs so hopefully the game ends up doing well. :)   Couple of things that stuck to me as SMALL negatives.  1. Although the game itself is really nice looking, I cannot say the same about the map. The map, pardon my crude language, is bunch of rectangles connected to each other, separated by color for different "biospheres". I am not sure if this is because the map is also visible all the time and needs to be performant, but I am pretty sure that the incredibly talented team behind this great game could have come up with more appealing and atmospheric map.   2. Voidwrought is a good game that could be great with a few changes. Unfortunately some of what needs changing is rather systemic, so I am hopeful that a Voidwrought 2 can exist and will be able to make them.  The damage balancing is...interesting. When you start the game, you need anywhere from 5-9 hits on just about every basic enemy in the game to kill them, which can be frustrating since they will attack you back and you have no recourse other than to step out of range and back in. The damage upgrades you do eventually get, however, are significant and feel like real steps up in power, which is great! But I wish the balance was such that you could still knock out the chaff early in the game relatively easily (2-3 hits).  You also can't duck in the game despite a fair number of enemies shooting projectiles at you or otherwise being low to the ground. It would help if you could duck since your defense options are pretty severely limited.  At some point you get access to a slottable ability that causes your dash to move through enemies. In my opinion, this is nearly mandatory to keep slotted for the entire game and drastically improves the feel of combat. You actually get options to be more aggressive, reading enemy attacks, and dashing through and away in a sort of dance with the enemy. This feels great, I just wish it didn't occupy a slot (and that it was available from the start of the game).  With all of that said, however, I played this game all the way through and picked up almost everything there was in the game (save for collectible items that there's no in-game way of finding without just exhaustively exploring every nook and cranny to find what you missed - I used The Explorer to find all the envoys and hidden areas, but am still missing a few items that I'm sure I just walked right past at some point). The game looks really good and movement/combat is generally satisfying, save for the issues mentioned earlier.  Again, I really hope this game does well enough for a Voidwrought 2, and that the developer tweaks a few design decisions to make a truly incredible game!

W SKRÓCIE Fabuła 3/10 Grywalność 3/10 Długość gry 3/10 Dźwięk 6/10 Grafika 8/10 Poziom trudności 2/5 (więcej = trudniej)  O ile Metroidvania to jeden z moich ulubionych gatunków gier, to niestety przy Voidwrought nie mogę powiedzieć, że się dobrze bawiłem.  Na plus: + ładna oprawa graficzna. Lokacje są zróżnicowane, a w każdej z nich spotkamy innych przeciwników + oprócz podstawowego ataku mamy do wyboru około 20 zdolności bojowych, z czego możemy mieć przygotowane 2 na raz, a ponadto możemy je w dowolnej chwili zmieniać z menu postaci + możliwość wybrania do 3 aktywnych dusz spośród około 20, z czego każda daje nam jakieś pasywne efekty  Na minus: - Quality of Life (a dokładnie jego brak) - gra nie szanuje naszego czasu. Trzeba się trochę nabiegać zanim odblokujemy możliwość szybkiej podróży. Gdy już ją mamy, to trzeba za każdym razem dotrzeć do punktu podróży (nie ma opcji powrotu z dowolnego punktu na mapie). Alternatywnie można dać się zabić, żeby szybciej odrodzić się w spawnie xD Kolejny mankament to osobny punkt podróży i osobny punkt odpoczynku/odrodzenia. Na domiar tego animacja szybkiej podróży jest niepotrzebnie wydłużona... - nie wiem, czy to niedoróbka, czy mechanika gry, ale nie możemy płynnie przechodzić z podstawowego ataku na zdolności specjalne. Trzeba na sekundę przerwać atak, by zdolność zadziałała. Da się to zrobić lepiej, czego przykładem są choćby gry "Ender Lillies" czy "Lost Epic" - ogólnie gra jest dosyć łatwa. Większość bossów przechodziłem za pierwszym razem, przy nielicznych tylko musiałem wykonać 3-4 podejścia i to przechodząc grę w stanie grypy - backtracking - o ile lubię ten nierozłączny element w Metroidvaniach, tak tutaj było to strasznie uciążliwe właśnie przez brak QOL - gra w żaden sposób nie informuje nas o postępach w eksploracji, przez co nie wiadomo w których regionach mamy jeszcze do zdobycia jakieś przedmioty/sekrety - fabuła - po przejściu ciężko mi powiedzieć, o czym właściwie jest ta gra. Nie za bardzo wiadomo kim (lub czym) jest główny bohater, co próbuje osiągnąć, kim są postacie, które spotykamy itp. - gra jest krótka jak na swoją cenę. Przeszedłem ją w 11 godzin (nie na 100% ze względu na powyższe uciążliwości), z czego jakieś 3-4 godziny to był bezsensowny backtracking i szukanie gdzie mam odblokowaną możliwość przejścia

Finally made it through the game in its entirety (one playthrough, that is). Well. Oof. [some spoilers about gameplay and story mechanics ahead]  Voidwrought is very clearly inspired by Hollow Knight, does mostly well in the graphic design and sound department (you can really notice the amount of work that went into those aspects), and somewhat manages to be a decent metroidvania in its collectathon phase. While I'm not a huge fan of creating "somewhat but not really" replayability with branching yet illusory choices, it's not exactly an unusual mechanic, and can be quite fun to explore when you're planning on replaying a game anyways.  But its movement, a central part to the gameplay feel, leaves [i]a lot[/i] of room for improvement: most abilities are much more sluggish than simply walking, and several of them feel redundant and/or overlapping in their use cases - which is supposed to be part of the nonlinear design, I get it, but the way it's done just feels like your controls get more and more bloated with useless moves. (As a side note, contraptions also take way too long to operate. Remember the time in HK when you needed to wait half a minute for an elevator to arrive, and then spend another 30s on the ride? Right, that happened exactly 0 times.) Attacking also feels somewhat monotonous, apart from the standard slash you only get relics that really feel more like utility items - there are hardly any options to use or adapt movement abilities for combat (nothing but [spoiler]a way to turn the dash into a proper i-frame evasion[/spoiler]), and if an enemy floats outside of the range of your tiny poking stick (something that happens quite often especially at earlier points in the game) you can either stare at it, hoping it descends again, or you can leave. Combat setups, apart from the occasional enemy you engage during exploration, can be broadly divided into two categories: - Wave fights, where each subsequent wave spawns in as soon as you clear the previous one - the combination of instantaneous spawn and contact damage meaning you either learn the spawn positions of every single enemy, or you'll take a couple of free hits each time. (Remember the floating enemy part? Well, good luck leaving an arena with closed doors.) - Boss fights, with 1 phase and 3-4 repetitive moves each for the most part (for bosses with more than one phase the transitions seem only loosely bound to the actual health pool, meaning sufficient dps can lead to skipping a phase). Add the occasional broken mechanic that unintentionally punishes playing "by the rules", and those fights turn into pure dps checks where you rather wildly wail on the boss rather than properly facing its attack patterns. The part where several bosses apparently got stat-boosted due to complaints about them being pushovers doesn't solve that issue, instead it makes it even worse (as properly engaging those health-inflated bosses is now even less of an option).  TL;DR: the game looks and sounds pretty - if you're interested in the story it tells, go watch a playthrough; but don't bother playing, the gameplay is anything but fun.

Cependant, même si le jeu s'en inspire clairement (et c'est pas un mal, HK est devenu une référence), Voidwrought arrive malgré tout à tirer son épingle du jeu. Overall, a solid indie metroidvania with a Lovecraftian atmosphere, and an interesting, if sometimes hazy storyline. Gameplay is a bit linear until the second boss is defeated, but then it opens up into a proper metroidvania - because of this, more casual players might find the first two bosses difficult, but most other bosses can be made pretty easy with enough powerups.  When it comes to the story, a casual reading of the in-game texts and descriptions will give you a basic idea of what's going on, but a lot of the story is ambiguous, with some parts needing you to read in between the lines and connect the dots between disparate pieces of information given in very different places/contexts. Even then, some plot points remain ambiguous. If you're a lorehound whose not okay with puzzling through hints and subtext and still having some open questions, the game's story might not be for you.  Gameplay is generally good, with a couple of caveats. The linear nature of the game until the second boss makes those two bosses [i]relatively[/i] challenging, afterward, the difficulty of the bosses varies drastically based on how many and what powerups you gather before facing them. In what's possibly the most dramatic case [spoiler]the Fermented/Water Boss[/spoiler] is near trivial if you have the [spoiler]spurs[/spoiler] power up and are decent at bounce attacks, but if you go in without that powerup, you might find it an extremely challenging boss.   Boss fights generally have only a few different attacks, and the key to defeating them is generally learning the pattern or telegraphs to those attacks and how to counter them. That said, the game does suffer from not displaying boss health, making it impossible to tell how close you are to victory until the boss is defeated, and obscuring whether you're making progress on failed attempts. This was probably the most frustrating part of the game for me, and seems to be a fairly common complaint.  The only other flaw is that the game does not have a lot of replayability. You'll need at least two playthroughs to get all achievements, but after that, you may find little reason to play again.  That said, as mentioned in the beginning of this review, I generally consider the game a fun and worthwhile experience despite the flaws mentioned above. (Though I do hope the devs add a boss health bar and maybe a dlc that clarifies some of the storyline points in a future patch.)

While Voidwrought might not be ground-breaking nor redefine the wheel, it is a solid title that takes clear inspiration from Hollow Knight. If you need a metroidvania to ease your Silksong craving, this will definitely to the trick.  The "inspiration" part goes hard here, staying dangerously close between "inspired by" and "ripping off" in some instances. Still, you can clearly see a lot of effort put onto this game, and if you think about it it's better to get inspired by the best of the best rather than some other games, right?  Visually the game totally does the trick. While not as polished as other titles, I personally was pleased by the looks all the way through. Environments, NPCs, skills and lights are all eye-candy enough.  The soundtrack is surprisingly good too, with some tracks going hard.  The storyline was very mediocre to me and I didn't click into it. It was also written in a very convoluted way, which is a tendency in these dark fantasy games that I'm okay with unless they cross the line a bit too much.  As for the game's length, it took me around 12-13h to 100% the whole map on my first playthrough. While areas are pretty interesting and immersive, sometimes they made backtracking a bit too lazy, i.e. adding unremarkable collectibles on all areas dependant on all sorts of skills that you need to acquire first. That being said, the game has a generous range of equippable skills (the equivalent of charms in HK) to use, probably too much variety for a game this short.   Difficulty-wise, the game starts a bit too harsh compared to how it feels from mid-game until the end. Enemies and bosses will go from being a bit too tanky to be completely trivialized. It will provide for an interesting action experience regardless.   Lastly, achievements are a bit annoying, considering it includes missable achievements for sidequests with different outcomes, one achievement for each, making them mutually exclusive. I personally dislike this way of designing them so I prefer to warn you too.  Overall, even if Voidwrought doesn't really excell on any aspect in particular, I feel like it clearly tried to become a competitive game on an extremely competitive genre, and it succeeds to some regard. Heart and effort has been put into this and you will enjoy it a lot if you simply look into a metroidvania to dump some hours into.

Voidwrought was fun, but there are a few issues you should be aware of before buying. First, and the biggest hang-up for me, is that the game does not allow you to map keys to your mouse, at all. If you're using a keyboard instead of a controller, this might be a dealbreaker for you. If I had known this ahead of time, I probably wouldn't have bought the game at all. As is, I completed the game using a keyboard and binding my attack, dodge and slither to the number pad, which worked but never felt right. I can't imagine why the devs don't allow key binding to the mouse, it's baffling. Second, there is a bug during certain puzzles where, if you've remapped your movement keys to wasd, only some of the keys work, and you must use the arrow keys to move the puzzles' pieces in other directions. Not a huge deal, but it took some troubleshooting to figure it out, so just a heads up. Third, the game controls okay, but there is a sort of slipperiness when jumping and landing on platforms, in that you sorta slide after landing with any momentum, meaning that you can sometimes land on narrow platforms and just slide right off and over the other side. Not too bad, but it can get frustrating during backtracking and such. All in all though, the game was enjoyable and if you liked Hollow Knight, Blasphemous, the Last Faith, etc. I'd say give this one a try. The art is beautiful, combats fun, lots of different movement options, cool bosses.

Voidwrought (at this time of this review) feels like it should still be in the early access stage, and I'm sad to say that I wouldn't  really recommend it at full price in it's current form. There's many good things to say about the game: excellent detailed graphics/environments, cool parallax backgrounds, good enemy variety, challenging boss fights, excellent thematic music for the different zones (think ambient short-form, though some of it does get a bit repetitive after a while).  For all the good though, there's probably just as much that detracts from the experience. I haven't completed the game yet, but the more I play it, the more apparent the game's shortcomings become.  Combat: -The game limits you to one dedicated attack button that quickly becomes tiresome, because many of the enemies are sponges that take quite a lot of hits to defeat. Instead of having only one attack button, there could be one for light attacks and one for heavy, and having attack combos would be another option as well. Offering skill-based combat options would help go a long way in making the game less of a button-smasher. Incorporating the charged attack into the base moveset instead of having it be an ability you have to find would help as well, without making the character too overpowered.  -'Relics' (purchased through an in-game merchant) are great, and necessary additions, but these are specials that use charges which are replenished when you attack things, not really in the same category as the base moveset.  -Knockback for me, but none for thee? I'd say just get rid of this behavior entirely. It only serves to frustrate the player, and many enemies' movements are unhindered by the player's attacks so it's even more frustrating when they can just push right through your attacks and still damage you. This seems unbalanced, and causes a lot of frustration when you get knocked off of ledges because of it.  -The dodge button isn't very useful for combat. It's slow to use, and doesn't offer much for evasion purposes. It's main use is focused on rolling under areas in the environment that otherwise are inaccessible.  -There is also no blocking/parrying mechanic. This could possibly just be a design choice, but it really doesn't feel like something that was very intentional; it feels like more of an oversight currently. If the dodging mechanic was more prominent, I could maybe see this not being entirely necessary, but as it stands, the game feels like it would benefit from a block/parry system.  -Throwables/Ranged attacks. There's very little to none (that I'm aware of so far). This would be a big help as well, especially for some of the tougher enemies.  Navigation: -Fix navigational/travel oversights. There was a point in the game where I found myself stuck in the 'grand bazaar' area  (It's where the arena battle takes place before it connects back into the 'commons' area). There is a save point directly to the right of this area (good), but the previous save area before that doesn't allow you to travel back up if you don't have the double jump ability (the first ledge is just out of reach). I did finally beat it and was able to keep progressing, but it was very frustrating getting trapped in that area. (Maybe as a quick fix the game could implement save revisions to be able to roll it back from 2-3 saves ago?)  -Improve the fast travel system. The game (eventually) allows you to unlock the ability to travel between the current and previous save game area. Why limit the functionality of it like that though? I don't really think it would diminish the value of the traversal any to make travel between all save points accessible. Otherwise the functionality of it just isn't very useful. Maybe this function could be unlocked automatically (early on in the game) and fast travel between all save zones could be unlocked/purchased later on?

Writing after game completion with 95% Achievements.  As a casual player of MVs, I found Voidwrought absolutely enjoyable. To me, it's a health serving of combat, exploration with a dash of collecting and puzzle-solving.   A lot of players are saying that it's an easy game so the mileage of combat challenge is highly debatable. I found myself trying out a handful of souls and relics to get certain boss fights done. Took a couple of tries with a handful of them trying to figure out timings and such. So filthy casuals, I would say would have some level of challenge. There are far more frustrating bosses in other titles is all I can say.   The world is decently sized with a very colorful pallette. There are several biomes you get to explore and some parts are neatly tucked away by movement mechanics. The back-tracking wasn't hard as fast travel points become available pretty early.   The game warrants a 2nd playthrough just to get all achievements as there are a couple of exclusive ones based on your quest decisions.   A worthy pick-up for MV fans. The game actually makes me consider getting Hollow Knight already given the closely similar aesthetic but I don't know if I'm ready for cutesy bugs with bullet hell frenzy.

This game wants to be hollow knight so badly, but without the flowing movement, hitboxes that make sense, seamless progression, and combat that feels rewarding through the game. Would recommend it for the first little bit, but aside from that this game is not worth your 26 dollars

I do wish the explorers ability was all encompassing and could beep for every item not found. So i didn't 100% everything because i don't have  the resolve to keep doing that.

Consiglio questo gioco per le ambientazioni e la suggestione che riesce a suscitare. Voidwrought è un gioco parecchio affascinante, che sa attirare un appassionato di metroidvania, anche se presenta alcune caratteristiche sotto tono. In particolare, cito gli aspetti che secondo me funzionano meno, ossia un bilanciamento del gioco molto altalenante (inizialmente avrete difficoltà, ma già dopo poche ore il vostro personaggio avrà un consistente boost al suo potere), e l'estrema dipendenza di abilità per collezionare vari poteri e abilità. Per quasi metà del gioco non avrete accesso a moltissime aree, risultando che otterrete un buon 70% degli equipaggiamenti solo verso la fine del gioco. Non si tratta di equipaggiamenti molto più forti di quelli iniziali, ma un limite per la varietà del gameplay. Al netto di questi due grandi difetti, reputo Voidwrought un buon gioco, al di sotto di molte altre produzioni, ma sicuramente un prodotto sufficiente e meritevole di essere giocato.

For me, this game is too difficult. I can't speak to how the game was on launch, but apparently it was too easy and a patch was rolled out to address this. I'm admittedly not the greatest gamer, and at 40 I'm probably not getting any better. However, I have beaten my fair share of MVs including HK's good ending, the Ori games, Blasphemous, most of Aeterna Noctis on hard mode along with many more MVs, so if I'm struggling safe to say plenty of others players will be as well.   The bosses and enemies, even your trash mob variety, have huge health pools. Worse is that they deal contact damage quite liberally and enemies respawn every time you reenter a room (not just when you save). Those are some annoying NES mechanics that make backtracking a slog. The controls are also clunky and don't have a fun, snappy feel. The jump animation seems a tad too fast and propels you a set distance unless you adjust mid air and it all feels very slippery. Don't expect tight controls like in HK or Rogue Legacy, both of which I think really nail it. Those games have an almost the-floor-is-lava feel to them, while in Voidwrought just hopping to a small ledge can seem dicey.  If the game had that special something to keep me motivated to get gud, I might persist on but I think I'll just move on to something else. There's a fine like between challenge and frustration, and for me this leans too heavily on the latter.

[h1]In Need Of Lots of Tweaking And Redesigning[/h1]  By no means Voidwrought is a "bad" game. It has some moments, but a lot of confusing design choices bring the experience down. From way too easy difficulty to overlapping gating abilities, from cramped and over-designed level to almost non-existent story, the whole package just feels outdated comparing to other metroidvanias released in the same year.  [h1]Things I Like About Voidwrought:[/h1]  [b]Some Compliments[/b] [list] [*]I love the art style. It has a good balance between simple and detailed. Each level has a clear and varied theme. [*]Monsters and bosses' designs are nothing short of amazement. Shame their combat capabilities can't keep up. [*]You can freely swap your Souls and Relics anywhere you want even in battle. Interestingly, the cool-down time for each relic seem to be calculated individually. You can swap out the one on cool-down for another and use it immediately. This trick adds a much-needed depth to the simple combat. [/list]  [h1]Things I Dislike About Voidwrought:[/h1]  [b]1. Over-designed Level And Overlapping Abilities[/b] This is the first time I encounter this kind of peculiar problem Voidwrought has. [list] [*]The gating abilities in Voidwrought are nothing impressive, which would be fine if they have any secondary functions. Alas, they don't. [*]Sliding has no i-frame and feels awkward to use; Wall latch and wall jump shouldn't be separated abilities; Slither form completely overshadow grappling hook, wall jump and even double jump; You can only grapple the circular hooks in the level. You can't grapple enemies; Heavy attack, tech-possession, and phasing are just keys in different shapes; Dashing has a weird freezing moment at the end. [*]There is also no "guidance" to which abilities players should get first. Allow me to elaborate: most metroidvanias have clear indications where players can go and can not. They can't access this location unless they have X ability. Voidwrought doesn't do that. Instead most of the level entrance features two type of traversal mechanic, like the cliff that clearly needs grappling hook to go up also has small ledges for players to just wall jump up.  [*]This sense of freedom is certainly interesting, but the more I play the more confused I become. To me this design lessen the impact a gating ability should provide and also dwindle the excitement of using said ability. If I can access this place without grappling, what is the point of grappling? If I can just keep going, why should I go back and explore other places for new abilities? [*]As a result, levels need to cull some unnecessary traversal designs and close off some areas for proper ability-check. [*]What's funnier is that pogoing enemies allows you go to higher places meant for double jump. For my 15 hour of playthrough I genuinely thought this metroidvania is brave enough to go without double jump. Imagine my surprise and confusion when I finally got it after I've explored 95% of the map... [/list]  [b]2. Difficulty And The Dull Combat[/b] I don't want to be that guy who likes to "first try ggez noob gamemrkhojhc", but clearly something is wrong if I can just face-tank and spam every single boss with ease. [list] [*]It's hard to pinpoint which aspect of the combats is wrong: maybe the health of every enemy needs some boost, maybe our attack speed need some slight speed change, or maybe the bosses need more distinct attack patterns. [*]Currently enemies feel way too squishy to our attack. It is way easier to rush through than to learn their move sets. [*]Bosses also lack more impactful performance. They get the striking appeal but not matching strength.  [*]Our combat options may also be the culprit to the problem: spam attack and use two relic abilities. The protagonist's attack is really fast, and the relics are really powerful even with no combo. [*]Platforming doesn't fare well either. Not a single platforming challenge requires you to use more than two abilities. [/list]  [b]3. Other Complaints[/b] [list] [*]I know story in metroidvania is often light, but Voidwrought is almost devoid of any story elements. All we have is dialogue. [*]The marking system is horrendous, archaic even. [*]A note for all the quest progression would be nice. [/list]  中文小簡評 →本身不糟，就是諸多詭異設定讓遊戲體驗很差。 →這款類銀河系惡魔城給我的感覺就是生錯時間了(X。 →很喜歡這款的畫風，有點像空洞騎士和終結者莉莉的集合體。怪物與NPC的設計也非常到位，可惜實際打起來還是差強人意。 →可以隨時隨地切換靈魂(被動技能)和遺物(主動技能)。有趣的一點，遺物技能放完會進冷卻，可以立刻切換新遺物放新技能，算是讓單調的戰鬥多點樂趣的法子。 →這款的關鍵技能設計平庸就算了，在戰鬥中竟然也無法靈活運用，形同變相鑰匙。 →掛牆跟蹬牆應該合而為一；滑行沒有無敵禎用起來也彆扭；蠕行完全可以取代勾鎖、蹬牆甚至是二段跳；續力攻擊、附身機關和定點穿牆就只是變相的鑰匙；衝刺結尾有個很詭異的僵直。 →有個特別的問題只有這款有，關卡並沒有明確掌握玩家動向。一般類銀河系惡魔城在關卡間會有明確的坎，玩家沒有某技能就無法通過。Voidwrought並沒有這樣設計，同一坎常常設計兩種以上的通過方式(明顯是要用勾鎖過的地方牆邊卻也有縫讓你爬)。或許這樣設計會讓探索的自由度上升，但對我來說反而讓關鍵技能的重要性降低、獲得技能的欣喜感也大幅降低，如果不用勾鎖都能過，那我又何必回頭找勾鎖呢? →玩的時侯我連這遊戲有二段跳都不知道，走完了95%地圖才找到... →如此一來，我認為諸多關卡需要好好重新設計，把一些坎做好。 →遊戲難度無論是小怪還是Boss都太低了，可能是血量太少，也可能是主角攻速太快、技能傷害太高，也有可能是Boss的技能組太不顯眼。 →跳台的部分也沒有好到哪裡去，幾乎沒有需要玩家用到兩個以上的技能的挑戰。 →我知道類銀河系惡魔城要講故事有難度，但這款的故事有跟沒有一樣，幾乎是毫無交代，只有NPC對話玩家難以融入。 →地圖標記系統有夠難用。 →任務如果有備忘錄該有多好啊。

Ну я поиграл - по первой локе (и то даже там на боссе слился раз 5) поясню немного - играл я с клавиатуры (увы без мышки это уже не очень то приятно). А волшебная указательная стрелка ведет в АД, в локацию где долбанная механическая сколопендра, в трейлере если что она есть.  Ну как итог для меня в игре плюсов не нашлось, я конечно понимаю что за ручку меня никто водить не должен, но я много игр проходил на подобии и большинство старых таких огрехов не имела.  Со старта ты почти ничего не знаешь и должен искать (хотя рывок(нормальное уклонение) могли бы и не прятать без него вообще не возможно, даже в соулсах он со старта, но не тут), как итог я открыл базовый перекат чтобы протискиваться в локи, затем возможность цепляться за стены и на самом дне первой локи за счет абузов(удар убежал) завалил гиганта и мага и за ними получил когти которые аж бафают твою атаку и обычный моб умирает не с 5 а с 4 атак. И всё больше открытых лок у меня нет, в остальные нужны какие то новые навыки которые хз где брать, а босс не факт что поможет.  В итоге меня всё задолбало я включил читэнджен, забил хп на бессмертие и прошел по локе которая "Поверхность" а за ней следом как раз место обитания сколопендры. Само собой не стал проходить ведь это не честно. Но то что Сколопендра просто бьет по всей арене шипами от которых не уклониться, так же как и шипы по очереди где сдвиг влево вправо чуть больше и урон снова идет.  После такого задумался - правильно ли я пошел и не пропустил ли микро кусочек карты где было нужное улучшение и упрощение жизни я хз. Как итог ты со старта можешь заруинить себе прохождение просто не разобравшись.   Ну  и да вишенка на торте это долбаный контактный урон, нет он мог бы быть, но не с боссами и снарядами которые появляются в тебе, как и противники, это уже проблема... Буду ли я еще играть попробую разок. Ну и да крайнее бесячее - отсутствие шортактов - чтобы спуститься вниз до первого босса и подняться нужно дофига времени полазить по всем уступам, а с учетом "скольжения" героя можно с них падать и начинать с начала, а там и моб и ловушка тебя еще ударят и потом снова с контрольной точки ползти...   Последнее чем дополню (уже через день после написанного), для меня не является каноном и иконой оригинальный Метроид. Канон у меня простой и уже старенький Valdis Story: Abyssal City уж прошу прощения, что тогда это была пиратка, но хочу в дальнейшем купить в стиме, так как вспомнил о ней сейчас спустя 11 лет играя в эту игру. И вот насколько она классная настолько тут я не получил ничего. Парирование боссы, сложность понарастающей и опять тебя не сильно то кто то ведет за ручку, сюжет и прочее. Ну и да причина пиратки еще была в русификаторе) увы по сей день не все проекты у нас имеют даже текстовые субтитры.

The Pros:  -The art and atmosphere are amazing. The art style was obviously inspired by Hollow Knight, but the game has its own theme and vibe with a mix of Ancient Egyptian and Lovecraftian elements.   -The music and sound design are quite good and I didn't get tired of the soundtrack. -Solid and rewarding exploration, though I felt there was too much backtracking overall (see Cons). I really liked the excavation system. -The story isn't super deep, but it was cool, which was just fine with me. You do need to read the lore items, journal entries, etc. to understand it though.   The Meh:  -Combat is decent, but not amazing. You can equip two different special damage abilities at a time by finding/equipping different relics, and three different modifications by finding/equipping different souls. and there some pretty cool ones. I enjoyed using the laser beams and enhanced healing abilities. Overall, combat is good but not quite at the level of the combat in other Metroidvanias such as Hollow Knight or Nine Sols. -A couple of little things don't have sound effects for whatever reason. There should be a slight sound effect whenever you gain a void charge back, but there isn't and the charge attack has one, but it's not loud enough.  The Cons:  -There was too much backtracking overall, which leads to some poor pacing. This is a Metroidvania, so a decent amount of backtracking is to be expected, but I felt the ability to warp back to the hub was unlocked too late in the game.  -I don't like contact damage in these games. -No rumble. It seems to be in the game's settings, but it didn't work on my controller whatsoever.   Overall, I enjoyed Voidwrought and couldn't stop playing until I finished it. Platforming and combat are significantly easier compared with some other MVs out there, but the game is still a very fun time. I'd give it like an 8/10 and recommend it to anyone wanting a fun MV to play!

This game is great! The art style is beautiful, the soundtrack is solid and the movement is super smooth. The abilities you gain through the game are typical for a metroidvania, but you do get a couple of unique and creative ones.  I have a few complaints, but nothing drastic enough to ruin the experience.  1- I got lost at one point and almost quit because I couldn't find where to go and felt I was ability gated everywhere. 2- This game is a collectathon which is great, but there's nothing to indicate what you're missing or anything to reveal the hidden items you may not have found. There is a soul that can help find secrets you missed, but if it's an item that's not behind a fake wall the soul doesn't help you find it. 3- In my playthrough I was able to get both endings on 1 save, but there's one or 2 achievements that require you to replay the game to unlock. I'm not a big fan of this personally. The game has 69 achievements and I can unlock about 67 of them on a single playthrough. I'm not running to play the game all over for 2 achievements, which sucks for trying to be a completionist. I have too many games in the backlog to play the game a second time.   Now that I got the negatives out of the way... I do highly recommend any metroidvania fan to play this game. It is fantastic. Bosses aren't especially hard, but I'm ok with that. I never finished Hollow Knight (I know, now some people will say my review is no longer worthy) I spent about 20 hours in Hollow Knight and dropped it. I know this game gets heavily compared to Hollow Knight, but which metroidvania doesn't. I don't personally think it's anything like Hollow Knight, other than maybe the pogo. I think it has its own identity of just being a solid metroidvania. Worth the time and doesn't require 40+ hours to finish, for those who have a life outside of gaming.  Well done @Powersnake!!!

붉은 별의 도래로 인해 변화한 세계, 그리고 지하에서 깨어나게 된 주인공이 자신의 목적을 이루기 위해 망가진 세상을 탐험하는 이야기.  Voidwrought 은 게임의 시작부터 “고대의 목소리”, “붉은 별”, “창시자” 등등의 쉽지 않은 단어들로 스토리를 간단한 텍스트 + 컷씬의 조합으로 보여주는 게임으로, 딱 봐도 코스믹 호러 장르에서 영향을 받은 설정들을 무더기로 볼 수 있는 게임이다. 이러한 특징은 주인공의 정체에서부터 묻어 나오는데, 인간형 주인공으로 보이지만 실체는 공허에서 유래한 듯한 점액 덩어리에 더 가까운 생명체이며 – 바닥에 몸을 맞대고 슬라이딩을 할 때, 우리가 흔히 생각하는 인간이 슬라이딩하는 모습이 아니라 아예 신체가 변화하는 모습을 볼 수 있다 – 태어나는 과정 및 지하의 성소를 재건하는 스토리에서의 행방 또한 코스믹 호러 장르 속의 주인공이 거칠 법한 내용을 담고 있다. 그렇다고 게임의 스토리가 이러한 장르의 게임 중에서 심오하거나 임팩트가 크다고 하기에는 애매한 편이다. 게임의 장르가 메트로배니아인 만큼 스토리에 대한 큰 기대는 하지 않고 시작하였으나, 게임 내 고유 명사의 남발 및 스토리 흐름과 플레이어의 관심이 크게 일치하지 않는다는 특징 때문에, Voidwrought 의 스토리는 크게 기억에 남을 만한 이야기는 아니었다. 게임 내 세계관을 보충해 주는 다양한 텍스트 및 세부적 설정들에 대한 내용이 담겨 있기는 하지만 – 예를 들자면, 각 보스들을 무찌를 때마다 그들에 대한 설명을 읽을 수 있고, 게임 내 세상의 구역이나 아이템들에 대한 설명을 읽으면서 세계관에 대한 이해를 시도해 볼 수 있다 – 이들을 꼼꼼하게 읽는다고 해서 주인공에게 몰입이 가거나 보스들의 서사가 이해되는 게 아니라 “아 그렇구나 …. “ 정도로 머무르는 단편적인 감상평에 그치게 될 것이다. 그나마 사이드 퀘스트 두 개 정도는 마음에 들었으며, 게임 내 세상을 탐험하고 곳곳에 존재하는 신도들을 모아서 (주인공 입장에서) 본거지 같은 곳을 확장하여 엔딩에 도달하는 성장 구조는 괜찮았으나, 막상 게임 내 세상을 탐험하고 보스들을 때려잡는 동기가 서사적인 면에서 그리 강하게 느껴지지 않았다.  특히, 최종 보스 및 진 최종 보스의 경우, GRIME 과 같이 스토리가 난해한 다른 메트로배니아 게임에서는 적어도 최종 보스를 왜 잡는지 + 이 보스가 서사 면에서 어떠한 위치를 취하는지는 플레이어가 이해하기 쉽게 설정해 놓아서, 속된 말로 플레이어가 보스를 잡는 데서 종지부를 찍는다는 희열감을 느끼게 만들어 주는데, 여기서는 도대체 왜 최종 보스를 때려잡고 있는지 의문이 들 정도였다. 진 최종 보스를 해금하는 과정도 악랄한 편인데, 부정적 평가 중 “할로우나이트로 치면 지도없이 애벌레를 전부 모아야 진엔딩을 볼 수 있다” 라는 한국어 평가가 있다. 다행인 건, 게임이 출시한 뒤 업데이트를 통해 탐험 난이도를 약간 낮춰서 평가를 쓰는 시간 기준으로는 이 정도의 난이도가 아니기는 점인데, 그럼에도 불구하고 지도 곳곳에 숨겨져 있는 특정 NPC 들을 모두 찾아야 진 엔딩을 볼 수 있기에 + 왜 이 과정을 거쳐야 진엔딩을 볼 수 있는지 플레이어 입장에서는 서사적으로 전혀 이해가 되지 않기에 + 진 최종 보스의 디자인이 그렇게 나쁜 건 아닌데 한 번 피격 받으면 회복하기 어려운 스테이지 구조 및 그리 재미있지 않은 보스 패턴을 지니고 있기에, 진엔딩을 보는 과정 및 진 최종 보스를 때려잡는 과정이 썩 재밌지는 않은 편이다.  물론, 이 게임의 장르가 위에서 적었듯이 메트로배니아 이므로, 스토리에 대한 불평불만보다는 게임플레이 및 탐험의 재미가 잘 뽑혀 나왔는지 생각해 볼 필요가 있다. 게임을 꼼꼼하게 살펴볼 의향이 있다고 가정하면, 게임의 지도 크기 및 탐험 관련 기동성은 그렇게 나쁘지 않은 편이다. 전자의 경우, 지도가 적당히 크면서 구역별 테마 및 색감 사용이 확연하며, 각종 숨겨진 벽 및 수집품을 찾는 소소한 재미도 잘 들어가 있다. 게임의 전체적인 비주얼 또한 잘 뽑혔기에, 게임 내 다양한 지역의 배경 및 보스들을 눈으로 감상하는 맛이 있다. 특히, 게임을 진행하며 처음으로 지표면을 보았을 때, 눈 앞에 보이는 지하 세계와의 극명한 차이점은 인상적이었다. 후자의 경우, 사실 게임의 초반부에는 주인공의 이동 능력이 좀 답답하며, 할로우 나이트에서 보이는 포고 점프를 억지로 사용해서 게임을 몇몇 구간을 진행해야 한다는 느낌이 강하지만 – “억지로” 라고 쓴 이유는 게임의 포고 조작이 그리 매끄럽지는 않으며, 후반부에 가면 이를 사용할 이유가 없기 때문이다 – 이후에 이단 점프 / 공중 대쉬 / 벽타기 및 틈새 이동을 획득하기 때문에 이 때부터는 그냥 허공답보 수준으로 맵을 탐방할 수 있기에, 모든 능력을 다 배우면 기동력 면에서 아쉬운 점을 느끼지는 않을 것이다. 전투의 경우, 깊이가 그리 있지는 않으며, 적과 충돌 판정이 있기 때문에 단순히 “적을 때릴 타이밍이 나오면 열심히 때리다가 플레이어에게 충돌하려 하면 도망치기” 의 연속이다. 물론 평타로 죽을 때까지 때리는 건 아니고, 다른 게임으로 비유하면 마나와 비슷한 자원을 얻을 수 있는데, 적을 때리다 보면 서서히 차오르며 이를 이용해 강력한 공격 / 버프를 시전할 수 있다. 의외로 이 자원이 넉넉하게 차오르는 편이며, 이 때문에 아득바득 아껴 쓰는 체력 회복 수단과는 달리 좀 더 여유있게 이러한 특수 행동들을 취할 수 있다는 건 나쁘지 않았다.  그러나, Voidwrought 는 완성도는 그럭저럭 괜찮았지만 게임을 플레이하면서 느낀 몇 가지 단점들이 게임에 대한 애정을 깎아 내리는 아쉬운 게임이었다. 개인적으로 메트로배니아라는 장르에 속한 게임들을 그리 많이 하지는 않았고, 인기 많은 메트로배니아들을 부정적으로 평가하여 이 장르의 팬들이 보면 경악할 만한 선택들을 내리기는 했으나, Voidwrought 은 애매한 편의성 및 조작 문제 때문에 이 장르의 입문용 게임으로 추천하기에 애매하며, 게임플레이 면에서 재미있는 게임 메커니즘이나 독특한 면모가 존재한다고 하기도 힘들었고, 그렇다고 Afterimage 처럼 “탐험에 취해 죽어 봐라 ㅋㅋㅋㅋㅋ” 라고 탐험을 하며 희열을 느끼는 등산가들을 위해 추천하기에도 쉽지 않다. 개인적으로 게임을 플레이하며 느낀 단점들 / 아쉬운 점들을 적어 보자면 :  > 위의 문단에서 게임을 플레이하다 보면 기동성 면에서 벽타기 및 틈새 이동 능력을 해금한다고 말했는데, 이 능력을 사용하면 주인공이 공허 덩어리로 변하면서 – 굳이 다른 게임과 비유를 하자면, 촉수괴물이 나와서 사람을 학살하고 다니는 Carrion 의 캐릭터 모습을 축소시킨 버전을 생각하면 쉽다 – 얇은 촉수와 같은 팔을 벽에 부착해서 벽을 기어다니고, 좁은 통로에서도 역시 이 형태로 변신해서 다닐 수 있다. 문제는 이렇게 변신했을 때의 속도인데, Carrion 에서는 주인공이 유연하고 날렵하게 움직였다면 여기서는 무슨 굼벵이가 수면제 먹고 비몽사몽 하는 속도로 움직인다. 다른 기동력 기능인 이단점프 및 공중대쉬는 사용할 때 날렵하고 시원시원한 느낌이었다면, 이 기능은 그 쾌감을 다 절단하는 기분이다. 또 다른 문제는 이 답답한 능력을 게임 100% 를 달성하기 위해 꽤 많이 사용해야 한다는 점인데, 위에서 언급한 포고 점프의 경우 게임의 초반에만 쓰지 이후에는 쓸 일이 없지만, 틈새 이동 능력의 경우 많은 방에서 우려 먹기 때문에 눈물을 머금으면서 주인공이 달팽이처럼 기어다니는 걸 관찰하고 있어야 한다. 여기에 더해, 몇몇 지역의 경우 이단 점프 / 공중 대쉬를 해금하지 않아도 어거지로 벽타기 능력을 써서 도달할 수 있는데, 문제는 이렇게 아득바득 지도를 밝하는 게 잘못된 건 아니지만 플레이어들에게 잘못된 진행 방향을 머릿속에 심어 줄 수 있다는 문제점이 존재한다. 몇몇 한글 평가에서도 이 문제점을 적어 놓은 걸 보면, 나만 이렇게 창의적으로 길을 뚫다가 몇 시간 동안 막히고 공략을 봐야 하는 난처한 상황에 처하게 된 게 아니라는 걸 알 수 있다.  > 단순한 전투 시스템 자체는 그리 불만이 크지 않은데, 크게 전투 관련 두 가지 특징이 게임을 부조리하게 만들었다. 먼저, 주인공 캐릭터가 피격을 받으면 확연하게 화면에 보이지는 않을 망정 희미하게 보이게 되는데, 도대체 왜 굳이 이런 피격 후 시각적 효과를 적용했는지 모르겠다. 거짓말이 아니라, 아예 아무런 시각적 효과를 넣지 않고 주인공이 움찔하는 모션만 넣었어도 지금보다 나을 것이다. 두 번째로는, 게임을 진행하다 보면 – 다른 메트로배니아 게임들에서 보이는 – 좁은 방에 억지로 가둔 뒤 여러 몬스터들을 무찌르도록 유도하는 전투 방들이 나오는데, 이 방 안에서 몬스터가 스폰되는 위치가 명확히 표시되지 않고 스폰되다 보니 스폰되는 위치에 서 있으면 그냥 얻어 맞는다는 점이다. 적이 스폰되자마자 바로 히트박스가 생기는 판정이다 보니, 피격당하지 않으려면 스폰 위치를 외우고 그 곳에서 피하는 방법밖에 없다. 이 외에도, 몇몇 적들의 애매한 히트박스 / 완전하게 가만히 서 있지 않으면 조작키가 높은 확률로 안 먹히는 현상 (특히 회복을 해야 할 때 이 문제를 많이 느낄 수 있을 것이다) / 화면을 전환할 때마다 스폰하는 적들 등등 별로라고 생각된 부분들이 있었다.  > 보스들의 난이도 및 난이도 곡선이 기묘하게 설정되어 있다. Voidwrought 에는 몇 개의 데미지 업그레이드가 존재하기는 하는데, 첫 번째 보스와 두 번째 보스는 데미지 업그레이드 없이 싸워야 할 것이다. 문제는 이 두 보스의 설계가 괴랄하다는 점. 첫 번째 보스의 경우 무려 5페이즈의 보스 전투인데, 물론 난이도 자체는 쉽지만 보스전의 길이가 짧지 않고, 플레이어가 그렇게 강하지 않은 데다가 게임의 미묘한 히트박스에 적응할 시간이 충분히 주어지지 않은 시점이라 게임의 첫 번째 보스 전투라고 하기에는 피곤하다. 두 번째 보스의 경우 페이즈 자체는 3페이즈 정도로 많지 않지만, 패턴은 단순한데 피통은 많은 잡몹들의 소환 + 마지막 페이즈의 히트박스를 때리기 귀찮다는 점 + 포고 점프의 강제화 때문에 역시 첫 번째 보스처럼 보스전이 피곤하다. 두 보스 모두 보스 자체의 체력이 과하다고는 할 수 없는데 그 외의 부분에서 귀찮은 점들이 존재하다 보니 플레이어가 짜증을 느끼는 보스라고 할 수 있다. 웃긴 건, 이후 데미지 업그레이드 및 체력 업그레이드를 모으다 보면 잡몹들 및 보스들은 최소한의 무빙 및 맞딜로 잡을 수 있어서 난이도가 초반의 그 쓴맛에서 많이 내려간다는 것. 어느 정도냐면, 최종 보스 잡는 데 걸린 시간이 첫 번째 보스 잡는 데 걸린 시간과 거의 동일하다.  > 게임의 특정 사이드 퀘스트를 깨면 게임 내 숨겨진 비밀들을 알려주는 패시브를 해금할 수 있다. 이 패시브의 경우 찾지 못한 비밀이 존재하는 방에 들어가면 플레이어의 머리 위에 시각적 표시를 해주다가, 비밀에 점점 가까워지면 그 시각적 표시가 강해지는 느낌이다. 쉬운 비유를 들자면, 금속 탐지기를 들고 땅을 탐사하다가 금속에 가까워지면 기기가 내뿜는 신호가 강력해지는 걸 생각하면 된다. 여기까지만 보면 메트로배니아에 있어서 매우 좋은 기능 같은데, 게임 내 비밀이라고 여기는 것과, 게임 내 존재하는 수집품의 수집 여부가 일치하지 않는다. 즉, 몇 가지의 수집품 및 사이드 퀘스트들은 비밀인데, 다른 수집품들은 비밀이 아니다 (?????). 메트로배니아 장르에 진심인 사람이라면 이 사람이 뭔 헛소리를 하는지 경악할 수 있는데, 거짓말이 아니라 수많은 수집품들이 비밀로 취급되지 않아서 플레이어가 진짜로 모든 수집품을 찾고 싶다면 인터넷에 지도를 찾아보던가 아니면 직접 발로 뛰면서 모든 구성을 살펴봐야 한다. 참고로 이 비밀로 취급되지 않는 수집품 중에 무려 체력 업그레이드가 있어서, 나처럼 “와 모든 비밀 발견했네!” 라고 안심하다가 체력 업그레이드 중 안 먹은 게 있는 걸 보고 맵을 두 세번 더 도는 참사가 벌어질 수 있다.  > 게임 내 수집품 경제관념이 이상하다. 당연히 체력 및 공격력 관련 업그레이드는 게임 내 한정된 수량이 존재하며 이들을 모두 찾으면 더 이상 얻는 방식이 없기는 한데, 그 외 잡다한 수집품들의 밸런스가 애매하다. 예시를 들자면, 지도 곳곳에 찾을 수 있는 수집품인 “신성한 잔재” 의 경우 특정 고급 아이템 구매에 쓰여서 플레이어가 극한의 노가다로 아이템들을 구매하는 걸 방지한다. 그런데 게임의 끝까지 플레이하다 보면 인벤토리에 20개 이상 남아 돌게 된다. 반대로 비슷한 위치의 수집품인 “유물 조각” 의 경우 게임 곳곳을 뒤져도 모든 수집품을 구매하는 데 부족해서 상인 NPC 에게 따로 구매를 추가적으로 해야 한다. 또한, 적을 잡다 보면 두 종류의 재화가 등장하는데, 왜 하나만 등장하게 설정을 안 했는지도 모르겠고, 이 두 종류의 재화 중 “미량의 금속” 은 넘쳐 나는데 다른 재화는 묘하게 부족하다. 그냥 단순하게 지도 100% 를 뒤지면 모든 물건을 구매할 수 있게 설정하면 되는 걸, 왜 여분이 남거나 부족하게 설정해서 플레이어를 불편하게 만들었는지 모르겠다.   결론적으로, 게임의 완성도 및 시청각적인 면모가 그리 나쁘지는 않은데, 직접 게임을 플레이하는 과정 및 게임을 완전 정복하는 과정이 같은 장르의 다른 게임들에 비해 매력이 떨어졌기 때문에 비추천. 플레이타임의 경우 게임 100% 완료에 16시간 정도 걸렸는데, 엔딩을 보는 것 자체는 8 ~ 10 시간 안에 볼 수 있을 정도로 그렇게 긴 게임은 아니다. 맨 위에서도 적었듯이 메트로배니아 입문용으로는 추천하지 않고, 이 장르의 굵직한 게임 몇 개를 해 본 경험이 있어서 약간의 짬밥이 있으며 + 이 장르를 좋아한다면 직접 플레이 하면서 큰 스트레스를 느끼지 않을 것이라 생각한다. 가격 대비 플레이타임은 그리 나쁘지는 않지만, 2024 겨울 할인 때 할인률이 33% 로 출시일 대비 높은 편인 과거가 있어서, 정가에 사는 것보다는 적당히 할인할 때 사는 게 나을 것이다.  여담) 업적의 경우 대부분 게임 100% + 모든 보스 잡기와 관련되어 있고, 기본 엔딩을 본 뒤 같은 세이브파일 안에서 게임을 이어 할 수 있기 때문에, “일반 엔딩 감상 > 지도 100% 밝히기 > 진 엔딩 보기” 라는 매끄러운 진행 방식을 통해 게임을 싹싹 긁어 먹으면 업적을 대부분 딸 수 있다. 문제는 게임 내 사이드 퀘스트들의 선택에 따라 업적이 묶여 있는데, 한 번 대답하면 그 선택이 고정되다 보니 대답 한번 잘못했다고 업적을 달성하지 못하는 경우가 많다. 게임 내 세이브를 수동으로 백업한 뒤 선택을 내리고 이전 세이브를 불러와야 강제 2회차를 강요받지 않을 것이다. 스팀 가이드에 사이드 퀘스트 관련 설명이 있는 100% 업적 가이드가 있으니, 꼭 이를 읽으면서 놓칠 수 있는 업적을 체크하는 걸 권장한다.  ** 2025.02.18 NG+ 업데이트 이후 정보 - 중요!)  새로운 업적 추가로 인해, 업적 100% 를 위해서는 위에서 적은 것처럼 게임 100% 클리어와 함께, 하드코어 모드 클리어 + NG+ (같은 세이브 파일에서 2회차 진행) 3번 클리어를 해야 한다. 업적 100% 용도로 추천하기에는 이제 힘든 게임이니, 여기서 언급된 업적들이 사라지지 않았다면 업적 애호가들은 고민을 해 보는 걸 권장한다.
Hollow Knight wannabe that is a surprisingly great game.

Juegazo!, visualmente hermoso, (muy parecido a Hollow Knight). Diseño de personajes, jefes y enemigos bastante original e intrigante, con un aura bastante oscura.  El sonido y la musica tambien son un punto a destacar, va perfecto con la estética del juego. Tiene mecánicas típicas de metroidvania clásico, digamos que no trae nada nuevo, pero bueno.... Si disfrutas del género no dudes en meterle unas horitas!

It's all right, but I have a few things worth mentioning for the devs in case they're reading.  1) EXPLORATION AND NAVIGATION  The map is lacking. It's kinda ok, but It doesn't show progress and doesn't show the void rooms. It's OK to omit void for lore reasons, but I still would like to know which rooms I don't have to explore over and over again...   Also, I'd like to have some in-built collectibles map or something. Cause at this point it's a real pain to find something you've missed if it's not behind a fake wall.  2) ABILITIES  Yes, once again with the abilities conflict. Slither ability gives too much compared to other unlocks. The Hook, on the contary, is acquired too late and gave me too little when compared to slither. Jumping off the walls gave me outright nothing compared to double jump and dash.  Also, how is swimming in the acid not an ability? It could be an upgrade to the sphere you create to teleport.  3) SMOOTHNESS  The movements still feel stiff. Surely there isn't much platforming here (which is a shame), but it still feels like the character could me more responsive. It took a while to get used to it.  4) DAMAGE  In the begining, you have to hit the most basic enemies at least 5 times and there are full rooms of them! Some bosses later are pushovers, others are ridiculously thick and you don't even know whether you are dealing damage to them. So boss HP bar might be a good option after all.   5) ACHIEVEMENTS  Even though I liked the game, I don't enjoy the idea of replaying it anytime soon. So all these missable achievements that have to do with choices are somewhat of a waste. One achievement for finishing a questline should have been enough.  Other than that, well, it's a pretty solid release and I enjoyed playing it! It has its rough patches and odd solutions, but it's also been fun to explore. Many thanks!
A true Metroidvania. I got all the lore, got the true ending, and still have no idea what happened.

I enjoyed the game for the most part, but some design choices somewhat lessened my satisfaction with it overall. I still recommend checking it out because it was mostly fun. However, I think some of the elements (relics and souls for example) could have been more well thought out and implemented in a more meaningful way. I also think, for completion's sake, that the map could have been done with a bit more love. I didn't really understand what the item you get that's supposed to help with that was supposed to be doing. Not to mention there are a few different, seemingly simple, things from the earliest and newer titles in the genre that could have been implemented to make completion more satisfying. Most of the music was pretty good (the metal tracks were awesome for some of the boss fights), but the some of the exploration music left something to be desired, in my opinion. All-in-all if you're looking for a new game in the genre to check out, I'd say this one is worth a go.
buen metroidvania, buena mecanica, falta un poco de lore, pero disfrutable

I do and don't recommend this game in the end. It was decent as a Metroidvania to a degree, but it was also annoying to a point where I wouldn't suggest going for 100% due to no real way for finding what you're missing without going over each area extremely carefully, I was working towards it, but got to the point where I didn't find myself enjoying it any longer.  Another issue I found was that while you did have a form of a dodge it feels like crap to use, and if you want what is imo a better dodge you have to use one of your "charm" slot for it which I thought was just bad when it could have just been built into the ability when you get it, and it would have felt fine.  It controls OK, but has some annoyance with not being able to chain your abilities quickly, which makes it feel off at times, or that during a boss you're standing around trying to activate your buffs and eating hits due to it not being quick enough.
Starts slow but it picks up the pace later on. The world is well crafted, interesting and beautiful.
Not as polished as HK but it will do. Starts off really slow though. If you can get past the 2 hour mark, it gets fun.

un metroidbania interesante al cual le pusieron un mapa muy poco detallado, tenes que estar atento a todo desde el principio para marcar lo en el mapa y después volver con nuevas habilidades, un poco desordenado el sistema de habilidades podes conseguirla fácilmente en orden aleatorio alguna ni son necesarias para terminar el juego (pero le dan mas opciones a las peleas) una misión opcional te da el "instinto" para detectar secretos pero es bastante complicado de conseguir en ese punto ya seria recorre todo el juego a ciegas de nuevo para hacer el 100% me gusta mas cuando te dan un mapa donde te marcan todo
Such a lovely game, if you are hungry for more metroidvania then this is a great game to play!
前期引导有点问题，并且中后期有点堆怪。地图足够自由，基本上可以满世界乱跑（强烈批评隔壁某个姓波的几乎纯线性流程的类银）。难度也不算高，我最开始没搞懂收藏库的用法，顶着一次攻击力提升硬打后期BOSS也是乱杀。

So far this has been a solid metroidvania experience. For me it has one of the best feels in its exploration, there is constantly new places to go and many paths to choose from. It is very freeing that way and progress feels fun and goes fast. You get a map that gets filled in automatically and you have markers that you can use when you see secrets/items that you can't collect yet. The normal enemies are usually on the easier side while the bosses are quite challenging, perfect difficulty for me. I also enjoy that it is not punishing, it is fine to die you don't lose anything. Art style and music is are good.  As for negatives one of the later traversal abilities feels slow and clunky to use and the story is confusing and uninteresting. I also think this game will be quite short compared to most other metroidvanias which is alright by me since it won't outlast its welcome. It can be quite unclear with what to do, there is little handholding here which I think works when the game isn't overly punishing. Lore and story is on the weaker side while focusing on the gameplay.  For me its a recommend for anyone that likes exploration, finding secrets and collecting things.  Other good metroidvanias in aproximate order: Hollow Knight, Nine sols, Deaths door, Ori and the will of the wisp 2, Haiku the robot, Lone fungus, Blasphemous 2, Ender lilies, Islets, Bo: path of the teal lotus
Excellent metroidvania

I was having fun, until I get a game breaking glitch preventing me from upgrading my stuff. There is a system called excavating in where you expand your home and get access to other areas. My game decided to not remove debris after I paid for the excavating locking me out of upgrades, abilities and whatnot. Plus it autosaved so I couldn't even go back to before I did it. This will stay here until it gets fixed so I can actually play the game I bought. Cause the last thing I want to do is restart the whole game just for it to do this again.

Шикарная метроидвания. Советую скачать демку, где вам сразу будет доступен персонаж в полном мувменте и обвесах, это даст вам представление о лейт гейме игры (а то люди в обзорах жаловалась что тут деша нет, хотя их два вида).   Начинать же как и обычно придется со скудного арсенала мувмента и спецприямов, впрочем оба достаточно быстро расширяются. Мир очень большой, 12 биомов, коллектоболсов море. Визуал очень приятный и в целом игра очень напоминает холлоу найт, только разве что более дружелюбную - вам хотя бы карту с самого начала дают, да и все биомы раскрашены своим цветом на миникарте что упрощает навигацию.  К разработчикам: Пожалуйста добавьте возможность выбора телепорта напрямую через навигацию по миникарте, через список это не очень удобно - у тебя телепорт на соседнем этаже а ты 7 раз кликаешь вверх/вниз чтобы его в списке найти.

Un metroidvania apenas entretenido. No es un mal juego, y las pocas cosas que hace bien, son aquellas copiadas directamente de Hollow Knight. Otras cosas en las que intenta innovar, están o mal planteadas, o mal ejecutadas, o tienen algún punto cuestionable. Da sus horas, a veces mas interesantes y otras tantas bastante aburridas, y me cuesta encontrar algún punto que lo haga especialmente destacable.   [h3] Cosas aceptables [/h3]  - [b][u] Copia a Hollow Knight [/u][/b]:  copiar no es algo necesariamente malo, siempre depende de cómo se haga. Este juego simplemente copia. Copia el feeling mecánico, la estética, el sistema de mejoras, y varias cosas mas. Tal es así, que ya en la primera zona, el juego presupone que sepas hacer el pogo y además lo hagas relativamente bien o no podrás avanzar. Es meritorio sí, que en términos mecánicos hayan logrando acercarse bastante al movimiento, hitstun, pushback, pogo y varias cosas tomadas de Hollow Knight. Muchos juegos han intentado eso y no lo han logrado, pero en Voidwrought se siente bastante clónicamente bien. Es una base que le hace el carrito al juego para que puedas seguir adelante en los momentos en los que te tira para atrás.  - [b][u]Estética[/u][/b]: sin ser especialmente atrayente, con pocos recursos logra un aspecto visual resultón. Es un poco mas colorido que Hollow Knight y eso se agradece. Sin embargo algunos enemigos y animaciones se les nota la falta de presupuesto o interés para que queden como lo que planteaban.  - [b][u]Contenido[/u][/b]: si eres un fan del género y simplemente quieres un juego mas para tener unas 15-20hs explorando y combatiendo, el juego eso te lo da... no serán las horas mas divertidas ni sorprendentes, pero jugar un rato, vas a jugar y el juego tiene contenido suficiente como para rellenar eso.  [h3] Cosas malas [/h3]  - [b][u]Balance[/u][/b]: el primer enemigo al que te enfrentas es un zombie cortado por la mitad que se arrastra por el suelo... para poder matarlo con tu daño inicial necesitarás unos 6 o 7 golpes... sí así empieza el juego, empiezas a pensar cómo terminará... las primeras 4 o 5 horas de juego es muy tedioso tener que golpear tanto a los enemigos para matarlos, es absurdo y mucha gente ha dropeado el juego en estas horas iniciales. El tema es que el juego ya pasando la mitad, te da daño y vida suficiente como para tanquear todo sin mirar qué pasa. A esto se suma que al inicio el juego no te explica cómo mejorar el daño... he pasado casi hasta la mitad juego sin saber que necesitas resolver los puzzles circulares para eso. así hay muchas cosas que no tienen explicación interna como tal,   - [b][u]Mejoras[/u][/b]: casi todo el sistema de mejoras es un despropósito absoluto que carece de sentido, tanto por timing, como por mecánica. Es una mezcla sin mucha idea de lo que estaban haciendo. Muchas de las mejoras del juego están puestas en sitios extraños, o que simplemente no cuadran con el recorrido... a veces habrás matado a un boss importante sin obtener nada mas que una llave, y otras encontrarás el doble salto, solo caminando por allí. Hay mejoras que pisan a otras y hacen que estas carezcan de sentido, o llegan en un punto del juego en el que ya no las necesitas. En mi recorrido, he conseguido la mejora de "reptar" (que te haces una masa amorfa que anda por las paredes y techos), y luego de eso conseguí el salto en la pared y el doble salto... o sea... para ese momento, estas dos últimas no tenían sentido. Con las mejoras de daño pasa lo mismo: el juego no te dice que esos puzzles circulares son para mejorar el daño, ni el personaje recibe algún feeback visual que muestre que hace mas daño y te haga sentir mas poderoso. El gancho que podría haber sido divertido de usar, llega en el punto en que es apenas una llave. Además de esto, el Voidwrought copia el sistema de glifos de Hollow Knight (como no podía ser de otra forma), pero se olvida de copiar algo importante.. el sistema de muescas.... o sea... que solo podrás usar tres glifos en todo el juego... y no mas... y te advierto que el dash no tiene invulnerabilidad, así que de por sí ya tienes un glifo casi obligatorio. La curación solo te cura 3 puntos de vida sobre 15 que tendrás en total.... se repite el error de Blasphemous 1 aquí donde las curaciones casi no servían para nada. Hay una suma de despropósitos en este punto que hace pensar que si bien el estudio es pequeño, no han estudiado mucho el género, ni cómo se organiza el recorrido en un metroidvania.  - [b][u]Problemas mecánicos[/u][/b]: Si bien parten de una base buena, mas o menos bien emulada, los errores que comete el juego son suficientes como para destacarlos. Por empezar, cuando te golpean el personaje se transparenta... pero no un poco... se hace casi invisible. Por lo tanto en una pelea contra un boss, casi que pierdes de vista a tu personaje en el medio del spam que proponen algunos combates. El ataque cargado apenas si se siente como tal, da un impacto común y corriente como cualquier otro. Además pierdes la carga si te tocan, si haces dash, si haces el barrido, si usas el gancho o hasta si pasas de habitación... ni en Megaman, ni en ningún juego con ataque cargado pierdes la carga por cualquier cosa que pase, y aquí es realmente molesto intentar utilizarlo por esta razón. Si al cambiar de habitación usas el barrido para pasar por lugares estrechos, al iniciar la siguiente habitación tu personaje repetirá la animación... con lo cual si hay un enemigo allí, te comes el hit porque sí. Hay varios problemas mas, que si bien no son destacables, hacen una suma de cosas que complican el único punto que hace llevadero al juego  [h3] Conclusión [/h3]  No se trata mas que de un juego realizado por fans de Hollow Knight, que no terminaron de comprender el juego del que partían... de hecho dudo que hayan jugado muchos otros metroidvanias, porque se nota que no se daban mucha idea con la organización general. Tienen mérito en haber emulado de manera bastante cercana el feeling jugable. El lore es bastante inentendible y soso, pero misterioso como para que leas algo del compendio de personajes.  Resalto que no es un mal juego, y te puede entretener unas cuántas horas. Pero tienes que estar dispuesto a aguantar cosas bastante mal planteadas, solo por entretenerte, porque el juego casi nunca te sorprenderá con nada bueno de su parte, mas que lo que toma de Hollow Knight.

아트 스타일이 마음에 들었고, 공허라는 소재에 흥미가 있어서 구매했다. 우선은 환불했지만 추천을 준다. 위원회까지 클리어하고 리뷰 작성함.  환불한 이유는 1) 화면이 캐릭터 머리를 기준으로 움직이는지 방향 전환을 하거나 공격할 때, 또는 바닥을 기어다닐 때마다 화면이 움직여서 멀미가 난다. 이 때문에 보스 파이트에서 반대쪽 끝으로 이동하면 방이 전부 화면에 들어오지 않아, 보스가 보스의 공격이 시야에서 벗어나게 되는 점이 특히 불만이었다. 2) 작은 플랫폼의 경우, 제대로 밟지 못하고 떨어지는 경우가 있다.  이 두 가지가 매우 치명적이었다. 조작감이 미끄덩하고 빠른 건 하다보니 적응이 되었음. 하지만 화면이 계속 움직인 것은 불편했다. 그 외의 자잘한 불만은 사람에 따라서 참을 수도 있고 싫을 수도 있다고 본다. 개발자가 개선할 의지를 보이고 있기 때문에 나중에라도 다시 구매할 의향이 있다.  개인적으로 할나보다는 블라스퍼머스 느낌이 더 많이 나는 것 같다.  NPC가 돌을 만져보라고 하는데 길이 없으면 드래그 [spoiler] 하단치기로 빛나는 벽을 부수면 된다. [/spoiler]

Better-than-average Metroidvania with some flaws. Recommended - but with caution.   The game features neat cartoon-style art, responsive controls, a variety of movement mechanics, enjoyable skills, good music, polished menus, a large game world, and numerous hidden items to discover. While the levels and enemies are beautifully crafted, they lack memorability and occasionally feel a bit too generic, despite being technically well-executed. Searching for certain story items becomes tedious, especially in the late game when one traversal mechanic forces you to use [spoiler]a black sticky blob[/spoiler] form that moves at a snail’s pace. This form’s overuse adds frustration, and the fast-travel system could also be slightly improved.  Combat requires better balancing. Boss fights are particularly imbalanced—the early bosses feel disproportionately difficult, while later bosses become almost too easy if you collect upgrades. I read that some bosses received health increases after patching, which might exacerbate these issues. Additionally, many common enemies are excessively spongy, making late-game combat feel unnecessarily tiring.   The UI, menus, and map system work well overall but have room for improvement. For instance, it would be nice to zoom out fully on the map, have in-game menus remember their previous state, and maybe include a separate button for a quick access full-screen map.  The game runs smoothly, in both full-screen and windowed modes, works without issues with an Xbox controller. However, it lacks accessibility and difficulty settings, which could enhance the experience for a wider range of players. I would have appreciated at least some form of difficulty adjustment option. Save system is really nice - I really liked it, basically you don't lose any progress on death, you only have to backtrack.  The game draws inspiration from Lovecraftian horror themes, which add an eerie atmosphere. However, the protagonist and NPCs are not particularly memorable, as most are nightmare-like creatures. While this fits the story’s tone, it also makes the characters less engaging. The lore is presented in cryptic manner, with an abundance of text boxes you must click through. Important story choices are often buried within these texts, which made me do some accidental choices I didn't want.

This review will contain minor spoilers regarding bosses and abilities. Even with what I stated below, I do recommend this game as an entry level metroidvania.  [h2]Story[/h2] I'm going to be perfectly honest, I cannot tell you the story of this game, what's it about, or even what's the borderline "basic" of it is. I was so confused on how this game started story-wise that by an hour into the game I stopped caring.  [h2]Music[/h2] Same as above, I played this game in silence, I know bad on me, so I cannot tell you anything about the music. There was only one instance that I happened to have my sound on, which was when I was fighting some boss that looked like it was hit by a frying pan in [i]The Court[/i].  [h2]Gameplay (Including abilities)[/h2] Now we can get into the part I can tell you about. So, gameplay wise, it was smooth. I didn't want to throw my controller out the window like another indie game that involved a character named Bo. Overall the game was fun.  Difficulty wise, it did bounce all over the place. Certain bosses were a pain, and other bosses were too easy I honestly thought they were regular enemies before the [i]aberration slayed[/i] came on screen. The 3 main bosses of this game were a bit easy, as in I used two runes that made my attack increase by 4 times the amount and they were slain in about 3 minutes each, some less than that. I beat the final boss, whom I didn't know was the final boss, in about 2 minutes using the same tactics as the other 3 main bosses.   Exploration wise, I was confused. Extremely confused. Most of the hours you see on my account for this game are used trying to find where to go. I go so lost that I made it into to area that you weren't supposed to with only 4 abilities. Let me tell you the order I got the abilities: [list] [*] Adaptation [*] Augmented Cortex [*] Improved Ligaments [*] Unstable Nuclei [*] Entozoon Sac [*] Phased Flesh [*] Twitch Fibers [*] Hollow Cartilage  [*] Spurs [*] Awakened Arm [/list] Now, the reason I mentioned the order, is primarily because I don't believe that's the intended order to find those abilities. I managed to get through a good chunk of the game with only the first four abilities above. [i]The Unstable Nuclei[/i] is an amazing ability, too amazing to be exact. A lot of the platforming, puzzle, and other aspects of this game can be [b]completely[/b] avoided using that ability. Heck, I got through majority of the game because of it. I kill 2 out of the 3 main bosses before I even got another ability, and that was by chance.   Once I actually got all the abilities I noticed that there were all pretty useless at that point, because I've already explored all the maps and killed 12/14 extra bosses. That's when I decided to go and kill the final boss, which again I didn't know it was until I received the achievement [i]beat Voidwrought[/i].   [h2]Conclusion[/h2] All in all, the game was fun to play, even though it was a tad confusing. The mechanics were smooth, the art style was unique, and the abilities you gain were different than most metroidvanias out there. Exploration is a bit confusing at times, the bosses were mostly easy, and the game style was interesting.  Even with these criticism, I do recommend this game. For veterans of the metroidvania genre, you may not find it amusing, but I did have fun with it. Using all the skills I learned from games such as Sekiro, Hollow Knight, Nine Sols, and so on. This game was a amusing playground to test those skills out on new game.  [h2]True Ending[/h2]  So I'm not going to spoil the true ending, but there is one. I went back and did all the requirements to get the true ending. I will say it was extremely frustrating without having a clear indicator to what walls csn be broken, everything does look very same-y in this game, not going to lie.   However, I will say it only took me 2 attempts to beat the true ending boss. It wasn't hard. After gathering nearly every item and relic, the health bar was nearly halfway through the screen.   I will still recommend this game, but small note for the developers. Please add an item or feature that can tell you were items/secrets are, or at least how many are left per map (not region). I honestly almost changed my review to Not Recommend because how many times I had to sweep the same room in the course of a few hours, only to miss something every time.  [h2]Note for Developers[/h2]  Extra note for devs, I say this about every game. If I have to play through it more than once to get all achievements, not counting missable achievements, that is bad design. Missable achievements are fine, I play games all the time with those. Missable achievement push you to be detailed oriented, while FORCED replayablilty because you want to make choice X block achievement Y because...? You felt like it? Sure. Not gonna make me wanna play your game again. Missabled achievements are lessons learned and drive you to not make that mistake or overlook that detail  again. Achievements which are forcibly locked by making certain choices is not okay. That's my two cents, take it or leave.

Может, это фишка, чтобы не использовать мышь, но я такое не люблю. Типа, выполнять движения одного типа используя не WASD очень не привычно, можно поменять, но меня это просто смутило немного.  3 - Механика мобов (пока что стартовых, может дальше иначе) мне не понравилась. I noticed this game a few months prior to launch, just after I finished Hollow Knight, and was looking for something similar. I got immediately interested, because the artstyle looked really great, and gave off similar vibe to Hollow Knight's (while being absolutely different). Later I heard a lot of trash talking from some people who played the demo, and and while I was still going to buy the game from day 1, I started being a bit concerned. Needless to say, my expectations were exceeded by a lot, and the only reason why I didn't finish the game earlier is that I softlocked myself because of overlapping keybinds, and it took me a month to fix it. Now that I finished my first run, with true ending, I want to note some things that I liked, and that should be worked on in the future, but as always I will start with the good ones:  - Soundtrack of this game was an extreme banger, and I listen to it on daily basis even outside of playing the game (I would probably compare it even to my all time favourites, like Elden Ring, Dark Souls 3 or Witcher 3) - Artstyle is also stunning, and as I said before, while being reminiscent of Hollow Knight, it is actually pretty different and really refreshing - Exploration is extremely well done, and almost every single room hides some secrets, that feel rewarding to uncover - Boss quality is overall fine. Nothing peak, but except for one or two bosses I was genuinely having fun fighting them. The only thing I think should be improved is the difficulty curve, because while the first few bosses took me between 5 to 15 tries, I steamrolled all bosses after The Fermented (including it) - Most of the achievements don't require too much grind (I still have to complete the grindier ones, and some that can only be done on new playthrough, but it still isn't too much) - Puzzles are well made, and while even a drunk dumbass could solve them, they still feel fun. Really you just have to pay attention to details, and I like that very much  Now for the negatives: - The game doesn't support mouse, and I have no controller, so I had to experiment with the keybinds until I was comfortable with it (in the end I ended up with attack on '[' and jump on left arrow) - True final boss is quite disappointing, but I won't spoil more, so that you can discover why he is disappointing on your own - There are many things in the game that aren't explained in any way, and you have to figure them out on your own (I still don't know what half of the things do)  Anyway, I'd give this game solid 8/10, and if the devs rebalance some late game bosses to be harder, add mouse support and fix some bugs it might become even 9/10
