Hello everyone! Following my play-through of Voidwrought, I wanted to share my thoughts on it!  As always, a video review has been created, which you can watch by following this link: https://youtu.be/lwc3XcS1Hp0  For those who do not wish to watch the video:  Playtime: 8 hours and 30 minutes with one ending (an additional two to three hours for the second ending)  Completion Rate: 89%  Pros:  - The setting of Voidwrought is just drenched in Lovecraftian dread, featuring some of the most beautifully hand-drawn biomes I have ever seen in the genre, with each area evoking its own brand of degeneration and existential terror that results from the tainted touch of grand abominations beyond one’s understanding. Special note goes to the various characters that populate this sanity-shattering realm, each one standing out in terms of design and, at times, personality!  - The world is not only beautiful but also home to an ungodly amount of secrets and collectibles, from health and damage upgrades to powerful accessories.   - The game features a sort of base-building cult-themed mechanic with which you expand your home area piece by piece according to the number of followers and amount of Ichor you have at any given moment. By doing that, you open up new paths that may lead to collectibles, hidden areas as well as new NPCs that will offer assistance to you. I have to admit I truly enjoyed that aspect of Voidwrought and found myself constantly curious as to what sort of secret I would be discovering with my next expansion.  - The game’s map is very user-friendly and functional, with clear distinctions between biomes, proper marking of special rooms, such as save, fast-travel, ability acquisition and vault points, as well as, thankfully, the option to place your own manual markers of diverse visual styles, which was a life saver since Voidwrought’s exploration can be quite challenging, especially when it comes to finding certain hidden rooms concealed by breakable walls that can be tricky to spot. I didn’t get to find anything that allowed me to see collectibles on the map, so I highly recommend you start using the manual markers from early on.  - The game also features a very handy fast travel system that unlocks within the first couple of hours, which allows you to jump around the biomes via some special rooms. You can also unlock one more form of fast travel via the base-building system, through which you can use the game’s mirror save points to travel between the save point of your base and the last save point you used in the map, which can be helpful at times though I didn’t use it that much.  - Voidwrought is one of the most open-ended metroidvanias I have played recently, featuring an incredible degree of non-linearity, meaning that you can go about the discovery of the world by carving your own path to a large extent. That means you can find creative ways to access areas for which you may not have the proper ability to do so at that moment, for example by pogoing off enemies to reach high places even though you may not have the double jump or climb ability yet, something that offers an immense sense of freedom.  - Combat wise, I’m glad to report that Voidwrought tries to go the extra mile with significant success. On a basic level, you get a lightning-fast melee claw combo with which you can tear your enemies apart in satisfying fashion. On top of that, you also get the aforementioned relics, which bring a variety of different effects in combat, such as producing light beams that set your enemies on fire, augmenting your claw damage and throwing explosives, among others, effectively bringing ranged attacks into the mix. Of note here is the responsiveness and accuracy of the pogo mechanic, which I found incredibly useful against certain enemies. Healing is carried out by pressing down a specific button which allows you to channel your healing shards and restore your hit-points.  - Boss-wise, I’m beyond elated to say that the bosses I got to fight were not only visually stunning but incredibly fun to duke it out with as well, each one featuring a healthy range of attacks that kept things interesting and required good observation of certain animations that telegraphed each move-set in order to make sure you emerged victorious. Main bosses aside, there are also several optional big bads for you to discover and take down, which is a big plus.  - I do wish to mention one more thing that I truly enjoyed to see in Voidwrought in terms of quality-of-life functions for the players, and that is the region tab located in its menu. The reason I make special mention of this is because this tab actually shows you the completion progress you have in each separate area, which makes it easier for you to track your progress if you intend to go for 100%.  Cons:  - In terms of platforming, I didn’t really experience anything extraordinary here, though, to be honest, part of the reason for that was the aforementioned climb ability which, once acquired, pretty much makes traversal of the environment quite trivial and can be used in place of most other abilities, with very few exceptions. In essence, even though there were some acrobatic segments that would have been a bit challenging otherwise, they were made easy by that particular ability that had me jumping off walls like a madman without a care in the universe. I have mixed feelings in relation to that skill since, on the one hand, it made the whole world my playground but, on the other, it reduced platforming difficulty to nearly non-existent. That aside, I do have one more minor complaint here, and that has to do with an area called The Void. During the preview I made a month ago, I mentioned how platforming in that biome was a bit finicky due to the platforms being lined by eyes that didn’t match the actual circumference of the platform, which led to me constantly being unsure if I was landing on solid ground or not and resulted to a few falls that could have otherwise been avoided. In this final version of Voidwrought, The Void got much darker and visually rough, which made it even harder to know where you’ll be landing, though I will admit that the developers increased the number of platforms across it so, ironically, I didn’t fall to my death as much as I did previously. Still, I would have preferred a more visually clear area.  - I do feel the obligation to mention that Voidwrought features what I like to call cage-fights, which refers to the fact that, from time to time, you’ll get locked inside a room and have to fight a few waves of enemies before you can move forward, which I know is something that some people may not find appealing, though I didn’t mind it.  - Though I did enjoy the bosses, I did feel like their difficulty became progressively easier the more upgrades I acquired to the point where I could face-tank the attacks of most of them while dealing lots of damage with my basic combo as well as relics. I still had to be careful for a few of them, but nothing compared to the earlier boss-fights which could quickly end you if you weren’t careful.  - The overall feeling of gradual reduction of difficulty as I got progressively stronger kind of chipped away at the oppressive atmosphere of the game's world.  - I did not like the rotatory puzzles you have to solve in order to open the vaults. I consider them too complex and a momentum stopper.  All in all, a great addition to the genre!

Es ist vollbracht! Und selten hat mich ein Spiel gleich zu Beginn so aus dem Ring geworfen, wie Voidwrought. Es begann dabei eigentlich super. Stimmungsvolle Musik begleitete mich bereits im Hauptmenü und die ersten Schritte durch die düstere Welt voller Geheimnisse erinnerten direkt an Hollow Knight.  Überall gab es Stellen, an denen ich mit den anfänglichen Fähigkeiten nicht weiterkam, aber ich wusste, dass sich mit jedem kleinen Fortschritt die Welt weiter öffnen würde und hier haben die Entwickler einen fantastischen Job gemacht.   Doch bevor ich das zu schätzen lernte, kam ein Bruch, der mich beinahe dazu gebracht hätte, das Spiel zurückzugeben. Der zweite Bosskampf! Klar, in solchen Spielen ist es üblich, dass die Endgegner den Schwierigkeitsgrad maßgeblich bestimmen und man meist mehr drauf haben muss, als nur um sich zu schlagen. Der erste Boss in satten fünf(!) Phasen war ein gutes Beispiel. Klar lesbare Attacken, denen mit etwas Übung gut zu begegnen ist und notwendiger Konzentration und Geduld erzeugten eine Herausforderung, die sich jederzeit machbar anfühlte. Schwierig, ja, langwierig auch, aber schaffbar. Der zweite Boss hingegen war ein Unding. Er rast von einem Ende zum anderen der Arena, verteilt diverse Attacken und löst Fallen aus, die in Form von Speerspitzen aus dem Boden schnellen. Manche mit Lücken, manche auch über die ganze Fläche. Hinzu kommen Trashmobs in Form von Zombies und Hubschraubern, die zu viele Schläge einstecken, mich als Spieler ständig blockieren und somit ein Zufallselement darstellen, denn je nachdem, wo und wie viele gerade rumschwirren, geht es leichter oder schwieriger voran. Ich weiß natürlich, dass es meine persönliche Erfahrung ist und es auch Videos gibt, in denen es Leute ohne Schaden schaffen. Aber für mich war das für den Anfang eine Schippe zu viel, zumal ich nicht wusste, wo und wie ich mich noch hätte verbessern sollen(??). Da ich netterweise einen Kommentar der Entwickler bekam, habe ich es weiter versucht und mit etwas Unterstützung meines Juniors (deutlich routinierter in solchen Spielen) schließlich geschafft, jedoch auch nach mehreren Versuchen.  Verbesserungsvorschlag: Trashmobs weglassen oder zumindest die dämlichen Helikopter. Damit könnte man zumindest halbwegs die Kontrolle behalten.  Doch lassen wir das Negative hinter uns, denn dieser Boss war das Einzige, was mir nicht gefiel. Alles andere war hervorragend und ich hatte Spaß wie selten zuvor, die große verrätselte Welt zu erforschen und mich den verschiedenen Aufgaben zu stellen. Voidwrought orientiert sich stark an Hollow Knight, denn auch hier kann man Lebensenergie, Heilungen und Grundschaden permanent verstärken, muss dafür aber wirklich viele geheime Räume und Durchgänge finden, alte Maschinen in Gang setzen, Siegel brechen und immer wieder bereits besuchte Gebiete erneut aufsuchen, um mit neuen Fähigkeiten bisher unerreichbare Stellen zu erreichen.   Mit der Zeit kommt ein Schnellreisesystem dazu und für die Risikominimierung gibt es zahlreiche Checkpoints, an denen das Spiel gespeicher wird und an denen man wieder einsteigt, sollte man den zahlreichen Gefahren erliegen.   Der Kampf besteht wie bei Hollow Knight aus einer einzigen Hauptwaffe, die man verstärken kann und diversen Spezialgegenständen, von denen man zwei gleichzeitig ausrüsten und gegen eine Energieladung einsetzen kann. Die Energie lädt sich durch normale Attacken dann wieder auf.  Die Viefalt ist dabei enorm und sehr effektiv, wenn man etwas mit einem Laser den gesamten Raum vertiakl durchschneidet oder mit einem Feuerstrahl Gegnern lange anhaltenden Schaden zufügt. Doch auch passive Dinge kann man ausrüsten. Mehr Geld oder mehr Leben? Schnellere Heilung oder größere Reichweite beim Aufsammeln von Ressourcen? Man muss sich entscheiden, kann das aber jederzeit im Spiel tun.  Das Bewegungsspektrum hingegen ist dauerhaft abrufbar und erweitert sich stetig. Darunter sind Klassiker, wie der Doppelsprung (den man sehr spät bekommt) oder der Vorwärtsschub, aber auch ein Enterhaken oder die Möglichkeit, Maschinen zu besetzten und deren Funktion zu eigen zu machen oder sich an bestimmten Stellen durch Wände zu teleportieren.   Die weiteren Bosskämpfe verliefen fair. Das heißt nicht, dass es einfach war, doch es bewegte sich wieder in einem Modus des Lernens und Durchhaltens. Geflucht habe ich trotzdem noch einige Male, doch meist über meine Blödheit, weil ich immer wieder in dieselben Attacken gelaufen bin.  Am eindrucksvollsten fand ich den Astronom und den Fermentierten, da sie beide auch eher schnelle Reaktionen abfordern, aber eben nicht noch nebenher irgendwelche Störfaktoren eingebaut wurden. Doch jeder gewonnene Kampf fühlte sich gut an und manchmal wurde es richtig knapp und oftmals musste ich es ein paar Male versuchen. Das Debakel vom Anfang wiederholte sich aber nicht. Bei weitem nicht.   Ich habe nun das Ende erreicht, wobei ich nur die Hälfte der Errungenschaften erzielen konnte. Daher gehe ich davon aus, dass mir noch einige Dinge verborgen blieben. Das ist gut so, denn es ist ein guter Grund für eine zweite Reise.   Was hier wirklich fasziniert, ist der Weg zu den Zielen, denn vieles fühlt sich ultra-geheim an, muss aber entdeckt werden, um überhaupt weiterzukommen. Das habe ich bei all den anderen Metroidvanias die letzten Monate nicht so krass empfunden und das macht Voidwrought für mich einzigartig, da ich sowieso ein Fan von Geheimnissen bin.   Technisch ist das spiel sauber, die Grafik ist effektvoll und stilistisch mit Hollow Knight verwandt und der Soundtrack ist schlicht ein Meisterwerk. Manchmal melancholisch ruhig, manchmal treibend rockig, manchmal unheimlich und auch gerne mal episch.  Die Steuerung machte einen durchdachten Eindruck und ließ sich trotz anderer Tastenbelegung als gewohnt, prima erlernen.  Somit entschuldige ich mich für meine erste Rage-Review, denn alles, was nach dem Frust folgte, war fantastisch. Voidwrought hat sich nach der Anfangsschwierigkeit nun direkt in die Top 3 meiner Lieblings-Metroidvanias gemausert.

It's fine, not a bad game by any means. Music and art are great. Gameplay is very obviously inspired by Hollow Knight.  Game is quite short and quite easy, though. If you're into Metroidvanias for the map exploration and movement upgrades and collecting I would say it's a buy. If you want a brisk, competently done by the numbers metroidvania I'd also say buy.  If you're into them more for tension and satisfying combat I would say pass. The combat has promise but the game is laughably easy after like 2 or 3 bosses. If you want a game that's longer than ~13-15 hours, I'd pass as well (took me 10 to true ending, a couple of those hours was rescouring map to unlock the true ending).

[h1]碎碎有廿廿，蔫蔫又荟荟，一条大道通往昔[/h1]  一款无论形式还是内容上都做的不错的类银河战士恶魔城游戏，保留了一部分下劈让你知道致敬了先辈，有自己的内容和思考，但总体来说还需要优化和改进。首先想吐槽一下这个键位适配的问题，既然战斗和部分玩法都致敬前辈了为何不在键位上也致敬前辈做好适配呢，攻击键和跳跃键我都忍了，最起码确认键得是空格或者回车吧混蛋！（不要改E有bug）   其二是对难度的优化，明显有点过于受苦了，小怪血厚过头了三刀都砍不死，当然普通小怪只要一直连基本也碰不到我们，很想吐槽那个拿长矛的碰撞箱判定，戳出来后会停滞一段时间让人忍不住上去敲他两下，但是那货停留的矛尖还会保留伤害，反直觉搞死我好几次了。当然最最最恶心的还得是第一个逆天四阶段boss，说真的我无论玩哪个游戏打第一个boss时都没有这么绝望过，打死一阶段出第二阶段第二阶段打死想着说应该没了吧结果还有第三阶段，硬着头皮耗死了第三阶段结果发现BGM尚未结束，在存档点我反复反思自己是否是去打了个“大树守卫”在想回头跑路时发现回头路已经走不通了.......  其三是便利的问题，本作的地图不算特别绕，哪怕凭着直觉走也能走上大道，但是没有做到四通八达以及柳暗花明又一村，就会导致在想走回头路时会很长—————————————，没有传送点导致跑图很麻烦，硬伤中的硬伤，其次便是货币多爆率低、前期缺少革命性的正反馈等诸多毛病，总得来说并非全部照搬有自己的特色内容，但是实际体验仍然需要优化。  [quote] 执笔勾墨色，挥毫著文章；阳春布德泽，璞玉生光辉 欢迎关注[url=https://store.steampowered.com/curator/44174077/]吟遊诗人的手札[/url]，书游戏特色，写游戏魅力 [/quote]

Le jeu s'inspire complètement d'Hollow Knight et ne se le cache pas: on retrouve les mêmes mouvements, le même gameplay, une DA similaire (mais dans un univers différent) donc si vous avez joué à Hollow Knight vous ne serez pas perdu.  L'OST est franchement réussi, l'histoire est intrigante et donne envie d'explorer pour en apprendre plus sur l'univers.  On regrettera cependant certains ennemis qui sont de vrais sacs à PV, les ennemis classiques comme les mouches par exemple doivent prendre pas mal de coups avant de tomber et c'est assez frustrant quand il y en a 4 ou 5 à l'écran et qu'on passe 5 minutes à essayer de les tuer.  Globalement c'est du bon, j'avais un peu peur d'avoir une copie ratée d'Hollow Knight mais on a un jeu vraiment qualitatif qui a l'air riche en environnement et en contenus. Si vous avez aimé Hollow Knight vous aimerez sûrement ce jeu, il y a une démo disponible pour ceux qui se tâtent à l'acheter.  Je n'ai pas encore fini le jeu, donc je mettrai l'évaluation à jour si des modifications sont apportées au jeu ou si le jeu est finalement plus court que ce qu'il ne laisse penser.

《星穹遗迹》是一款充分致敬前辈《空洞骑士》，中规中矩的类银河城作品。能够看出开发团队在受到前辈影响的情况下，还有许多自己的巧思和设计，种类丰富的特殊能力体系搭配不错的地图设计功底，使得本作的探索体验优秀。 但战斗层面的薄弱以及一些缺乏人性化的设计可能让玩家的体验大打折扣，需要开发团队进一步优化改进。 不过在我个人十几个小时的游玩过程中，总体上还是非常愉快的。所以如果你是小骑士或者类银河恶魔城类型的忠实玩家，我还是会推荐你尝试本作，值得一玩。 但需要注意的是，不一定每个玩家都会喜欢它。 [h2]亮点 [/h2] ++很空洞骑士的体验 ++种类繁多的特殊能力 ++优秀的地图连通设计 +不错的美术和音乐 [h2]遗憾 [/h2] ---糟糕的遭遇战设计 --战斗难度的后继无力 --剧情上虎头蛇尾 -手感上缺乏打磨，需要适应

推荐，高素质的空洞骑士高仿，美术风格优秀。 目前还未通关，故事引人入胜。采取克苏鲁架构的故事风格，充满了紧张感和异教崇拜。 前期有点坐牢，技能多了之后好一点，制作组可能希望让玩家慢慢玩，打不过去就多找找路，但是第一个boss太坐牢了。 手感有些飘，熟练了之后还是比较爽的，动作上应该可挖掘的地方蛮多的。 地图上可探索的空间应该挺大的，可能有不同的技能获得顺序。 总体来说推荐，尤其是银河城的忠实玩家。

[h2]Коратка[/h2] Вполне себе приятненькая проходная метроидванька на 8-10 часов. Коротенькая, правда.  [h2]Каг абычна[/h2] - Верблюды идут на восток! - Что? - Слоны затаились на крыше! - У Вас всё в порядке? - Да что ж такое! Забыл пароль. Ладно, пришёл я за этим самым... Ну, Вы понимаете... - За чем? - Ну, Хорнет... Там... Шёлк там... Песнь ещё... Вторая часть как бы... - Песнь какая-то. Хор какой-то. У нас серьёзный здесь магазин с играми! Хотите оперу послушать - это вам в магазин с DVD фильмами... - Да нет же! Мне тут сказали, что у Вас появилась ранняя версия... Ну рыцаря. Метроидвания... - А! Метроидвания! Ни слова больше! Вот, возьмите Voidwrought! Отличная и главное свежая метроидвания! - Но причём тут Хорнет? - Как при чём? Посмотрите на эту картинку, на эти анимации. Возьмите в руки геймпад (на клаве не проверял - пишут, что не так всё хорошо). Как главный герой управляется? Отлично же! Надо брать! - Это по-моему не то, что я хотел... - Русский язык? Есть! Способности? Есть! Активных - 2 штуки. Система амулетов? Вот! Активных 3 штуки. Устроим тест драйв! - Ну как-то не особо способности и амулеты на геймплей влияет... Сюжет интересный? - Сюжет тут вроде бы есть. Но кто ищет в метроидвании сюжет? Прости, Шантей. Игроки ждут от метроидваний именно боёв с боссами - здесь такие бои имеются! - 11 штук? Малова-то... Да и странно как-то у меня при прохождении получилось - в начале боссы были прям сложные, но чем дальше, тем боссы становились проще. В финале же вообще боссов воспринимал как толстых врагов, не более.  - Главное же! В Voidwrought есть классический для метроидваний мир, который постепенно расширяется при получении способностей! Наматывать 10-й или даже 11-й круг по одним и тем же локациям - это ж так весело! - Ну на самом-то деле, искать тайнички со всевозможными бонусами было действительно занятно. - А ещё здесь! Вы не поверите! Двойной прыжок! - Ага, который появляется на 5-ми часу игры. Аккурат в середине.  Я уже начал было волноваться. - А это у нас такой платформинг! В меру сложный, есть где подумать иногда. Совсем упоротых мест нет. - Ну тут согласен. - Значит оформляем! С Вас 710 рублей. Наличные, карта? В кредит? Пакет нужен? - Т.е. Вы серьёзно считаете, что Voidwrought лучше творения Team Cherry на 160 рублей?  - А уже не важно, что я думаю. Вы дошли до финала во время нашего разговора. Да, игра коротенькая. И по условиям лицензионного соглашения назад пути уже нет. - Эх, жаль я пароль забыл... Не эту игру хотел, совсем не эту... - Ещё один довольный покупатель! Следующий! Касса свободна! Пока народу нет можно покурить... Кстати, пароль-то был Тетриандох... Никто запомнить не может... Пятый бедолага уже за сегодня. 14時間くらいプレイ。クリア済。 最初に一言、面白い！とても好みのゲームでした。 アクションはレスポンスも良く、進めるとできるアクションが増え自由に戦闘や探索ができて気持ちいい！ 逆にスキルを手に入れるまでは少し爽快感は少ないかもしれません。  他の方も言っている事なのですが、宝物庫を解除していかないと自分の火力がほぼ上げれないので敵が硬い＆稼ぎにくいと感じるかもしれません。(私は気になりませんでした。 火力を上げきってしまうと、ボスがかなり柔らかく感じたのでやりごたえが無くなるかもしれません。  アーティファクトの構成などで色々ビルドが組める感じもあります。 もちろん明らかに「コレ強いな」と思えるものもあります。 ですが、強いものを使わなくてもクリアできるので、エフェクトが好きなものだけを使ったりして各々の好きなものを使えるのはよかったです。  探索に関してですが...個人的にはかなり難しいです。(探索率、アイテムの入手数、撃破数などの表記はあるのですが) アイテムを探し出す(隠し通路を見つけたり)のが私はかなり苦戦しました。  ところどころバグがあるのですが...どうにかなるものなのでとりあえず良しとしました。 (色々と重なるとかなりもやっとするかもしれませんが...  敵のラッシュみたいなのがあり、その時部屋が閉じて壁がおりてくるのですが音でラッシュだと判断でき壁がおりてくる前に外に出ら れます。 でると敵を倒さないと壁が無くならないので場所やスキルによっては積んでしまいます。  一周で実績解除はできないと思われます。  デザイン面はダークな感じや少々気味の悪い敵、登場人物でデザインされていました。 好きな方(クトゥルフとか)はかなり好きな感じに思えます。 (もう少しエログロ可愛い感じも取り入れても良いかもなんて思たっり...) 中々に硬派にダークな感じでデザインされている印象です。 絵のタッチはHollow Knightに少し近いものを感じます。ゲーム面も少し似てる感じかもです。  総評としては私はおすすめします！ (とても面白かったので長々となってしまいました。長文失礼いたします。

To get this out of the way, Voidwrought is very much inspired by Hollow Knight in almost all aspects of its gameplay though it manages to carve out a unique visual identity of its own (despite having a very similar style) and the overall mood feels strangely both similar and very different. You play a character that feels like a copy-cat of the main protagonist from Dead Cells going through a world eerily mirroring the decaying insect kingdoms of Hollow Knight, only here it's pyramids, zombies, and a very strong Lovecraftian vibe with you on the side of the monsters.  As the game can't escape comparison to Hollow Knight, the brass tacks are that the game is around 10 to 12 hours long (took me 13 though I'm a slow player), the only difficult bosses are the first two, with the biggest spike the second one. After that, you collect so many skills, power upgrades, and health upgrades that you likely will beat every boss on the first try with the final boss almost comically weak by the time you reach him. Not sure whether that was just an overall bad balancing job or they didn't care anymore making them difficult enough (visually they all look gorgeous though).    Plot is dished out like in all Soulslike-inspired games via cryptic item descriptions or opaque diatribes from the various NPCs you meet. The game is very generous upon death, whatever you've collected and explored on the map will remain even after spawning at the latest safe points, so in theory, you can do a death run but given the low difficulty you don't really need to. There's lots of stuff to buy (relics are subweapons/spells, crests are power-ups) and various things to do. Even after finishing the game, I know based on the achievements I'm still missing at least one region and 2 or 3 bosses.  This is really the biggest draw of the game. Exploration is king here and unlike most metroidvania, you can easily bypass bosses and just go spelunking, though with the adverse effect that by the time you get back to the boss you bypassed you're likely so overpowered he's a complete pushover. Still, every new area feels distinct, with its own set of enemies, and it's fun going around and just mapping out the game.  Still, I hope they either patch the bosses somewhat or introduce a hard mode to make this more of a challenge compared to the joke that difficulty is at the moment.

A solid game worth your time. I had a lot of fun with Voidwrought - it's beautiful, plays well, and has a great sense of exploration. I think its nonlinearity is a bit detrimental though, I was quite far into the game when I acquired what felt to be some of the core abilities.  Player movement feels mostly good, although you do retain a bit of momentum before coming to a stop making it feel a bit "slippery" at times, especially when trying to land on a single tile block. I did get used to it pretty quickly though, it wasn't really an issue after the first hour or so of play. Speaking of movement, I found this game's version of [spoiler] the morph ball [/spoiler], although creative and interesting, to be painfully sluggish. I understand the intent with this ability from its visuals, but it was so cumbersome to move in this form I dreaded every time I had to use it. I really think it could be 2x the speed with half the acceleration and deceleration times.  Hitboxes and hurtboxes seem fine on regular enemies but don't seem to line up with the sprites on some of the bigger bosses. At times it felt like a bosses hurtbox disappears entirely when it transitions from one attack to another, meaning if you're pogoing attacks you just fall into the boss and take damage (even though your attack sprite was well and truly in front of the boss sprite). The bosses go from challenging at the beginning of the game to extremely easy once you have some attack and health upgrades - the attack upgrades felt appropriate for regular enemies though as they are pretty spongy at base damage.  A final and very minor gripe is the movement speed that the currencies fly towards the player. You can outrun these, and often I found myself waiting at each rooms exit waiting for the money to catch up with the player so I don't miss out on it. It's only for a second or two of course, but when it is every single room you definitely notice it.  Those points aside, the pros vastly outweigh the cons with this game and I recommend Voidwrought. Smacking stuff is satisfying, the visuals and sound design are top notch, and it is a well crafted game.

Another thing that annoyed me during my playthrough was that i didn't really know what i was giving to the npcs when they asked for something, there is no indicator of what item the npcs are taking just what they say on their dialogs refering what they want. 自分は楽しむことができたけど改善すべきところも多く、万人が楽しめるゲームではないと感じました。  個人的に収集要素が好きなので、どこへ行っても何かしらのアイテムが落ちているのが良かったです。  また、敵の動きが特徴的で、対処の仕方が分かりやすい敵が多かったのも楽しさに繋がっていると思います。  改善したほうがいい点は結構あって、まず序盤の敵が硬すぎるのは何とかした方がいいですね。 はじめに出会う敵は、他のゲームでは1〜3発で倒せることが多いですが、このゲームは5発殴らないと倒せない。爽快感に欠けています。 最終的には1発で倒せてサクサク進めるようになるのですが、あのサクサク感は序盤から必要だと思いました。  次に、回避の反応が悪いです。 序盤は特に回避が重要なのですが、攻撃中に反応してくれないことがしばしば。結構ストレスを感じました。  最後に、マップが不親切です。 NPCや虚無ゲートの位置、能力で壊す壁など、あらゆるものがマップ上に残らないためとても不便です。 途中からピン機能を使いましたが、ピンの種類が少ないのでカバーしきれずストレスでした。  他にもありますが長くなるのでここまで。メトロイドヴァニアの良いところも悪いところも入ってる作品です。

It's a pretty decent metroidvania with some caveats:    -After the first first few boss fights you'll be so powerful that you'll just face-tank every boss (I killed the final boss on my first try, with 90% of my health still remaining..), the game would be a lot better if it was more difficult.  *EDIT* - [i]Apparently they attempted to address the difficulty, resulting in spongey bosses, so it sounds like one way or another this aspect of the game is going to be a weak point.[/i]  -On the one hand, you've got a lot of non-linearity for a metroidvania which is great, however one ability in particular make others [b]mostly[/b] irrelevant.  It was the third or fourth ability I found and it opened up most of the game.  ..So later when I got some of the more standard movement upgrades they were not very impactful because their role had been fulfilled with this better ability.    -[spoiler]The transformation ability you pick up acts a bit like the Morph Ball in the Metroid games.  But it is slower than that, which makes it comparatively cumbersome.[/spoiler]  -To obtain the big damage upgrades you are required to do some logic puzzles, which is not the sort of thing I like in videogames.  I was able to fiddle them for 15 minutes and eventually get lucky to solve them, but I can see someone else just throwing in the towel out of annoyance.  It's always dangerous to insert that sort of thing into a different genre.. People buy this seeking a metroidvania, not a puzzle game.  The music was a bit light/soft but it added a nice ambience and did actually contain some metal.  My favourite piece sounded very different to the others; The market area sounds lively and like it came straight out of Blasphemous 2.
This is one of the most amazing games I've ever played! totally recommended

Второе, это отсутствие как такого челенджа, а его очень хочется, hk да и тот же grime приучили меня к тому что он есть, а тут боссов лупишь в зажим и платформенные отрезки слишком короткие, это очень не хорошо. Из этого вытекает третье - мир ощущается крохотным, я это понял когда начал сравнивать ощущение даже не с hk, ведь никто не может сравниться с несравненным, а с grime, в grime было это, ощущение маштаба, мир казался огроменным и это восхитительно, у меня есть несколько предположений почему так вышло, но все эти мысли связанны с разработкой, сроками и амбициями, в общем буду надеяться что игру допилят с помощью длц или еще чего нибудь. И что через пару лет, я вернусь в игру и буду приятно удивлён. Далее четвёртое - ужасные хитбоксы на первом боссе, но я думаю что сверху, об этом уже вкурсе, НО не только он был с ужасными хитбоксами, в игре присутствует жук, с которого капает зелёная кислота и вот эта кислота, действительно сломана, она уже испарилась, но всё равно дамажит - это ужас просто, общались на этот счет с другом и сошлись во мнение, что этот жук сломан Пятое - очень неприятный и громкий звук, похожий на звук "курка" в самом конце загрузки игры из главного меню. Шестое, это моя глупость, но всё же, местами очень не очевидные места в полу которые можно сломать, в моменте даже пришлось воспользоваться видео прохождением что бы понять, а куда собственно идти. Но это я еще готов списать на свою глупость. Седьмое, всё таки для меня больше минус - это слишком сильная способность превращения, которая просто делает не нужными целый ряд способностей, теже крюки - просто не нужны, хотя их вполне можно было бы хорошо реализовать, достаточно посмотреть в сторону "grime" и сделать какое то количество мест куда без них просто не попасть. Восьмое - нет возможности выйти из диалога кнопкой, если нажал на нпс придётся проскипывать речь что бы выйти Девятое - не нравиться то что кнопки хила каждый раз надо перенажимать и нельзя просто зажать и отхилить всё, пусть и со стандартным кд Десятое(забыл сразу написать) - не детализированная карта как в blasphemous, хотелось бы чего то получше, как в grime или last faith Из минусов вроде всё и самый критичный - это конечно же отсутствие челенджа, как с боссами так и с испытаниями. Заключение - в конце хочу сказать, что игра мне в принципе то понравилась, задушиться я не успел, так же желаю разработчикам успеха и надеюсь со временем они хорошенько допилят игру, либо же учтут все недочёты в сиквиле или следующем проекте. Спасибо за игру и хотелось бы увидеть от вас еще более годную/прокаченную метроидванию

I just about 99% Voidwrought recently. (missing one item on the map because of tedious collectable tracking + extremely hidden breakable walls) A very good attempt of Hollow Knight wannable Metrodvania. The art and map design is good overall, but there is some pacing issue + frustration in the beginning~ mid point of the game.  One core issue of this game is it's TOO OPEN in the beginning of the game.  A good Metrodvania , like Hollow Knight, will start the game with a semi linear progression path , and branch out to open world AFTER they give you sufficient tools to traverse the world.  In Hollow Knight's case, the normal pathing you'd take is Green Path -> Mentus Lord , which grant you access to air dash and wall climb . Unless you do some sequence break via poggling , you're are pretty much blocked off venturing into other Biomes UNTIL you go through Green Path and Mentus Lord. Dev make sure you have means  to traverse the rest of the world.  Voidwrath does not do that.  Around 80% of the crucial traversal methods are either optional unlockable , or hidden at the edge of the world . I literally get Wall Climb and Double Jump after I already explored like 95% of the world already.  This makes my whole exploration a tedium to get through obstacles because I simply don't have the tools to traverse the world, and lead to lots of needless backtracking. Sometimes I venture into couple biomes without any means to progress and have to just turn back multiple times.  Also bosses become too easy later on because our weapon upgrade is too op and our character has way too much health.  Healing is kinda weak in this game because you can't replenish the flask easily , and they don't heal much, so in return , dev just gives you so much health you can pretty much trade blows with bosses until they die , which take away all the challenge in the game.  Some other minor issues including way too many extremely hidden breakable walls at very random spot with zero hint makes 100% completion super tedious .   Charms locking to 3 without any method to expand the slot kills the diversity of the build . We should be able to expand the charm slot later on, but given the bosses are so weak, maybe that's not very necessary, but nevertheless a wasted potential . Relic has some balancing issues too, with around 20% very good and 80% completely pointless. Also no means to upgrade the damage of the relics too = another wasted potential.   Overall this game gives me an enjoyable 20 hrs~ adventure , but with some tweaking and polishing, it could have been so much more.

[quote]关注[url= https://store.steampowered.com/curator/36224058/]老实人鉴赏家 [/url]  [url= https://steamcommunity.com/groups/laoshirenLSR]老实人评测[/url]：独立游戏爱好者聚集地，黄油绅士交流中心。  不错过游戏的亮点，不放过游戏的槽点，带给玩家最直观的测评感受。[/quote]  《星穹遗迹》是一款手绘2D风格的类银河战士恶魔城游戏，你需要扮演探险者去击败遗迹中的众神。   [h1] 浓浓的“空洞味” [/h1] 游戏上手的第一感觉，就是和《空洞骑士》实在是太像了，无论是主角的模型动作还是各种动态效果，确实一股浓浓的“空洞味”！首先是美术，因为大部分是手绘的背景，所以整体暗黑风格的质感还是非常好，还有一些能够进行交互的场景，美术这一部分至少是及格线以上了。  说到交互，游戏的作者真的是非常爱下劈这个动作设计啊，不仅很多怪物需要下劈才能攻击，还有很多隐藏的场景需要你熟练运用下劈才能过去。没错，至少在我游玩的这几个小时里，下劈这个动作贯穿了地图的大部分设计。真的是看得出设计师对于下劈这个动作的情有独钟，虽然但是，有些地方下劈进入的难度确实是太大了（可能后面学到的技能可以帮助你更容易地进入隐藏区域）。  [h1] 不错的地图设计 [/h1] 而地图设计呢，我觉得这一部分内容做的还是挺丰富的，作为一款类恶魔城游戏，我觉得地图探索是非常重要的一部分。而游戏同样也设定了一些隐藏区域和一些隐藏的道具，例如你一开始就能学会的滑铲，后面学到的攀附、二段跳等等，这些都能帮你到达第一次你没法到达的地方，增强了重复探索的乐趣。还有游戏的大部分遗物都得在地图中收集，只有少部分能在商人手中购买。而且游戏里还设计了不少跑酷的关卡，虽然玩起来也有”熟悉的感觉“，但战斗间穿插着跑酷和解谜的部分也不错。  [h1] 潦草的战斗手感 [/h1] 从地图探索的来看是相当不错的，但同样的，游戏也存在的很多的问题。首先最大的就是手感，影响手感的因素有很多，包括游戏的视角移动、美术的流畅度、打击感等等，我感觉目前游戏影响手感最大的问题可能在于动作模型，无论是攻击还是滑行，都感觉有种飘飘的感觉。这是什么感觉的，就感觉主角的模型在一层“油”上滑行。  第二就是怪物的设计，初期的小怪真的是太多了，不仅是密度大而且血也贼厚（谁家设计师在BOSS房里还要塞点小怪啊！），难度设计的平衡性也太不合理的。而且这也反应出了一个严重的问题，作为一个平A作为主要输出的游戏，攻击手段实在是太过单一了。至少对于普通玩家来说，游戏前期的难度实在是太不合理了，充满恶意的怪物难度加上需要大量下劈的才能进入的隐藏区域，确实会劝退大部分的玩家。   [h1] 值得一玩 [/h1] 总之，这是一款画面、音乐中规中矩，探索不错，战斗略显潦草，综合来说值得一玩的类银河城游戏。

已改好评，游戏质量还是很不错的，就是游戏难度和上手门槛有点高开低走了，前期boss阶段和血量过多，再加上前期角色性能很低又强制搞机制boss（下劈）手感一时间又难以适应导致体验糟糕，越到后面解锁更多黑科技能力和道具越发觉得好玩，制作组其实挺用心的，游戏内容也很丰富，还有内置怪物图鉴，一些地图和机制的设计也很新颖，比如基地不是一个一成不变的场所而是随着进度会不断扩展的区域，很喜欢这种设计，但是游戏对难度把控有点失衡吧，如果想做硬核类型门而设置极具门槛的boss后又因为能力提升导致挑战性降低，又有点自相矛盾，前面像魂后面就像银河城式的战斗了，希望制作组能吸取经验，本作不乏是一款优秀的银河城作品，如果只是让玩家浅尝辄止就太过可惜了 ----------------------------------------差评分割线--------------------------------------------------------- 1个boss做100个阶段真是天才设计，还有主角那么短的的手就不要强制搞你那下劈了

这是一款类空洞骑士这横板动作游戏。  游戏的画面是手绘风格，攻击方式多样，怪物种类也比较多，总体来说看着还是比较舒服的。  剧情没太看懂，又是神，又是地下城市遗迹，又是畸变怪物什么的，中间还有谜语人，总之玩家要做的就是探索地图，打败boss。  玩法上是横板动作射击，地图的设计是类银河恶魔城的，玩的时候我感觉总体手感还行，但是部分地方差点意思。  攻击敌人玩家会后退这都是小问题，稍微大一点的问题就是玩家在普攻的时候既不能冲刺也不能跳跃，所以就感觉不够灵活，后续能不能改一下，最少让冲刺的优先度高于攻击，让玩家能在攻击贪刀的时候看到敌人攻击可以冲刺进行闪避，不至于因为普攻硬直躲都躲不掉。  而且冲刺貌似有内置CD，这都让手感差了点意思。  不过和空洞骑士一样可以下劈跳高，还能下劈过尖刺，这个好欸。  角色性能是根据流程和探索地图慢慢变强，一开始只有普攻，随着探索，跳跳乐和boss战，能够解锁新的技能，被动以及能力--比如冲刺，二段跳和爬墙之类的，都是需要boss战才能获得的。  技能和被动都需要装备之后才能使用，比如说冲刺的时候无敌就是个被动。  被动和技能都有各自的槽位，种类数量还是挺多的，不过槽位数量有限，所以装备的时候需要进行一下取舍。  难度还是有一些的，小怪还好说，boss战的体验略微游戏奇怪，游戏设计的是碰到怪物身上角色就会掉血，地上走的boss还好，空中飞的boss，由于要跳起来攻击，就不太好打。  而且某些boss碰到它有的部位会掉血，有的部位就不会，就感觉稍微有点迷。  总体来说还是需要熟悉boss的招式才能过，还是挺有挑战性的。  没有标EA，内容应该算是完整的了，体量也不错，还是挺有意思的。如果喜欢空洞骑士的话，那本作当成代餐绝对合格了，推荐尝试。 [quote]欢迎关注我们的鉴赏家:[url=https://store.steampowered.com/curator/35402982/]走心评测[/url] 遵从内心，写出真实感受 欢迎加入我们Steam组:[url=https://steamcommunity.com/groups/ZXPC]走心评测[/url] 点击加入，一起探索游戏的乐趣 喜欢这篇评论，也麻烦点个赞，让更多人能够看到！[/quote]

[h1]《Voidwrought》：此“空洞”非彼“空洞”[/h1] 《Voidwrought》作为一款与《空洞骑士》风格相似的 2D 动作冒险游戏，在游戏机制与玩法自然有一些借鉴，当然也有自己的创新之处。相似的是，玩家在本作仍需通过跳跃、攻击和冲刺来面对敌人波涛汹涌般的攻击。不同的是，本作中操控人物所具有的滑行能力不禁令人眼前一亮，玩家可以凭此通过狭窄的通道和躲避敌人的攻击。  此外，从游戏的文本对话和画面风格上可明显看出制作组试图构建一个庞大、复杂的地下世界，但在地图设计方面稍显不足，不仅一些关卡的布局和设计略显单调，而且本作还存在技能逐渐解锁的机制，尤其是冲刺和抓墙这两个比较重要的玩法在本作前期并不能使用，令玩家在初期探索时频繁遇到许多无法通过的障碍，严重影响了部分玩家的游戏体验。至少在Demo时期，玩家进入游戏是直接可以解锁这些技能。或许这是制作组考虑扩充游戏内容而刻意为之，但笔者认为一些比较基础、通用的技能直接解锁就可以，根本没有必要逐步解锁，类似滑行等比较有特点的技能倒是可以通过玩家推进地图再逐步解锁。  同时，早期技能逐步解锁的设定也导致玩家探索时的不可逆性。由于一些技能在前期未解锁，因此玩家可能走错一个区域就无法返回上一个区域。再加上本作的地图传送点比较少且前期也未解锁，不仅增加了玩家重复不必要探索的时间，而且很可能玩家尚未做好准备就不小心进入BOOS战区域，然后无法返回其它区域进行探索，从而只能重开存档。不过相比于Demo，正式版中的地图标记细化许多，提供了更多类型的标记，使得游戏的探索体验将会更加顺畅。  本作与《空洞骑士》相似的游戏风格特点使得玩家将本作与《空洞骑士》对比在所难免。目前来看，本作与《空洞骑士》还存在一定的差距，打击感和操作引导的不足限制了它的整体表现，但整体表现依然可圈可点。坦白来说，从Demo试玩来看本作的玩法真的很不错。但前期的技能限制，可能会影响玩家进一步游玩本作的兴致。希望未来制作组能进一步优化和完善，为玩家提供了更加流畅和丰富的游戏体验。在等待“丝之歌”发售这漫长的时间里体验一下本作，也未必不是一段全新的体验，值得一试。  [code] 点击关注[url=https://store.steampowered.com/curator/26214428] 電玩狂熱愛好者 [/url] ，以获取更多游戏评测、资讯、攻略 [/code]

[h1][b]Review[/b][/h1]  [b][i]Voidwrought[/i][/b] is rich; aesthetics, soundtrack, combat, exploration, and overall gamefeel are superb. It delivers almost everything in such an exquisite way that makes the whole experience remarkable and fulfilling.  [h3][b]The Highlights[/b][/h3]  [list] [*]The aesthetics are captivating; it reminded me of Egyptian aesthetics, and the game confining a place called "The Gray Pyramid" is no coincidence. Besides that, the protagonist (and all other beings) have this thick black outline; it stands out and combines very well with the overall aesthetics. Enemy designs as well are pleasing to see, especially the bosses.  [*]The music is spectacular; some are very atmospheric, eerie, and desolate. Several tracks have this middle eastern vibe, which blends well with the aesthetics. But a major highlight is that the soundtrack features electric guitars, and the tracks they're used on are INSANE. [b]The Eternal Engine[/b] track is unbelievable; it sounds just like a heavy metal song, and the intensity is out of the charts, jeez! I wish more Metroidvanias featured tracks with guitars and more contemporary instruments.  [*]I've seen some people claim that the movement feels floaty, but IMO the movement feels very tight; it's precise and responsive; it feels good to move around. Not only that, but with each ability you get, the more movement freedom you experience; at the endgame, you basically swift through the areas. And the Unstable Nuclei is pretty sleek; I would even dare to say it's a better iteration of the Morph Ball.  [*]I love that the game has a bestiary, but not only that, it also features lots of things like characters you met, areas you explored, bosses, lore, and abilities. The game provides written details for each element. Also, each area in the menu shows the map percentage and what is available to collect, so it's easy to see what's missing.  [*]Relics are very distinct and very fun to use. It's safe to say that players will lock into a specific build and play like that until the end, but I enjoy the difference between them that gives so much diversity in playstyle.  [*]It not only has relics that are part of the offensive, but it also features the charm system of [b][i]Hollow Knight[/i][/b] (always a pleasure to see games implementing it) with items called "souls", which gives you passive bonuses.  [*]The game has LOTS of secrets; the quantity is nothing less than remarkable, as it provides that sense of exploration that is so important in Metroidvanias, but also (almost all) the secrets aren't sordid or cheap; pretty much all of them are rewarding to find. However, it's essential to say that the game gives more items than are needed to complete certain quests.  [*]The game's length is also a big highlight, as it has lots of content to be experienced. I finished with 100% in 23 hours, but I take my time to play, so you can probably finish with less and even fewer hours just doing the default ending. But more importantly, it's a game that pulls you in and gives you that feeling of wanting to play more. It's worth every penny. [/list]  [h3][b]The Drawbacks[/b][/h3]  [list] [*]The major—and I would say—the only significant downside is: It gets VERY easy. After you gather more upgrades, you become so strong that nothing stands in your way. I think after I beat the second boss, all the other ones (save for one) I beat in my first try, including the true final boss. Hell, I even avoid killing one boss too quick because I was vibing so much to the soundtrack. But I must give praise when it's deserved: the second boss was insane! Such a good fight, and featuring probably the best track in the game. [b]Edit:[/b] The developers made a patch about the issues I mentioned above and other QoL things. I haven't played it again, so I can't say if it's better or it's fixed. If you want to see the changelogs, you can find the [url=https://steamcommunity.com/games/2014550/announcements/detail/7119722540407193659]patch here[/url].  [*]There are a few small visual nuisances that I came across playing it, but it's so minor that it's barely noteworthy. [/list]  [h3][b]The Neutral Ground[/b][/h3]  [list] [*]A "bug" occurred to me, and one item simply disappeared from my inventory. I talked to the devs, and they said it actually is supposed to be like that; I didn't believe it at first, but after I talked to other people, it seems like that is really the case. When this happened, I thought my save was doomed and almost discouraged me from getting 100%. In the end, it all worked out, but it was such a weird situation. [/list]  [h3][b]Conclusion:[/b][/h3]  [b][i]Voidwrought[/i][/b] is a gorgeous Metroidvania, with such well-crafted aesthetics, alongside an electrifying soundtrack at times, which makes your gameplay feel absorbing and does not let you go. The exploration is excellent; having you find so many secrets keeps the player's attention sharp, and it's engaging. Combat-wise is fun; it's nice to have so many ways to defeat monsters, but it's a shame that it's not challenging enough, as it becomes easy after progression. But the predominant feeling that remains is of satisfaction; it lingers with you through the whole journey you followed, and it is no overstatement to say it's a memorable Metroidvania game. Given ALL that, the score has to be:  [h2][b]9/10[/b][/h2]

本来是不想推荐的，但是这个是活动获得内测KEY，就不好意思写不推荐了。 1不知道为什么这个游戏不支持我的手柄（盖世小鸡G4Pro）有线连接，其他游戏都行，但是这个游戏不行，但是换个手柄（北通阿修罗）又可以，只能说山寨低价手柄的受害者。 2游戏玩起来但是中规中矩的类银河游戏，但是也遇到了其他评论说的走起路来脚底抹油的操作感觉。还有打怪有反作用力也有点难受，在一些平台边缘有时候打着打着因为反作用力掉下来了。 3有些boss实在是太难，第二个BOSS个人感觉实在太难 4通关了，总的流程并不长。

《星穹遗迹》是一款类银河战士恶魔城游戏，第一次见到这款游戏时，就明显感觉到一股《空洞骑士》的味道。在上手体验后，就可以肯定，这款游戏狠狠地致敬了《空洞骑士》，对于《空洞骑士》的粉丝来说，这款游戏可以当一波代餐，但是对于类银游戏的新玩家来说，就有点难受了。  首先是游戏的美术方面，制作组算是下来不小的功夫，每一张大地图都有着独特的风格，而且在背景的制作上也不含糊，能看到很多细节，可以感受到美术资源的丰富。游戏的整体色调偏灰暗，可能是为了契合游戏末日的背景，因此也很难看到什么美丽景色，不过地表那颗红星还是挺有压迫感的。  游戏中怪物的血量比较厚，即使是小怪，也要砍上好几刀才能解决掉，这让开荒的压力有点打。加之游戏的闪避功能用这有点难受，每次想用闪避功能越过骷髅兵，每一次成功。还有一点，当玩家对小怪进行攻击时，小怪并不会硬直，也不被击退，这就让在某些场合下战斗非常的难受。不过好在游戏没有死亡惩罚，死了不会掉落任何东西。只是从存档点复活。  游戏在BOSS方面有着些挑战性。首先第一个BOSS，上来就小小的上一波强度，直接整一手四阶段，加上游戏中没有血条的设定，打的时候还是有一点绝望的。不过好在第一个BOSS的攻击欲望不高，也十分的有规律，摸清规律后就好打了。第二个BOSS就有些让人难受了，需要玩家熟练掌握下劈不说，还会招一堆小怪，每次清这些血量高的小怪的时候，还容易被BOSS打，十分考验玩家的能力。  虽说游戏中敌人的小怪血厚，BOSS难度有点高，不过战斗的打击感和音效做的不错，给人的反馈很好。然后就是游戏的地图设计，个人认为还不错，给人很强的探索欲望，存档点的分布也挺合理的。就是非常进出地图，敌人就会刷新这一点，对于我这种爱跑图的不是很友好。  和《空洞骑士》一样，游戏中下劈非常重要，无论是用来躲避敌人的攻击，还是前往高处，都需要熟练运用下劈，对于新手来说可能需要多加练习。此外，游戏的移动手感有点太滑了，跳跃时经常会跳过，停不住，这一点需要适应一下。  《星穹遗迹》作为一款普通类银来说，可能比较中规中矩，但是如果你是一位等待着《丝之歌》发售的玩家，那值得来体验一下。 [quote] 妙笔提手点墨花，妙语成章满纸纱。 欢迎关注[url=https://store.steampowered.com/curator/44852326/]猪式会社[/url]，用心绘测游戏。[/quote]
探索要素強めなホロウナイトライクなメトロイドヴァニア。 実績コンプは1周では不可能。 隠し通路等が非常に多く、全てを回収するのは結構大変。 戦闘面は簡単に感じました。 攻撃力を一時的に上げるレリックを使用してゴリ押しでも充分に攻略可能。

I need to do a review of this game early because I didn't expect to be this good. The music is awesome, I didn't expect this quality of soundtrack in this game, I love it. The combat early game feels like you don't do damage or that the enemies have a lot of health but later in the game you get upgrades that improve this issue. Also after these upgrades I realize that If I would have had this before I wouldn't have even been able to see the enemies moveset which would have been a shame. Besides all the areas are different from each other and the enemies in them are so unique.   For now I think this game is so worth it for the price, you should give it a try.  Edit : I finished the game recently and I have a big complain, the bosses. It’s not that they are easy is more like the don’t have enough health to endure more than 15 seconds ( this happens with the majority of bosses however there are bosses that have enough health ) maybe if they buff their health it will be better.  I have one more thing that isn't essential but would make the game feel smoother. I would like them to change the pins, I feel like they are too specific, I would prefer dots with different colors to be more general and assign each one to an upgrade. I also need more of them, there aren't enough for all the upgrade paths.

Общие моменты: 1. Не сложная. В начале были неудобства, но только из-за того что механики типо рывка/двойного прыжка не открыты, и хила мало. 2. Здоровья больше чем хилок(восстанавливаются на сейфлоках). Всего в игре может быть до 5 хилок по 3хп каждая, когда общее хп в конце около 15. хотя иногда можно найти на локации одноразовый хил но пользы от него почти не было. 3. Музыка. Хорошая, но не на столько что хочется переслушивать её как в том же Hollow Knight. 4. Боссы. Всего в игре может около 12ти считая не сюжетных, атаки их простенькие, длительность боёв была тоже приемлемая(то есть боссы не отваливались за секунд 40-60). 5. Экономика игры. Тут всего 2 торговца, и количество валюты которое они просят за товары просто нечеловеческое, даже пробегав 90% игры с пассивкой на доп дроп + убивая каждого моба по несколько раз, валюты выпадает просто копейки. Пройдя игру я не купил у торговца пассивками 9 из 10 пассивок тк там нужно около 20-25к валюты, при этом нужно ещё раскапывать места в хабе которые тоже стоят как самолёт. 6. Души aka. ваши пассивки, с ними вообще беда большинство пассивок открываются благодаря секреткам а для них нужны определенные механикаи и чаще всего чтобы получить желаемый предмет нужно иметь 2+ механики, которые ещё нужно найти. 7. Реликвии aka. ваши активные способки. Тоже большинство сложно открыть не имея 1-2 опреденных механик, большинство наносят копеечный урон. Поетому в основном бегал с реликами усиливающими урон ближкой (типо когтей, урны на доп урон по мобам, аое замедло времени). 8. Секретки.  Это игра любителям душ зайдёт, тк тут без преувеличения в каждой комнате секретка, бить каждый сантиметр комнаты станет вашей рутиной. 9. Проходимость. из-за того что камера слишком приближена к персу иногда можно не увидеть всю картину происходящего. Рычаги для открытия заблокированных путей тоже не показывают что именно ты открыл, так при ударе рычага, ты понимаешь только по звуку в какой стороне открылась дверь. 10. Игра достаточно короткая. Даже не смотря на что я после каждой новой открытой механики шёл открывать найденные ранее секретки, пройдя почти все локации на 100%, пропылесосев каждую комнату по несколько  раз, после фин босса время прохождения составило всего 10ч56м. Вывод: Даже не смотря на все выше перечисленные моменты, игра интересная, и своих денег стоит.

Unfortunately, I can't recommend this game. The first two bosses were challenging and interesting. The next two were average. And the rest (including the last one) I managed to kill just by continuously pressing the x button. They were definitely doing something in the background, but you can just ignore it, there's enough health and damage to kill them before they kill you. The slime wall-climbing ability provides access to almost the entire map, making the latter abilities almost unnecessary (the final mandatory boss can be killed before getting the wall-jumping ability). Slots for passive skills are too few, and they themselves cost too much at the merchant, there is no motivation to farm resources further. The logbook shows how many items are collected on which map, but fully explored rooms are not marked on the map itself (like in biomorph). At the same time, there is no special item, ability or at least the possibility to get a mark on the map with a missing item (as in crypt custodian). Also, I wouldn't classify this as a flaw, but some of the enemies and locations are so similar to their Grime counterparts that it's on the borderline between homage and lazy design.

第一次听闻这款有游戏的名字，还是被一众玩家将这款游戏称之为小《空洞骑士》的时候，而本作也确实在很多地方有着不俗的出色之处。作为一款类银河恶魔城游戏，本作在画风上采用了半卡通风格，同时很多设定上也和前者较为相似，并且游戏的内容也较为丰富，但是稍显遗憾的是游戏攻击敌人没有硬直，同时游戏的部分关卡设计不是很严谨，稍稍影响游戏体验。  太长不看版购买指南 丝之鸽出之前的代餐品 整体的内容较为丰富，画风笔者较为喜欢 战斗玩家攻击敌人小兵和boss都不会出现硬直，操作起来稍显奇怪 操作手感稍显一般，跳跃时会移速增快和攻击时玩家会向后移动 建议手柄游玩，键鼠的体验并不是很好  [h1]优缺点明显的《丝之鸽》代餐品[/h1] 不得不说本作的体验确实在一些地方出乎了笔者的预料，但是也在一些地方跌破了笔者的预料。首先就是游戏的画面，个人对于这种半卡通风格的画面还是非常喜欢的，并且游戏整体的画风和空洞骑士确实有种异曲同工之妙，同时游戏的战斗部分的操作手感确实意外的不错，无论是攻击频率还是攻击动作都非常的流畅。  而虽然本作在战斗部分的动作较为流畅，但是遗憾的是就战斗的呈现效果来说确实是出乎了笔者的预料的，游戏中和敌人战斗最大的两个问题，一个是敌人受击之后没有硬直，玩家攻击敌人附带特效，特效加上敌人没有硬直，经常会因此挡住玩家的视线，第二个就是玩家的普通攻击为什么会造成人物往后滑动，这个设计虽然很好的解决了部分敌人的攻击，但是却也让战斗体验变得略微奇怪。  在人物的操作方面，人物跳起来有一些飘，简单来说就是跳跃起来的速度和下降速度有些过快，同时滑步的移动有些慢，就会有种动作之间的不连贯感觉，人物跳跃之后会有个滑动的感觉。在地图的设计方面，本作有着一个即是优点又是缺点的问题，玩家可以通过跳劈在前期就进入一些需要后期能力才能去到的地方，这就让游戏的地图探索稍显繁乱，而对于一些不熟悉此类游戏的玩家来说，很难在游戏的初期找到这个提前探索地图的方式。  [h1]结语[/h1] 作为代餐来说，整体来说还算不错，值得喜欢的玩家一试。 评分8/10  [quote] 游玩百态，将游玩心得用汉语化作玩家的指明灯，(๑•̀ㅂ•́)و✧ [url=https://store.steampowered.com/curator/35402408/]点击这里关注汉化语录~[/url] 如果喜欢这个评测，请点个赞让更多人看见 [/quote]

とりあえず一周クリアしたので ＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝ 【GOOD】 ホロウナイトをプレイしたことのある人ならビビッと来る 滑らかに、そして絶妙なキモカワとグロさを兼ね備えたキャラグラフィック 壁蹴り、2段ジャンプ、ダッシュ、隙間潜り込み、フックなど 王道メトロイドヴァニア要素をしっかりと詰め込んである堅実な造り 雰囲気を盛り立てるBGMもなかなかに良い アクションの挙動も少し着地慣性が強い以外は警戒に動けるので後半になるほど 動かしてて楽しくなってくる  【BAD】 宝物庫を開放することで通常攻撃とチャージ攻撃が強くなるのですが 強化するまではかなり弱い。最初のエリアの敵ですら5発くらい殴らないと死なないこともある  レリック、ソウルのバランスがあまりよくない 組み合わせでビルドに近いこともできるのが魅力なのですが、有用なものとそうでないものがかなりはっきり 分かれてしまうので、今後の調整に期待したい  探索が不便 メニュー画面のリージョンという項目で、それぞれのエリアのマップ踏破率、 アイテム収集率、特殊な敵の撃破数などは確認できるのですが ファストトラベルの数が少なめなのと、サーチ系の装備がないため 何処の何を逃してるのかがわからない この辺りは些か古臭さを感じてしまった  周回前提なのか、一周では絶対にコンプリートできないような 不可逆なフラグと実績がある これ自体は良いと思うが、引継ぎ要素などを入れてくれると嬉しかった  ＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝ ザックリですが良くも悪くも良くまとまったメトロイドヴァニアなので 雰囲気が好みならオススメできます

The latest review (51h of play time):  This game is incredible. Despite some complaints about the late-game difficulty (e.g. it becomes too easy to play), I enjoy the exploration-based reward system. It's a great balance for both casual and speedrun players.  The pros/cons remain the same as below.  Final score: ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐  Updated review (15h of play time):  I must admit, my original negative review was completely unfair. I apologize to the developer team for my hasty judgment. I encourage anyone reading this to spend more than two hours exploring the game before forming an opinion. Many design choices that initially seem questionable will become clear as you progress through the game  Pros:  - An amazing exploration experience. After 15+ hours, I'm still enjoying it immensely. I can't recall another Metroidvania that encourages exploration as much as this one, prompting players to revisit seemingly familiar areas. Sometimes using differen routes!  - Gorgeous graphics and music - Challenging bosses and unique "unusual activities" that you have to do to power up  Cons:  - Some platforms still feel a bit too small and slippery. - While I understand the design choice, the inability to climb edges by pressing 'up' can sometimes makes platforming unejoyable. - A few elements feel out of place in terms of size and visual style, as if they were hastily added to fill space. - The default controller mapping feels a bit awkward. I remapped dash and other actions to make it more intuitive (a subjective preference). - After acquiring powerful abilities, some collectibles become less essential.  Overall Score: 4/5 so far.  I'll update this review once I've completed the game.  Original negative review (2.8h of play time):  I waited for this game and I really wanted to love it, but the mechanics, "slippery" platforms and the fact that the game was deliberately designed to use some structures that if permanently destroyed will not allow you to reach some artifacts is just bad idea in my opinion.
I had high expectations that it would be a good game, but honestly it is boring, tedious and soulless, even though I love the metroidvania genre, I dropped the game after 2 hours of gameplay.

Having a great time! Second boss was really hard, but after getting a new relic it was almost too easy. Excited to see what comes next.  Edit after finishing the game: I stand by what I said earlier. This game was very fun. Although not perfect, I had a great time overall. The game felt very much like Hollow Knight. unfortunately the later bossfights felt a bit too easy. I managed to beat most of them on my first or second try.  Still, I recommend this game for people who enjoyed Hollow Knight.

Writing this at exactly 50% of the way through the game (as per the completion rate on the file itself).  Based on my experience so far with the comparative difficulty of different zones, and my propensity to always go the 'wrong' way, there does seem to be scope for some form of sequence breaking and collecting upgrades out of order - which is always a big positive for me in a metroidvania.  I'm enjoying trying out the different combinations of relics (active sub-weapons) and souls (passive buffs) that can provide unexpected synergy. Once acquired, these can be changed at any time in the pause menu, which gives more of a incentive to experiment and change things on the fly as the situation calls for it.  If I had to scrape the barrel for something to complain about so far... farming currency seems to be a bit of a slog at the moment, as there's a lot of things to spend it on - but that may improve as I continue through.

As of release I would recommend against buying this, there are quite a few bugs and the game is missing difficulty tuning entirely - Bosses are [b]extremely[/b] easy.  My playthrough was a pretty 'meh' experience due to the state the release version is in. But with a couple patches this game could at least be mid and be recommended at the 15-20EUR price point.  Kinda sad though, its never gonna be one of the great Metroidvanias... So much wasted potential.  [list]  [*]-Voidwrought is not like Hollow Knight, and shouldn't be marketed like it is. To me it felt more like Blasphemous without the gore & penance.  [*]-this game is stupidly easy after the first 2 or 3 bosses. I'm talking 'facetank the bosses without healing and kill them in 10 seconds easy'. The tutorial boss was probably the hardest. [*]-about half of the challenge and major ability (simulacrum) upgrade rooms have doors, which are supposed to close once you enter the room, forcing you to fight. But the doors are so slow, that you can just walk through the room, ignore the fight and pick up the upgrade. [*]-inputs often don't register, unless you're standing perfectly still (i.e. healing) [*]-movement feels exceedingly floaty. [*]-too many trashmobs, too much mob spam (even in bossfights) and mobs respawn the moment you change screens. Tedious. [*]-the crawlspace ability (like morph-ball in Metroid) is painful: Its horribly slow and Its overused, making traversing those zones painful. It also seemed to make other, later simulacrum upgrades unnecessary?  [*]-zone progression felt weird: Everything just kept getting easier and easier, so I had no idea where to go. That's fine for a metroidvania, but its unusual to have finished the game and have no clue about the 'supposed' order of the zones/bosses/simulacrums.  [*]-quicktravel is way too slow: incredibly slow animation and for whatever reason, the rooms are separate from the checkpoints and placed poorly, so there's a ton of backtracking. [*]-some of the puzzles are really cool, but most of the secrets are pointless rewards, making the world feel kind of empty. Divine Remnant#278 [/list]

And to this we can add incredible landscapes, good music, interesting theme and immersive lore. Definitely recommended.

Voidwrought is a very solid metroidvania.  It excels in its exploration and discovery with some really good level design and lots of hidden secrets. Back tracking is encouraged and feels rewarding.  The combat was alright though nothing to get too excited about. The first couple bosses were a decent challenge, but after that I feel like i was a bit overpowered though i was being pretty careful to find all the upgrades. If you are looking for more of a challenge in terms of combat maybe hold off on upgrading your attack power levels. The presentation, both the audio and visuals are both quite good. Overall a fun game.

整体来看质量还是不错的，画面也精细，除了大家吐槽的脚底抹油的行动方式（有点烦但也不是不能接受）。但是有几个点想说说，虚空门传送前置动画有点墨迹，这种类型的游戏应该不需要预加载吧，每次传送都要等个7,8秒（可能实际没这么长）没有意义。存档点到boss入口前总放一些机关和小怪拦路，重新开始后都是恰好机关释放，不想受伤就要等，小怪卡在障碍物不远处，不想受伤就要等小怪慢慢走过来，这么强制延长游戏时长对于这种类型的游戏有点恶心。还有就是游戏指引基本算是没有，npc全程谜语人，地图动不动就是单行路线（前期没技能），下去就上不来，看着地图上一个个没进去的门，总觉的错过了什么又回不去，小怪血厚，打起来很费劲（游戏里的恢复手段有限，打怪又不掉血包，为什么切换场景怪物就刷新？没有一点清图的欲望，存档点弄的跟篝火似得，怎么就不学学人家的机制）以至于我走到一个新场景总觉得是走错了，是不是有什么关键技能没拿，但是不能回头强迫你一个劲的往前走，关键是基本上每个场景都有前期你上不去的个别路线或者道具，我地图上基本都标满了，只能后期技能拿完了再从头到尾再走一遍，因为实在记不住（标识也不是精准标识，没法精准定位）类恶魔城的游戏这么玩真的很难受，还有就是材料道具种类太多了，有必要吗？反正感觉这个游戏的设计初衷就是让你一遍又一遍的在地图中往返，其他类似游戏也是这样，设计的好，玩家乐在其中，像这么刻意的，玩家只能是烦躁。

I wish there was a less binary way of rating games on Steam. I don't hate this game. I think there are plenty of people that would like it. But it is not what I want out of a metroidvania in terms of gameplay.  In my opinion, the controls aren't particularly tight, but maybe that means something different to me than to the developers. I can't tell you how many times I've landed, hit the health button, and it doesn't start charging. Maybe that's my own lack of coordination but it doesn't [u]feel[/u] like it.  Probably I'm just not keeping up with the times, and maybe I'm just getting older, but when I game I'm not looking for something brutally difficult (I know, "git gud"), I'm looking for fun. Now, is it satisfying when I eventually killed the first and second bosses? Sure. Would I rather they were a tad easier? Yup.  For instance, [spoiler]take the adds out of the second boss fight,[/spoiler] and I think it might actually be really fun - and around the difficulty I'm looking for. There are some cool mechanics but my after-work exhausted brain just doesn't have the patience for it. I end up rage-quitting after a dozen attempts only to come back a few hours later and do it again.  With that said I think the art, music, general aesthetic of the game is phenomenal, and frankly is really what's keeping me playing.  Edit: Alright 15 more hours in this feels like a pretty solid Metroidvania to me, other than some general scaling issues. Like some others have said, the first bosses feel too difficult but the later bosses feel too easy. Still a fun, beautiful game to get lost in.

操作手感其实还行，主要问题还是在于人物移动脚底抹油的问题比较严重，成长曲线适中，探索收益中规中矩，遗物纹章虽然数量还是有那么多，但是能用的好用的并不多，大部分感觉类似于凑数的存在，目前探索全通，最终隐藏boss也打完了，整体难度其实并不高，我这种手残但是重探索的玩家，除了第一个以外的所有boss清一色都是拼血站撸过的，印象其实最不好的还是有些任务线NPC一周目只能在帮助和不帮助中选择，但却并没有让人开二周目的冲动。以上，游戏属于可以玩的范畴，刚开始很期待游戏，但体验感会随着游戏时间而下降，也就是说游戏还是高开低走了，银河城游戏核心考验的就是如何在扩充尽可能多的内容的前提下能够不让玩家失去兴趣刺激和玩下去的动力，个人评分只能算是一部及格的作品，6.5/10，打折或游戏荒的且不那么挑剔的玩家可入。

Si te apetece un buen Metroidvania ¿a que esperas? Está bien de precio, es entretenido, (muy entretenido), tiene la dificultad ajustada, recompensa la exploración, tiene buen rendimiento... Lo siento pero no le encuentro nada malo. Por poner algo "malo", en una ocasión se creó una pared y un suelo invisible que tuve que escalar, pero salí de la sala y cuando volví a entrar ya no estaba. Solo llevo 13 horas y pico, pero si completo el juego y sigue cumpliendo como hasta ahora, sin ningún error, lo recomiendo encarecidamente. ¡Cómpralo rata! xD. EDITO Ya he completado el juego y pondré lo que para mi ha sido su parte más floja: -No se han currado un final. Te pasas el juego, salen los créditos y fin. La verdad es que una pequeña escena, con el diseño artístico que tiene el juego no estaría mal, no pido mucho, entre 30 y 60 seg de animación serían suficientes. -La jugabilidad está muy bien y equilibrada, aunque no he podido parar de pensar que habría algún objeto que te pudiera dar la opción de equiparte o más almas o más reliquias. Como he escrito esto es lo "flojo", porque no es realmente malo, sin embargo el juego acaba siendo más simple de lo que uno se imagina al principio. Espero que los jugadores dejen de compararlo con Hollow Knight para buscar justificaciones y después usarlas de argumentando en reseñas negativas. Allá cada cual, pero yo creo que así no ayudan.

应该说是目前为止最有“空洞”味道的游戏，先下结论：相当不错的仿品。 游戏的画面精致，音乐可圈可点，战斗跳跃手感都有点飘，尚在接受范围内。地图相当之大，隐藏的东西也多，不过打过第二个boss就能获得传送能力，跑图轻松不少。相对于空洞骑士来说，本作开局有点难，体现在敌人血量比起同类游戏厚实的多，一个初期小怪都要砍好几刀，并且敌人是有碰撞判定的（没装纹章刺客，冲刺穿过敌人会受到伤害）再则没找到隐藏血瓶的话，很长一段时间你都只有一瓶血恢复。探图难度有点大。 游戏把空洞骑士的下劈发扬光大了，初期很多地方都可以引怪下劈，到达高处。下劈爬到高处取得的第一个遗物应该是“活体利爪”，往前方射出三条长矛，初期特别是第一个boss战会对战斗有很大帮助。遇到第一个商人后，建议买增加两格血量的纹章，以及遗物“神之眼”，其余的等后期再慢慢购入。 PS：再有足够的资金后，优先购入纹章“刺客”，效果为冲刺无敌，游戏整体难度立马下降很多。再推荐购入遗物“裹尸布”，效果为释放后持续吸取敌人体力，快速消灭敌人后会出现血结晶，可谓探图必备神器。 另外再来说说游戏的一些缺点，游戏提供了视角移动的功能。但是操作是按住左摇杆上或下一段时间才执行。而且没有左右移动视角的功能。这个功能完全可以替换到右摇杆上面，同时补上左右移动视角的功能，再把指令执行的时间缩短一点，现在反应太慢了。还有玩家在遇到房间遭遇战的时候，并不会马上封门，玩家快速出门后，门才落下来。这就会导致bug：玩家无法返回房间，前面路又走不通，当场卡死。解决方法很简单，玩家进入遭遇战房间后，限制住不动，等房间的门落下后，玩家才能行动。目前就遇到这一个bug，希望制作组能尽快推出补丁解决。 另外觉得打起来晃的玩家可以在设置里面把“屏幕抖动”关闭，感受就好多了。 在幸运等待“丝之鸽”的日子里，“星穹遗迹”算是款不错的代餐，首发60元的价格也值得入手。

The game is really beautiful. Unique monsters, NPC, biomes, relics, and etc. What I like the most are the immersive music, and the fair difficulty of the bosses (you get overpowered if you roam a lot, so I'd say it's fair). During the first five hours of gaming, and MAN! Couldn't really get my hands off the controller.  And tomorrow's my exam for Immunology and Serology T^T. You know it's good when I sacrifice my study hours for a game! (Well, that's just academic negligence.) Anyway, I'm dropping a good review 'cause I really want people to appreciate this hidden gem! Enjoy the game, and wish me LUCK! ;)  Edit: I didn't nail the exam, but I certainly nailed three bosses today. Worth it.  Edit 2: 10+ Hours of game play. Okay, I've figured out some things that some people would like to know before buying this. I have four concerns: First of, controls are a bit "rough". When I say rough, you couldn't really control some mechanics like when you want to heal; there would be a two seconds delay before healing especially when you just ran or attacked, or even unleashed a skill. It's really awkward and might affect the overall game play, and yeah, what I'm talking about when you are fighting a boss. Second, I've encountered some game-changing bugs. These are those that might affect the player's experience when playing: 1. The objectives displayed on the map disappears randomly, like which bosses would you fight or places to go; 2. NPC(s)  [spoiler] the void priest [/spoiler] appeared late than it was supposed to; 3. NPCs dialogue will disappear after interacting with a "stone" in some occurrence.  These are all of the bugs I encountered so far. Now, for the last part; this is just an opinion and preference. I suggest that when you die, there would be a marker placed on the map where you died. In some cases, I get lost a lot when finding the place where I died (not because I lost some money which is not a thing in this game) because I often die at a hidden area or mini bosses. There's also this inaccurate information on the mini map, it's not as smooth as I expected, your icon will just blink when you enter a new area. Overall, there's a lot to improve, but I would still recommend this game. Cheers!

I will withhold any other criticisms for now until I have played more of the game, but really loving it.

幽默，玩起来就和完全没有打磨过一样。 需要花钱的地方非常多，钱的种类还不止一个，但这都不成问题，关键是，为什么有时候杀小怪都不给钱，合着我费老鼻子劲打死一个很难搞的小怪，结果什么也没有。 这个主角的性能也是，我都不知道算好还是算坏，一个跳劈飞的比直升机都高，飞起来就是一顿乱晃，这就导致你有的地方可以偷鸡，本来上不去的地方骗个小怪一个跳劈就上去了，直接可以去到前期去不了的地方，稍微影响体验。主角跑起来更是脚底抹油，巴掌大的砖块死活不上去。 手柄默认按键玩起来非常变扭，键盘按键更是极品，连数字键都整出来了。 战斗系统，很粗糙，感觉很怪。敌人方面，血量高，密集，暂时没有遇到设计出色的boss ———————————————————————————————————————————— 这个纹章和遗器都可以在战斗中替换，那你只给我两个遗器槽和三个纹章槽就没有意义了，倒不如设计一个切换系统，可以在两套技能和纹章间切换。但是说实话根本就不需要，血上限给的太多了，boss一个技能最多就打两格血，我什么都不做原地平A都能打死boss。 这数值成长曲线非常不合理，前期主角打不死怪，后期怪打不死主角，血瓶就是摆设，不仅回的少还慢。 收集方面，后期资源完全溢出，什么神器碎片，金属锭完全没地方用，还有这个解码器，一共就四个宝藏库，那你给我那么多干什么？我四个全开完还剩四个解码器，收集除了做成就变得毫无意义

Итак, сама по себе игрушка крутая, я и ожидал, что она будет хороша. Модельки врагов красивые, крутые. Локации симпатичные. Артефакты тоже сами по себе интересные, как и души, но мне кажется этого всего слишком много будто бы, хотя мне наоборот в радость. Есть еще приколюха, что примерно 3 отдельные способности объединены в 1. Я где-то в 2/8 игры получил способность превращения в дым и эту способность можно использовать практически везде. Эта дымка спокойно ползает по стенам и потолкам, заменяет двойной прыжок и секундное превращение в лужу. Немного будто бы поломано, но не критично. Присутствуют головоломки, которые было приятно разгадывать. У разрабов отлично получилось создать такую атмосферу некоего увядшего, древнего города + у меня какая-то помешанность на таком древнем и загадочном. Однако одна проблема, о которой я ныл еще с самого выхода игры - нет привязки клавиш к мышке. Джойстик я презираю, поэтому всю игру проходил чисто на клавиатуре, и скажу, что это было вполне себе удобно. Боссы интересные, по своему симпатичные, но тут проблема - либо я просто уже мастер этого жанра игр, то ли боссы были простые. У большинства (если не у всех) отсутствует какая-либо вторая фаза и не добавляется каких-либо атак. Некоторых я проходил максимально быстро просто потому что есть такая способность, как отскакивать от врагов при помощи атаки. Таким образом я даже не прочувствовал робота из архивов. Мне игра понравилась, хотя на разрабов я все еще зол, так как они почти за год не добавили привязку к мышке.

Controls on pc are a absolute mess, and there's no mouse support so changing them to my preference was an impossibility and I 100% consider this a fail in the dev department. Before any controller andys come out, yes im aware I could play with a controller, and no i have no interest in doing for a couple of reasons that I wont bother listing. That being said unfortunately until this game gets a patch updating the mouse compatibility its a hard no from me.   I also gotta say maybe because im just spoiled from playing hollow knight and Nine sols but i was expecting a solid 50 hour journey with replay value. To find out that you can complete this game in like 10 hours was kinda disappointing to me, maybe I should look at the quality and not the quantity on this aspect but i can't ignore that feeling that i'd get one solid day game session with this title and it'd be if not complete at least 85% complete.

its ok not as amazing as i hoped. Having now finished the game i wanted to return to edit my original post. the start of the game is very weak, the enemies feel like damage sponges and progression is very slow. making the first boss feel like a slog  After the second boss, the game really opens up and shows you what its been hiding.  You become more powerful and eventually become op to the point where you laugh at everything in the game.  You are rewarded for exploration and it will take a while to get everything.  Great game with a rough start.

Just finished the game. According to the ingame timer roundabout 10 hours with 86% completion.  Overall I really enjoyed it. A few things still missing. At least one boss (according to the handbook there should be one more "herald") and also a few pieces of the map are still missing and so are som relics, souls and other collectible stuff.  I think, it is a pretty cool Metroidvania. Not the best I've ever played but by far not the worst.   The combat is fun although the mechanic is pretty simple, but I like the enemy variety and the relics add a nice touch to the combat. At the beginning it can be a little bit challenging and for my taste some of the standard enemies take quite a lot of hits, but there is a progression mechanic which will help here and actually makes the game way easier. For my preference even a bit too easy - especially in regards of the bosses.  It doesn't need to be on dark souls level but at some point I didn't even need to avoid the boss' attacks anymore. As long as I am constantly hitting them, I can finish them sometimes even without the need to heal. It shouldn't be like that, in my opinion. I don't feel that I actually "outsmarted" the bosses or that I was playing quite well. It felt like that I just had more HP and made more damage while smashing the attack button. The "final" boss for example is ridiculously easy (I actually believe/hope, that there still is something left, when completing the game 100%).  The big star of this game however is the exploration and map design. I really enjoyed exploring the game world and finding new secret passages and even whole new kinda secret biomes, solving the few puzzles and finishing some of the souls-like cryptic quests. Quite some nice surprises are waiting here and it was always exciting for me to go deeper and deeper. The eerie art style and atmospheric dark music helps a lot here.  The metroidvania typical progression skills are mostly pretty much genre standard, I would say, but it is well integrated, although it feels like the controls are ignoring sometimes the input, which can be annoying from time to time. But what I did like the most about this type of progression system is that it doesn't feel so linear like a lot of other metroidvanias. I am not 100% certain, but at a certain point it is pretty much open and left to the player how they will progress and which abilities they will get next. In some cases it feels like you can also easily sequence break the game (for example when using the "bounce attack" to gain some extra height) but I am quite certain that this was considered to happen and adds some additional nice ways to progress throughout the game.

i like this game so far, does it surpass the highs of a hidden gem like Deviator for me, maybe not. but it does surpass many other metroidvanias easily within the first hour, even while the abilities are not unlocked. games like mind seize, abyssal city, dust, juxtia, moonscars, mummy demastered, etc. so many games, and this game manages to look better than all of those, play better, and honors the modern contemporaries. I can tell this will be a good journey. keyboard and box controller support is fantastic, game runs clean. feels like a finished product already.   hope this developer keeps at it. this game along with deviator are bringing all the vibes i need this week. these days, i aint missin silksong much tbh.  update halfway through the game- game has opened up better than expected, i now like this game more than deviator due to its superior exploration and depth. while i still love the combat in deviator, it just doesnt have the content this game does, so i believe this game is already fairly underrated. going from biom to biom in this game is extremely satisfying and well designed. it makes you feel like you are uncovering your own secret path to the end of the game, more metroidvanias need to take note of this. like when u can finesse ur way to some early abilities and early boss kills before having even visited all of the unchecked areas on your map, that is where modern metroidvanias shine, and this game has done that aspect better than nearly all before it. it will take a crowsworn, or a silksong to teach this game, and this game dev team? anything that furthers this genre significantly. and even then, games like those better come out swinging..... when they do eventually come out that is.   GGs strongvania/10

Буквально начав играть я умудрился сломать игру. Я решил изменить управление под себя, и поставил  "использовать" на клавишу e вместо f. И казалось бы, что тут такого, но за счет этого теперь возможность просто поставить новое назначение клавиш пропала. Все дело в том, что прокрутка между вкладками в настройках, такими как "игра", "видео", "звук" осуществляется именно через букву e и q, причем прокрутка стоит главнее, чем выбор определенного пункта, поэтому теперь я просто не могу выбрать пункт "изменить настройки клавиатуры", у меня прокручивается на вкладку "видео". Такая же беда постигла установку меток на карте - прокрутка между вкладками "карта", "персонаж" и тд также осуществляется на e и q. И даже кнопка сброса настроек по умолчанию не помогает, т.к. он сбрасывает именно настройки конкретной открытой вкладки. Т.е. для того, чтобы сбросить настройки клавиатуры по умолчанию, мне надо эти настройки открыть, а это физически невозможно. В остальном игра, хоть и была только начата, ощущается очень здорово. Визуал, музыкальное сопровождение, всякие секреточки. Прям чувствуется дух hk, а это, в виду отсутствия hkss, очень радует. Надеюсь, что в ближайшем времени разраб починит вышеописанную проблему, и можно будет насладиться наличием меток и удобного управления по полной. А, ну еще из относительных недостатков можно упомянуть невозможность бинда способностей на мышь. Не знаю, с чем связана эта вечная проблема только вышедших платформеров, но в целом с этим можно смириться.

Voidwrought é uma experiência metroidvania imersiva e bem-produzida. O mundo é visualmente impactante, com áreas detalhadas e uma atmosfera soturna intrigante, apesar de sua narrativa ser obtusa. A exploração brilha com progressão não linear, boa diversidade de situações e interessantes habilidades de navegação. Já o combate, apesar de satisfatório e variado, peca no balanceamento, ficando mais fácil conforme se avança. Mesmo com sua falta de inovação em alguns aspectos, Voidwrought entrega uma aventura envolvente, que prende com seu universo misterioso. Ya he perdido la cuenta de la cantidad de Metroidvanias que he jugado, pero  jamas olvidare las sensaciones que dejo Hollow Knight, esa maravilla de Team Cherry. Desde entonces he buscando por cielo y tierra algo que siquiera me dejase una pizca de aquella emocion. De aquel primer amor. Durante esa travesia descubri juegos preciosos (Blasphemous, Rain World, Headlanders, The Messenger, Rusted Moss y tantos otros) Pero… en el fondo siempre esperaba por Silksong. Sin saberlo, Hornet había robado mi corazón. Pense que este sería su año, y sin embargo… sigo esperando por un día que jamas vendrá. Alli, cuando la esperanza era reemplazada por miedo. Cuando deje de creer en el genero, llego Voidwrought para enviarme a un mundo de horror cósmico digno de un sueño de H.P. Lovecraft.   Powersnake deja salir un estruendo de creatividad. Una aventura con raíces muy arraigadas en el genero, tiene claras influencias, pero también mucha personalidad. Esa mezcla de Sundered más Hollow Knight es embriagante. Casi excitante. Desenmarañar los secretos de sus ruinas biomecánicas se ha convertido en una tarea de tiempo completo. Machacar criaturas amorfas e indescriptibles me ha dejado alucinando con colores imposibles. Arroja toneladas de Lore a cada paso. Arroja cientos de bichos en cada sala. Arroja arte en cada uno de sus pixeles. No sera una maravilla, pero es una alternativa más que competente con la apaciguar la espera de Hornet.
I recommend this game, genuinely good, :)
